Mirrors measure_slice * so layout and paint stay consistent for the same slice.", "kind": "function", "line": 3027, "name": "draw_slice", "signature": "static void draw_slice(cairo_t *cr, double x, double baseline, const char *s, size_t n)"}, {"doc": "True if a fragment needs the per-cluster path (text-transform other than none/unset, or a non-zero letter-spacing). Otherwise the fast whole-slice path is * byte-identical to the pre-Hito-23b-6 renderer.", "kind": "function", "line": 3040, "name": "frag_styled", "signature": "static int frag_styled(const rc_frag *f)"}, {"doc": "Advance (px) of a fragment's text under its text-transform + letter-spacing. The current Cairo font must already be selected. Mirrors styled_draw exactly so layout * and paint stay consistent.", "kind": "function", "line": 3047, "name": "styled_advance", "signature": "static double styled_advance(cairo_t *cr, const rc_frag *f)"}, {"doc": "Draws a fragment's text starting at (x, baseline) under its text-transform + * letter-spacing. The current Cairo font/source must already be set.", "kind": "function", "line": 3063, "name": "styled_draw", "signature": "static void styled_draw(cairo_t *cr, double x, double baseline, const rc_frag *f)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 3079, "name": "block_style", "signature": "static void block_style(const ui_theme *th, const rd_block *b,\n                        double *si..."}, {"doc": "Vertical margins (px) of a block from the user-agent box model (box_style), resolved against the block's own font size (em -> px). The user-agent notice has no HTML tag, so it keeps the theme's paragraph gap as a separator and does not * regress. The single place that turns \"what tag is this\" into block spacing.", "kind": "function", "line": 3103, "name": "block_margins", "signature": "static void block_margins(const ui_theme *th, const rd_block *b,\n                          double..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 3115, "name": "flush_line", "signature": "static void flush_line(rc_layout *L, rc_state *s, const ui_theme *th)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 3135, "name": "open_line", "signature": "static void open_line(rc_layout *L, rc_state *s)"}, {"doc": "Emits one fragment at the current pen position, advancing it. Shared by the * whole-word path and the word-break split path in flow_text.", "kind": "function", "line": 3173, "name": "flow_emit_frag", "signature": "static void flow_emit_frag(rc_layout *L, rc_state *s, cairo_font_extents_t *fe,\n                 ..."}, {"doc": "owning box (for the hover-cursor lookup), -1 if none.  word-break/overflow-wrap (s->break_words): a single word wider than the WHOLE line (not just the remaining space -- a normal wrap already handles that) is split at UTF-8 cluster boundaries into pieces that each fit, instead of overflowing the box edge unbroken.  text-overflow: ellipsis (s->text_overflow, only meaningful with s->nowrap: this engine does not paint overflow:hidden clipping yet, so nowrap is the only signal that the line is meant to stay on one row): once a word would push the line past content_w, the line is truncated at the cluster boundary that fits, an ellipsis is appended, and the rest of this call's text is dropped (single-line truncation; text from a LATER sibling run on the same visual line, if any, is a * known v1 gap -- see spec/css.md).", "kind": "function", "line": 3220, "name": "flow_text", "signature": "static void flow_text(cairo_t *cr, rc_layout *L, rc_state *s, const ui_theme *th,\n               ..."}, {"doc": "Flows one text/link/notice block into L at content_w using state s. The caller sets s->bg_rgb (the block's author background, or -1) beforehand; the foreground * color and link/heading styling are derived here.", "kind": "function", "line": 3368, "name": "flow_text_block", "signature": "static void flow_text_block(cairo_t *cr, const browser_window *w, rc_layout *L,\n                 ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 3449, "name": "effect", "signature": "* effect (byte-identical default). */\nstatic int container_has_flex_items(const rd_doc *doc, size..."}, {"doc": "Maps a css_align_kw (align-items/align-self) to the box_tree cross-axis alignment it drives. BASELINE/AUTO/UNSET/space-* (align-content only, never seen here) all fall back to START; STRETCH is v1-approximated as START too * (see spec/box_engine.md) -- box_tree.c's BT_ALIGN_STRETCH already does that.", "kind": "function", "line": 3465, "name": "css_align_to_bt", "signature": "static int css_align_to_bt(int align_kw)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 3473, "name": "layout_container", "signature": "static void layout_container(cairo_t *cr, const browser_window *w, rc_layout *L,\n                ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 3523, "name": "ITEMS", "signature": "* between ITEMS (not between the lines inside one item). column-reverse\n     * reverses the visua..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 3601, "name": "slot", "signature": "* layout slot (item 0 → rightmost, last item → leftmost). */\n        if (head->flex_direction == ..."}, {"doc": "if (bt_layout(&root, content_w) != BT_OK) return; /* Translate each item's rows into its column rectangle. for (size_t j = 0; j < g; ++j) { const bt_node *kid = &kids[pos_of[j]]; for (size_t r = row_start[j]; r < row_start[j] + row_count[j]; ++r) { L->rows[r].top += base_top + kid->y; L->rows[r].x_off = kid->x; } } s->cur_top = base_top + root.h; } /* A border/outline width in px, or 0 when unset/non-positive.", "kind": "function", "line": 3719, "name": "box_edge_px", "signature": "static double box_edge_px(int wpx)"}, {"doc": "True iff a border/outline style paints a line (solid..outset); none/hidden/unset * paint nothing. The fancier 3D styles collapse to solid at paint time.", "kind": "function", "line": 3725, "name": "box_line_visible", "signature": "static int box_line_visible(int style)"}, {"doc": "Closes the open block box: flushes the current line, reserves the box's bottom * padding+border, and finalizes the recorded border-box height. No-op if none open.", "kind": "function", "line": 3731, "name": "close_top_box", "signature": "static void close_top_box(rc_layout *L, rc_state *s, const ui_theme *th)"}, {"doc": "Content rect (left, width) the current run/box is laid out in: the innermost open * box's, or the page content box when no box is open (default flat flow).", "kind": "function", "line": 3762, "name": "rc_box_context", "signature": "static void rc_box_context(const rc_state *s, double content_w,\n                           double..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 3777, "name": "children", "signature": "* own content rect onto the stack so its children (text or nested boxes) place inside\n * it. At t..."}, {"doc": "Reconciles the open-box stack so it equals block b's box path (root..b->block_id), derived from the box-def parent_id chain. Closes any open box not on the path (innermost first), then opens any path box not yet open (outermost first), nesting * each inside its parent's content rect. Bounded by RC_BOX_STACK_MAX.", "kind": "function", "line": 3893, "name": "reconcile_boxes", "signature": "static void reconcile_boxes(rc_layout *L, rc_state *s, const ui_theme *th,\n                      ..."}, {"doc": "Box path root..block_id via the box-def parent_id chain (root first), written into * out (bounded by RC_BOX_STACK_MAX). Returns the path length; block_id < 0 gives 0.", "kind": "function", "line": 3928, "name": "box_path_of", "signature": "static int box_path_of(const rd_doc *doc, int block_id, int *out)"}, {"doc": "The innermost box that is an ancestor (or self) of EVERY block in [start, end), via the longest common prefix of their box paths — the box a float band nests inside, so a wrapping position:relative/background panel is opened in flow and paints its * background behind the columns. -1 when the blocks share no box (top-level band).", "kind": "function", "line": 3944, "name": "band_common_box", "signature": "static int band_common_box(const rd_doc *doc, size_t start, size_t end)"}, {"doc": "Lays a float band [start, end) — a maximal run of blocks each with float_id >= 0 — side by side inside the current box context (spec/float.md). Blocks are grouped by float_id into items (document order); each item's width is its author box_w (px or % via bx_width_cap, Hito 32), and width-less items split the leftover evenly; left/right sides pack via fx_float_pack_wrap: an item that no longer fits opens a NEW band row (spec/float.md §7b), so consecutive full-width floats stack. Each item's blocks flow into its column (a fresh sub-state, like the flex per-item pass); the band height is the sum over rows of each row's tallest column. * Structure, applied by default.", "kind": "function", "line": 3966, "name": "layout_float_band", "signature": "static void layout_float_band(cairo_t *cr, const browser_window *w, rc_layout *L,\n               ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 4064, "name": "layout_doc", "signature": "static void layout_doc(cairo_t *cr, const browser_window *w, double content_w,\n                  ..."}, {"doc": "Stage 2: resolves out-of-flow positioning for every absolute/fixed block in the document and stores the stacking-ordered result in L->positioned. Runs after layout_doc (which has already placed in-flow blocks). The geometry arrays are built from the rc_box rects (in-flow boxes) plus a content-measurement pass for out-of-flow blocks; pv_box_defs that are not present in either get zero geometry * (the solver treats them as zero-size at the containing block's origin).", "kind": "function", "line": 4259, "name": "position_doc", "signature": "static void position_doc(cairo_t *cr, const browser_window *w, double content_w,\n                ..."}, {"doc": "make the painter repaint the box on TOP of its rows — covering everything past the first block with the box background. So drop the in-flow (relative) boxes here; the positioned repaint pass is left with only the true out-of-flow boxes * (absolute/fixed, which layout_doc skipped so they have no rc_box). size_t keep = 0; for (size_t i = 0; i < L->npositioned; ++i) { size_t bid = L->positioned[i].box_index; if (bid < BT_MAX_POSITIONED && in_flow[bid]) continue; L->positioned[keep++] = L->positioned[i]; } L->npositioned = keep; } /* Width of a painted text-input box: the preferred width clamped to the content.", "kind": "function", "line": 4360, "name": "input_box_width", "signature": "static double input_box_width(double content_w)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 4363, "name": "select_box_width", "signature": "static double select_box_width(double content_w)"}, {"doc": "} L->npositioned = keep; } /* Width of a painted text-input box: the preferred width clamped to the content. static double input_box_width(double content_w) { return (content_w < UI_INPUT_WIDTH) ? content_w : UI_INPUT_WIDTH; } static double select_box_width(double content_w) { return input_box_width(content_w); } /* Width of a painted button: its label plus horizontal padding, clamped.", "kind": "function", "line": 4369, "name": "button_box_width", "signature": "static double button_box_width(cairo_t *cr, const ui_theme *th, const rd_block *b,\n              ..."}, {"doc": "when no decoder is running (decoder_pid == 0). NULL-safe on w. /* EINTR-safe pipe write (local version of the tab.c helper). static int v_write(int fd, const void *buf, size_t n) { const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)buf; size_t done = 0; while (done < n) { ssize_t w = write(fd, p + done, n - done); if (w < 0) { if (errno == EINTR) continue; return -1; } done += (size_t)w; } return 0; } /* EINTR-safe pipe read (local version of the tab.c helper).", "kind": "function", "line": 4819, "name": "v_read", "signature": "static int v_read(int fd, void *buf, size_t n)"}, {"doc": "/* EINTR-safe pipe read (local version of the tab.c helper). static int v_read(int fd, void *buf, size_t n) { uint8_t *p = (uint8_t *)buf; size_t got = 0; while (got < n) { ssize_t r = read(fd, p + got, n - got); if (r < 0) { if (errno == EINTR) continue; return -1; } if (r == 0) return -1; got += (size_t)r; } return 0; } /* --- Audio output helpers (PCM S16LE via aplay) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 4832, "name": "audio_spawn", "signature": "static void audio_spawn(browser_window *w, int rate, int channels)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 4870, "name": "audio_write", "signature": "static void audio_write(browser_window *w, const uint8_t *data, size_t len)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 4876, "name": "audio_stop", "signature": "static void audio_stop(browser_window *w)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 4891, "name": "video_stop", "signature": "static void video_stop(browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "Reads one decoded ARGB frame from the decoder process pipe and caches it in w->video_frame. Returns 1 if a frame was read, 0 if none available * (EAGAIN / EOF), -1 on error.", "kind": "function", "line": 4941, "name": "video_read_frame", "signature": "static int video_read_frame(browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "Fetches a single resource (m3u8 or TS segment) under the full policy gates: impersonation, routing, auth, navigability fallbacks. Returns 0 on success; * *resp is populated and must be freed with sf_response_free.", "kind": "function", "line": 5073, "name": "video_fetch", "signature": "static sf_status video_fetch(const char *url, browser_window *w,\n                              sf..."}, {"doc": "Starts video playback from an m3u8 playlist URL. Fetches the playlist, parses it, handles multi-variant master playlists, spawns the decoder process AND a feeder thread that downloads TS segments and writes them to the decoder pipe. The main (Wayland) thread never blocks on HTTP for video: the event loop drains decoder frames and paints. * Returns 0 on success, -1 on failure.", "kind": "function", "line": 5090, "name": "video_play", "signature": "static int video_play(browser_window *w, const char *m3u8_url)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 5188, "name": "video_stop", "signature": "* each segment loop so a video_stop() in the main thread (which sets it to 0\n * then calls pthrea..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 5241, "name": "paint_video_row", "signature": "static void paint_video_row(cairo_t *cr, browser_window *w, const rd_block *blk,\n                ..."}, {"doc": "Horizontal shift a row's text gets from author text-align (center/right): the slack between the available width and the line's right edge. 0 for left/justify/ unset, and for non-text rows. Shared by the painter and the link hit-test so the * click target matches exactly what is drawn.", "kind": "function", "line": 5342, "name": "row_align_offset", "signature": "static double row_align_offset(const rc_layout *L, const rc_row *r, double content_w)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 5376, "name": "upstream", "signature": "* upstream (see spec/css.md). */\nstatic void box_path(cairo_t *cr, double x, double y, double w, ..."}, {"doc": "Paints one block box's decoration (Hito 23b-8 Step C): box-shadow, background fill, the four borders and the outline, behind the rows it encloses. (ox, oy) is the absolute offset for the box's layout-space rect. Solid lines in v1 (dashed/dotted collapse to solid). border-radius rounds the shadow, background, outline and -- when the four borders are uniform -- the border ring; mixed per-side borders keep square corners. Content is not clipped to the rounded rect (v1). The box * decoration was gated behind caps.css upstream (render_doc).", "kind": "function", "line": 5399, "name": "paint_box_decoration", "signature": "static void paint_box_decoration(cairo_t *cr, const rc_box *bx, double ox, double oy,\n           ..."}, {"doc": "Paints one laid-out row at vertical position ry. Shared by the on-screen painter and the PDF exporter so both render identically (same fonts, colours, emphasis, links and backgrounds). band_w is the full-bleed width for notice banners (the window width on screen, the page width in the PDF). show_hover draws the link * hover highlight (on screen only; suppressed when exporting).", "kind": "function", "line": 5680, "name": "paint_content_row", "signature": "static void paint_content_row(cairo_t *cr, browser_window *w, const rc_layout *L,\n               ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 5764, "name": "cairo_set_dash", "signature": "cairo_set_dash(cr, (double[])"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 5766, "name": "cairo_set_dash", "signature": "cairo_set_dash(cr, (double[])"}, {"doc": "} if (f->overline) { double oy = fbaseline - f->font_size * UI_OVERLINE_OFFSET; cairo_move_to(cr, x0, oy); cairo_line_to(cr, x1, oy); cairo_stroke(cr); } cairo_set_dash(cr, NULL, 0, 0.0);  /* restore solid for next line } } } /* Max overflow:hidden nesting depth (anti-DoS). #define OV_MAX_DEPTH 16 /* Returns nonzero if a box clips content on either axis.", "kind": "function", "line": 5792, "name": "ov_box_clips", "signature": "static int ov_box_clips(const pv_box_def *d)"}, {"doc": "Walks the ancestor chain of block_id and collects overflow:hidden box IDs * into out[] (outermost first). Returns count, limited to OV_MAX_DEPTH.", "kind": "function", "line": 5800, "name": "ov_collect_chain", "signature": "static int ov_collect_chain(const rd_doc *doc, int block_id, int *out, int cap)"}, {"doc": "static int ov_collect_chain(const rd_doc *doc, int block_id, int *out, int cap) { int tmp[OV_MAX_DEPTH], n = 0; for (int id = block_id; id >= 0 && n < cap; ) { const pv_box_def *d = rd_box_at(doc, (size_t)id); if (d == NULL) break; if (ov_box_clips(d)) tmp[n++] = id; id = d->parent_id; } /* tmp is innermost..outermost; reverse to outermost..innermost for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) out[i] = tmp[n - 1 - i]; return n; } /* Looks up an rc_box by block_id.", "kind": "function", "line": 5814, "name": "ov_find_box", "signature": "static const rc_box *ov_find_box(const rc_layout *L, int bid)"}, {"doc": "Computes the padding-box content rect (in page coords: y, x, w, h) for a box. * Used as the clip region for overflow:hidden children.", "kind": "function", "line": 5822, "name": "ov_content_rect", "signature": "static void ov_content_rect(const rc_box *bx, const pv_box_def *d,\n                            do..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 5842, "name": "fragment", "signature": "* first fragment (rc_frag.block_id, stamped at flow_emit_frag time) -- using\n * blk->block_id alo..."}, {"doc": "Does this box need its own offscreen compositing group? Single source of truth: the compositor's cx_forms_stacking_context (spec/compositor.md), fed straight from the box's resolved style -- the same predicate box_tree.c already uses to decide Stage-2 paint ORDER now also decides the painter's compositing MECHANISM. * NULL-safe (cx_forms_stacking_context(NULL) is 0).", "kind": "function", "line": 5897, "name": "box_forms_stacking_context", "signature": "static int box_forms_stacking_context(const pv_box_def *def)"}, {"doc": "transform (M1.2 translate; M1.2b adds scale/rotate): builds the box's full 2D affine transform -- translate, then rotate, then scale, pivoted at the box's own center (CSS's initial transform-origin, 50% 50%, the only pivot this engine supports) -- as a Cairo matrix. Identity when the box has no transform, so callers can apply it unconditionally with no visual change for the untransformed common case. box_x/box_y are the box's DEVICE-space top-left (e.g. ox+bx->x, oy+bx->top -- what paint_box_decoration receives). Forward application (cairo_transform around a box's paint calls) lives at the three call sites below; hit-testing (the inverse) is out of scope for * this increment, see include/page_view.h pv_box_def.", "kind": "function", "line": 5928, "name": "box_transform_matrix", "signature": "static void box_transform_matrix(const pv_box_def *def, double box_x, double box_y,\n             ..."}, {"doc": "Maps CSS mix-blend-mode to the Cairo compositing operator used when a box's offscreen group is blended back over its backdrop. Cairo's separable and non-separable blend operators are a 1:1 match for the CSS values (both trace to the same Porter-Duff/W3C Compositing spec); NORMAL/unset is the ordinary OVER * operator every other paint call in this file already uses implicitly.", "kind": "function", "line": 5952, "name": "bui_blend_operator", "signature": "static cairo_operator_t bui_blend_operator(int mix_blend)"}, {"doc": "Composites the currently-pushed group back onto cr using def's opacity/mix-blend (the group must already be open via cairo_push_group). Restores CAIRO_OPERATOR_OVER afterwards so callers never leak a non-default operator into later paint calls. * elapsed_ms: time since page load in ms; used for animation-blended opacity.", "kind": "function", "line": 6084, "name": "bui_pop_group_composite", "signature": "static void bui_pop_group_composite(cairo_t *cr, const pv_box_def *def, uint64_t elapsed_ms)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 6193, "name": "limits", "signature": "* documents narrower v1 limits (no overflow:hidden, no negative z-index). A box\n * grouped this w..."}, {"doc": "(blk->block_id match), together, when the box forms a stacking context -- so a translucent/blended box's background and its text row(s) fade or blend as ONE coherent unit. Grouping decoration alone is not enough: a row's own background fill (paint_content_row's r->bg_rgb branch) cascades the SAME author background-color as the box, but paints in the caller's separate row pass -- left ungrouped, it shows as a solid, un-faded rectangle sitting on top of an otherwise-correctly-translucent box, which looks more broken than doing nothing. Consumed rows are marked in row_done (indexed like L->rows) so the caller's row loop skips them. Glyph color still uses the pre-existing pv_run.opacity/set_rgb_alpha path (untouched, already correct); this only adds the row BACKGROUND and the box's own decoration to the group. Ungrouped boxes are unchanged: paint_box_decoration only, rows stay in the normal row loop. Does not recurse into nested child boxes/their rows -- a full nested layer tree * is out of scope, see spec/compositor.md.", "kind": "function", "line": 6233, "name": "paint_box_and_direct_rows", "signature": "static void paint_box_and_direct_rows(cairo_t *cr, browser_window *w, const rc_layout *L,\n       ..."}, {"doc": "Group compositing (M1.1 increments 3-4): a box that forms a CSS stacking context (box_forms_stacking_context: opacity<1, mix-blend != normal, isolation:isolate, transform != none, or the position+z-index triggers cx_forms_stacking_context already needed for paint ORDER) is composited as ONE unit -- its decoration and content blended together first, then the whole result faded/blended -- not each piece faded independently (naive per-draw alpha double-blends wherever e.g. background and content overlap). cairo_push_group redirects the box's paint calls to a fresh offscreen surface (bounded by the current clip, so this is exactly the \"layer\" spec/compositor.md describes); bui_pop_group_composite blends that surface back with the box's opacity and mix-blend-mode. box_transform_matrix (M1.2 translate; M1.2b scale/rotate) shifts where decoration/content PAINT -- the box's stored rect used for hit-testing is * untouched, see include/page_view.h.", "kind": "function", "line": 6305, "name": "paint_positioned_one", "signature": "static void paint_positioned_one(cairo_t *cr, browser_window *w, const ui_theme *th,\n            ..."}, {"doc": "R6: recursively paints child boxes of `parent_id` that form stacking contexts, inside the parent's already-open cairo group. Non-SC children * are painted flat. Marks children in box_done.", "kind": "function", "line": 6399, "name": "paint_nested_children", "signature": "static void paint_nested_children(cairo_t *cr, browser_window *w,\n                               ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 6428, "name": "paint_structured", "signature": "static void paint_structured(cairo_t *cr, browser_window *w, double content_top,\n                ..."}, {"doc": "Writes the window's current laid-out document to a vector PDF at `path`, paginated to US Letter. Returns the page count (0 when the document lays out to nothing), or -1 on a Cairo error. The page is laid out and coloured in a forced light theme so the print is dark-on-white; the live theme is restored before return. No window/Wayland state is touched -- only w->doc and w->theme are read, through the same layout_doc / paint_content_row the screen uses -- so the GUI * \"Save as PDF\" and the headless --download-pdf path render identically.", "kind": "function", "line": 6571, "name": "write_doc_pdf", "signature": "static long write_doc_pdf(browser_window *w, const char *path)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 6676, "name": "export_pdf", "signature": "static void export_pdf(browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "Writes the window's current laid-out document to a single full-height PNG at `path` (the same layout/paint path as the screen and the PDF export, in a forced light theme so it is dark-on-white). Returns the image height in px (0 when the document lays out to nothing), or -1 on a Cairo error. No Wayland state is * touched -- only w->doc and w->theme are read.", "kind": "function", "line": 6728, "name": "write_doc_png", "signature": "static long write_doc_png(browser_window *w, const char *path)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 6834, "name": "export_png", "signature": "static void export_png(browser_window *w)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 6869, "name": "caller", "signature": "* caller (freedom.c --download-pdf) owns the fetch/parse pipeline and supplies the\n * out_path ve..."}, {"doc": "Headless PNG export (no Wayland; see include/ui.h). One full-height bitmap of the whole page, the cheapest artifact for visual review (no PDF rasterise step). Same * zeroed-window setup as ui_render_pdf.", "kind": "function", "line": 6892, "name": "ui_render_png", "signature": "ui_status ui_render_png(const rd_doc *doc, const char *out_path, long *out_h)"}, {"doc": "Headless PNG/PDF export WITH image decoding (see include/ui.h). Unlike the plain ui_render_png/pdf (which always draw placeholders), these decode the page's allowed images through the still-open confined worker `t` -- the hostile image bytes never touch this process -- exactly as the on-screen window does. Remote image bytes come from `img_fetch` (the caller's policy-applying fetcher, e.g. headless_fetch); local file:// images are read from disk (confined to the document directory by render_doc). top_url is the page origin (https or file://); a NULL origin loads no images. Any image that fails falls back to its placeholder, byte-identical to the window path. The zeroed window mirrors ui_render_png; cur_top drives the local-vs-remote branch in * load_images and is released before returning.", "kind": "function", "line": 6918, "name": "render_doc_images", "signature": "static ui_status render_doc_images(const rd_doc *doc, tab *t, const char *top_url,\n              ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 6948, "name": "ui_render_png_images", "signature": "ui_status ui_render_png_images(const rd_doc *doc, tab *t, const char *top_url,\n                  ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 6954, "name": "ui_render_pdf_images", "signature": "ui_status ui_render_pdf_images(const rd_doc *doc, tab *t, const char *top_url,\n                  ..."}, {"doc": "Headless layout dump: runs the same layout_doc + position_doc pass as the on-screen/PNG renderer and prints the resolved box geometry (in-flow boxes and out-of-flow positioned boxes) to stdout as agent-readable text. This is the layout-side counterpart to --dump-dom (which prints the pre-layout render tree): it makes the EFFECT of the layout engine verifiable without a display or a rasterised image (CI, an AI agent) — the Stacking/positioning bugs that a PNG would reveal are inspectable as plain numbers. See `--dump-layout` in spec/freedom.md. Pure I/O on already-resolved, already-fuzzed data; no socket, * no file, no mutation of the doc.", "kind": "function", "line": 6970, "name": "ui_dump_layout", "signature": "ui_status ui_dump_layout(const rd_doc *doc)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 7022, "name": "link_at_point", "signature": "static const char *link_at_point(browser_window *w, double px, double py)"}, {"doc": "First non-unset author `cursor` on block_id's box or an ancestor (nearest wins, like the rest of the box-decoration fields), or CSS_CUR_UNSET if none set the property or block_id < 0. Bounded by the box-def parent chain (RC_BOX_STACK_MAX, * same cap as every other box-path walk here).", "kind": "function", "line": 7116, "name": "resolve_box_cursor", "signature": "static int resolve_box_cursor(const rd_doc *doc, int block_id)"}, {"doc": "True when author `pointer-events: none` removes block_id's content from hit-testing (2026-07-10): the nearest box in the parent chain that sets the property wins, exactly like resolve_box_cursor. The box tree only exists with * caps.css, so the default render hit-tests everything (byte-identical).", "kind": "function", "line": 7130, "name": "box_pointer_events_none", "signature": "static int box_pointer_events_none(const rd_doc *doc, int block_id)"}, {"doc": "Returns the resolved author `cursor` (css_cursor) at (px, py), or CSS_CUR_UNSET when outside content / no box sets one. Unlike link_at_point this tests EVERY fragment (not just linked ones): cursor:pointer commonly styles a non-link clickable element (a JS-driven button/div). A separate layout+hit-test walk from link_at_point/node_at_point (matching this file's existing per-purpose hit-test * style); update_hover calls all it needs once per pointer-motion event.", "kind": "function", "line": 7146, "name": "cursor_at_point", "signature": "static int cursor_at_point(browser_window *w, double px, double py)"}, {"doc": "Returns the DOM node id of the element under (px, py), or DOM_NODE_NONE if the point is over blank space / outside content. Mirrors layout and scroll clamping * exactly so the hit matches the painted frame.", "kind": "function", "line": 7198, "name": "node_at_point", "signature": "static dom_node_id node_at_point(browser_window *w, double px, double py)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 7246, "name": "reference", "signature": "* reference (downgrade, foreign scheme, no resolvable base) navigates nowhere:\n * hostile content..."}, {"doc": "Applies a click result returned by the worker: rebuild the rendered document and refresh inputs/console, but keep the current page in history (a click is not a navigation). Images are not re-fetched: a click handler may add text, but v1 does * not introduce new remote images.", "kind": "function", "line": 7267, "name": "apply_click_result", "signature": "static void apply_click_result(browser_window *w, tab_page *page)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 7290, "name": "memory", "signature": "* memory (the href pointer, not its contents, was all the old code preserved). */\nstatic void dis..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 7373, "name": "GET", "signature": "* the network under weaker rules than a GET (Zero Trust). */\nstatic void do_submit_post(browser_w..."}, {"doc": "Builds ~/Downloads/freedom into out and creates both levels (best effort; an existing directory is fine). Returns 1 on success. Falls back to $HOME or \".\" * for the base, matching export_pdf's directory choice.", "kind": "function", "line": 7407, "name": "ensure_download_dir", "signature": "static int ensure_download_dir(char *out, size_t outsz)"}, {"doc": "Writes len bytes to path with 0600 perms via a temp file + atomic rename (the disk_store convention): a crash mid-write never leaves a half file at path, and * a download is never world-readable. Returns 1 on success.", "kind": "function", "line": 7422, "name": "write_file_atomic", "signature": "static int write_file_atomic(const char *path, const void *bytes, size_t len)"}, {"doc": "Saves a fetched resource to ~/Downloads/freedom instead of rendering it. The filename is derived fail-closed from the hostile Content-Disposition / URL * (download module); the body is size-capped. The current page stays on screen.", "kind": "function", "line": 7444, "name": "save_download", "signature": "static void save_download(browser_window *w, const char *url, const char *bytes,\n                ..."}, {"doc": "Ctrl+S: save the current page's cached source to ~/Downloads/freedom. No network * round-trip -- the bytes already in the page cache are written.", "kind": "function", "line": 7477, "name": "save_current_page", "signature": "static void save_current_page(browser_window *w)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 7486, "name": "deliver_fetch_result", "signature": "static void deliver_fetch_result(browser_window *w, fetch_job *j)"}, {"doc": "Drains every completed fetch the worker threads have posted (the read end is non-blocking; pointer-sized writes are atomic). Called when the loop sees the * fetch pipe readable.", "kind": "function", "line": 7541, "name": "drain_fetch_results", "signature": "static void drain_fetch_results(browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "Toggles distraction-free (reader) mode and re-renders from cache (no network): the worker drops boilerplate, author styling/images are gated off, and the content * is centered in a reading column. Shared by the menu item and the Ctrl+D shortcut.", "kind": "function", "line": 7617, "name": "toggle_reader", "signature": "static void toggle_reader(browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "Toggles distraction-free (reader) mode and re-renders from cache (no network): the worker drops boilerplate, author styling/images are gated off, and the content * is centered in a reading column. Shared by the menu item and the Ctrl+D shortcut. static void toggle_reader(browser_window *w) { w->reader = !w->reader; apply_theme(w); /* recompute the reading-column margin for the new state browser_set_status(&w->bs, w->reader ? \"Distraction-free mode ON (boilerplate and author styles hidden).\" : \"Distraction-free mode OFF.\", now_ms()); profile_sync(w); render_current(w); } /* True when options-menu item i is currently enabled (drives its checkmark).", "kind": "function", "line": 7628, "name": "menu_item_checked", "signature": "static int menu_item_checked(const browser_window *w, size_t i)"}, {"doc": "Toggles options-menu item i and applies its effect. Theme and force-colors only affect presentation (a repaint, which re-runs layout, suffices); a capability * change re-renders from cache.", "kind": "function", "line": 7650, "name": "menu_item_toggle", "signature": "static void menu_item_toggle(browser_window *w, size_t i)"}, {"doc": "A small spinner meaning \"busy\". Now that the fetch runs off the event-loop thread, the loop ticks ~12 fps while loading and rotates a leading arc so the user sees real * progress. phase in [0,1) is the rotation, derived from the wall clock.", "kind": "function", "line": 7760, "name": "draw_clock", "signature": "static void draw_clock(cairo_t *cr, ui_rgb color, double cx, double cy, double r,\n               ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 7771, "name": "draw_hamburger", "signature": "static void draw_hamburger(cairo_t *cr, ui_rgb color, double bx, double ttop)"}, {"doc": "The reload button glyph: a ~300-degree circular arrow centred in a UI_BTN_W button starting at bx. Drawn with Cairo (not a font glyph) so it never depends on the * chrome face having a reload codepoint.", "kind": "function", "line": 7788, "name": "draw_reload", "signature": "static void draw_reload(cairo_t *cr, ui_rgb color, double bx, double ttop)"}, {"doc": "double a1 = a0 + UI_TWO_PI * 0.82;      /* leave a gap for the arrowhead cairo_new_sub_path(cr); cairo_arc(cr, cx, cy, r, a0, a1); cairo_stroke(cr); /* Arrowhead at the arc's start (top), pointing clockwise. double hx = cx + r * cos(a0), hy = cy + r * sin(a0); cairo_move_to(cr, hx, hy); cairo_line_to(cr, hx - 4.0, hy - 1.0); cairo_move_to(cr, hx, hy); cairo_line_to(cr, hx + 1.0, hy - 4.5); cairo_stroke(cr); } /* Draws the options-menu panel (checkbox per capability) when open.", "kind": "function", "line": 7810, "name": "draw_menu", "signature": "static void draw_menu(cairo_t *cr, browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "Persistent bottom strip showing the target of the link under the pointer, so the user always knows where a click will go. Returns the strip height (0 when * nothing is hovered) so the toast can stack above it.", "kind": "function", "line": 7921, "name": "draw_hover_url", "signature": "static double draw_hover_url(cairo_t *cr, browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "Draws the transient status toast (a banner near the bottom of the window), * raised by bottom_offset so it stacks above the hover-URL strip when both show.", "kind": "function", "line": 7953, "name": "draw_toast", "signature": "static void draw_toast(cairo_t *cr, browser_window *w, double bottom_offset)"}, {"doc": "Paints the tab strip: one cell per tab (the active one connected to the content background, the rest dimmed), each with its clipped title and a close 'x', then a trailing '+' to open a new tab. The geometry mirrors the pointer hit-test (tab_width/newtab_x) so a click lands on exactly what is drawn. Uses the chrome * (monospace) font already selected by paint().", "kind": "function", "line": 7983, "name": "draw_tabstrip", "signature": "static void draw_tabstrip(cairo_t *cr, browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "Omnibox autocomplete dropdown: a panel of favorite-host suggestions below the URL bar, drawn as an overlay (on top of content) while the URL bar is focused and a * query matches. The highlighted row (omni_sel) is shaded.", "kind": "function", "line": 8038, "name": "draw_omnibox", "signature": "static void draw_omnibox(cairo_t *cr, browser_window *w)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 8071, "name": "paint", "signature": "static void paint(browser_window *w)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 8315, "name": "redraw", "signature": "static void redraw(browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "cairo_destroy(cr); } static void redraw(browser_window *w) { if (!w->configured) return; if (ensure_buffer(w) != 0) return; paint(w); wl_surface_attach(w->surface, w->buffer, 0, 0); wl_surface_damage_buffer(w->surface, 0, 0, w->width, w->height); wl_surface_commit(w->surface); wl_display_flush(w->display);  /* drain connection buffer after each paint } /* --- xdg-shell ---", "kind": "function", "line": 8327, "name": "wm_base_ping", "signature": "static void wm_base_ping(void *data, struct xdg_wm_base *b, uint32_t serial)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 8333, "name": "xdg_surface_configure", "signature": "static void xdg_surface_configure(void *data, struct xdg_surface *s, uint32_t serial)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 8341, "name": "toplevel_configure", "signature": "static void toplevel_configure(void *data, struct xdg_toplevel *t,\n                              ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 8357, "name": "wl_array_for_each", "signature": "wl_array_for_each(st, states)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 8362, "name": "toplevel_close", "signature": "static void toplevel_close(void *data, struct xdg_toplevel *t)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 8370, "name": "deco_configure", "signature": "static void deco_configure(void *data, struct zxdg_toplevel_decoration_v1 *d, uint32_t mode)"}, {"doc": "Applies the hand (over a link) or default arrow cursor for the current pointer enter serial. A no-op when no themed cursor is available (the compositor keeps * its own default).", "kind": "function", "line": 8384, "name": "set_cursor", "signature": "static void set_cursor(browser_window *w, int hand)"}, {"doc": "Recomputes which link (if any) is under the pointer; on a change, updates the cursor shape and repaints so the hover highlight follows. The author `cursor` (css_cursor) at the point is folded into the hand-vs-arrow decision: a cursor:pointer element (a JS-driven button/div, not just an <a>) shows the hand even without an href. v1 only distinguishes pointer from everything else (the rest of the keyword set resolves for completeness/debug_dom but still paints as the default arrow -- see spec/css.md); overriding a LINK's hand cursor away * (e.g. `a{cursor:default}`) is a known v1 gap.", "kind": "function", "line": 8406, "name": "update_hover", "signature": "static void update_hover(browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "struct wl_buffer *buffer; void  *shm_data; size_t shm_size; cairo_surface_t *cairo_surface; double split;          /* log-pane fraction of the body height double scroll;         /* log scroll offset (px) int    dragging_split; /* the divider is being dragged int    hover_copy;     /* mouse is over the copy button int    copy_status;    /* 0=idle, 1=no-clipboard(red), 2=OK(green) uint64_t copy_ts;      /* last copy timestamp (ms), for \"Copied!\" feedback tf_field editor;       /* the JS REPL input (newlines allowed) }; /* y of the divider between the log pane and the editor.", "kind": "function", "line": 8466, "name": "fbw_split_y", "signature": "static double fbw_split_y(const freebug_window *fb)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 8474, "name": "freebug_ensure_buffer", "signature": "static int freebug_ensure_buffer(freebug_window *fb)"}, {"doc": "struct wl_shm_pool *pool = wl_shm_create_pool(fb->owner->shm, fd, (int32_t)size); fb->buffer = wl_shm_pool_create_buffer(pool, 0, fb->width, fb->height, stride, WL_SHM_FORMAT_ARGB8888); wl_shm_pool_destroy(pool); close(fd); if (fb->buffer == NULL) { munmap(data, size); return -1; } wl_buffer_add_listener(fb->buffer, &buffer_listener, fb); /* release is a no-op fb->shm_data = data; fb->shm_size = size; fb->cairo_surface = cairo_image_surface_create_for_data( (unsigned char *)data, CAIRO_FORMAT_ARGB32, fb->width, fb->height, stride); return (cairo_surface_status(fb->cairo_surface) == CAIRO_STATUS_SUCCESS) ? 0 : -1; } /* Color for a console level (dark devtools palette).", "kind": "function", "line": 8502, "name": "fbw_level_rgb", "signature": "static void fbw_level_rgb(int level, double *r, double *g, double *b)"}, {"doc": "} /* Color for a console level (dark devtools palette). static void fbw_level_rgb(int level, double *r, double *g, double *b) { switch (level) { case FB_ERROR: *r = 0.95; *g = 0.36; *b = 0.36; break; case FB_WARN:  *r = 0.93; *g = 0.78; *b = 0.36; break; case FB_INFO:  *r = 0.46; *g = 0.80; *b = 0.95; break; case FB_DEBUG: *r = 0.60; *g = 0.60; *b = 0.66; break; default:       *r = 0.86; *g = 0.88; *b = 0.91; break; /* log } } /* Counts the visual lines a console buffer occupies (entries split on So \"example.com\" becomes https://example.com and \"best linux distro\" becomes a * search, instead of the old \"cannot read file\" dead end.", "kind": "function", "line": 9077, "name": "go_omnibox", "signature": "static void go_omnibox(browser_window *w)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 9121, "name": "ptr_button", "signature": "static void ptr_button(void *d, struct wl_pointer *p, uint32_t serial, uint32_t t,\n              ..."}, {"doc": "} } /* PV_IN_BUTTON (reset/generic) is inert in v1. } else { /* Clicking non-input: dispatch blur on old if any. if (old_nid != DOM_NODE_NONE) dispatch_js_event(w, old_nid, \"blur\", NULL, 0, NULL); dispatch_click(w, w->ptr_x, w->ptr_y); } } redraw(w); } /* One body line of scroll, in pixels (the common step unit).", "kind": "function", "line": 9351, "name": "scroll_line_px", "signature": "static double scroll_line_px(const browser_window *w)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 9354, "name": "ptr_axis", "signature": "static void ptr_axis(void *data, struct wl_pointer *p, uint32_t time,\n                     uint32..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 9378, "name": "ptr_frame", "signature": "static void ptr_frame(void *d, struct wl_pointer *p)"}, {"doc": "} static const struct wl_pointer_listener pointer_listener = { .enter = ptr_enter, .leave = ptr_leave, .motion = ptr_motion, .button = ptr_button, .axis = ptr_axis, .frame = ptr_frame, }; (void)o; (void)a; } static void data_offer_action(void *d, struct wl_data_offer *o, uint32_t a) { (void)d; (void)o; (void)a; } static const struct wl_data_offer_listener data_offer_listener = { .offer = data_offer_mime, .source_actions = data_offer_source_actions, .action = data_offer_action, }; /* A new offer is being introduced: start scanning its mime types.", "kind": "function", "line": 9426, "name": "data_device_data_offer", "signature": "static void data_device_data_offer(void *data, struct wl_data_device *dev,\n                      ..."}, {"doc": "The clipboard selection changed. Commit the new offer (or NULL when the clipboard * was cleared), destroying any previous one we held.", "kind": "function", "line": 9438, "name": "data_device_selection", "signature": "static void data_device_selection(void *data, struct wl_data_device *dev,\n                       ..."}, {"doc": "wl_data_offer_destroy(w->selection_offer); if (offer == NULL) { w->selection_offer = NULL; w->selection_offer_has_text = 0; w->sel_mime[0] = '\\0'; return; } w->selection_offer = offer; w->selection_offer_has_text = (offer == w->incoming_offer) ? w->incoming_offer_has_text : 0; snprintf(w->sel_mime, sizeof w->sel_mime, \"%s\", (offer == w->incoming_offer && w->incoming_mime[0]) ? w->incoming_mime : \"text/plain\"); } /* Drag-and-drop events: not supported (the data device is clipboard-only).", "kind": "function", "line": 9457, "name": "data_device_enter", "signature": "static void data_device_enter(void *d, struct wl_data_device *dev, uint32_t serial,\n             ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 9462, "name": "data_device_leave", "signature": "static void data_device_leave(void *d, struct wl_data_device *dev)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 9463, "name": "data_device_motion", "signature": "static void data_device_motion(void *d, struct wl_data_device *dev, uint32_t t,\n                 ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 9467, "name": "data_device_drop", "signature": "static void data_device_drop(void *d, struct wl_data_device *dev)"}, {"doc": "wl_fixed_t x, wl_fixed_t y) { (void)d; (void)dev; (void)t; (void)x; (void)y; } static void data_device_drop(void *d, struct wl_data_device *dev) { (void)d; (void)dev; } static const struct wl_data_device_listener data_device_listener = { .data_offer = data_device_data_offer, .enter = data_device_enter, .leave = data_device_leave, .motion = data_device_motion, .drop = data_device_drop, .selection = data_device_selection, }; /* Another client now owns our copied text: drop our source.", "kind": "function", "line": 9478, "name": "data_source_cancelled", "signature": "static void data_source_cancelled(void *data, struct wl_data_source *src)"}, {"doc": ".enter = data_device_enter, .leave = data_device_leave, .motion = data_device_motion, .drop = data_device_drop, .selection = data_device_selection, }; /* Another client now owns our copied text: drop our source. static void data_source_cancelled(void *data, struct wl_data_source *src) { browser_window *w = (browser_window *)data; if (src == w->copy_source) w->copy_source = NULL; wl_data_source_destroy(src); } /* A paster asked for our copied text: write it to the pipe and close.", "kind": "function", "line": 9484, "name": "data_source_send", "signature": "static void data_source_send(void *data, struct wl_data_source *src,\n                            ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 9497, "name": "data_source_target", "signature": "static void data_source_target(void *d, struct wl_data_source *s, const char *m)"}, {"doc": "Formats the entire Freebug console buffer and places it on the Wayland clipboard, so the user can paste the developer console output elsewhere. The format matches * print_console in freedom.c. Called from the Copy button in the Freebug header.", "kind": "function", "line": 9509, "name": "freebug_copy_console", "signature": "static void freebug_copy_console(browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "Inserts pasted bytes into whichever text target currently has focus (page input, User-Agent box, or the URL bar). Control bytes -- including embedded CR/LF/TAB that a multi-line clipboard would carry -- are dropped so a paste cannot inject a newline * into a single-line field; UTF-8 continuation bytes (>= 0x80) pass through.", "kind": "function", "line": 9567, "name": "insert_pasted_text", "signature": "static void insert_pasted_text(browser_window *w, const char *text, size_t len)"}, {"doc": "Ctrl+C: copy the focused field's text (or, with nothing focused, the page address) * to the clipboard by owning a wl_data_source that serves it on demand.", "kind": "function", "line": 9631, "name": "clipboard_copy", "signature": "static void clipboard_copy(browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "Cut the omnibar selection: copy it to the clipboard, then remove it. v1 cut is the * URL bar only (page inputs / the UA box have no selection model yet). static void clipboard_cut(browser_window *w) { if (!w->url_bar_focused) return; if (!browser_url_bar_selection(&w->bs, NULL, NULL)) return; clipboard_copy(w);                       /* copies just the selection browser_url_bar_delete_selection(&w->bs); } /* --- keyboard with xkbcommon ---", "kind": "function", "line": 9678, "name": "keyboard_keymap", "signature": "static void keyboard_keymap(void *data, struct wl_keyboard *kbd,\n                            uint..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 9699, "name": "keyboard_enter", "signature": "static void keyboard_enter(void *d, struct wl_keyboard *kbd, uint32_t s,\n                        ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 9707, "name": "keyboard_leave", "signature": "static void keyboard_leave(void *d, struct wl_keyboard *kbd, uint32_t s, struct wl_surface *sf)"}, {"doc": "Maps an xkb keysym to a JS event.key string. Returns NULL for printable chars * (the utf8 bytes should be used as the key).", "kind": "function", "line": 9715, "name": "key_sym_to_js_key", "signature": "static const char *key_sym_to_js_key(xkb_keysym_t sym)"}, {"doc": "Maps an xkb keysym to a JS keyCode number. For printable ASCII, returns the * ASCII value; for special keys, returns the standard JS keyCode.", "kind": "function", "line": 9741, "name": "key_sym_to_keycode", "signature": "static int key_sym_to_keycode(xkb_keysym_t sym)"}, {"doc": "Dispatches a JS DOM event to the worker for the given node_id. The worker returns a re-derived view which is applied via apply_click_result. Since that replaces w->doc and rebuilds w->inputs, focus is cleared before the call and restored afterward if the input still exists. The text value is saved before * dispatch and restored after, so live typed content is not lost.", "kind": "function", "line": 9766, "name": "dispatch_js_event", "signature": "static void dispatch_js_event(browser_window *w, dom_node_id node_id,\n                           ..."}, {"doc": "Performs the effect of a single key press. Factored out of keyboard_key so a held key can be re-fired from the repeat timer with the exact same semantics (the caller * recomputes sym/utf8/modifiers from the live xkb_state each time).", "kind": "function", "line": 9823, "name": "handle_key_press", "signature": "static void handle_key_press(browser_window *w, xkb_keysym_t sym, const char *utf8,\n             ..."}, {"doc": "Keys whose held-down auto-repeat is safe and useful: text editing, cursor motion and scrolling. A Ctrl chord (tab spawn, reload, image toggle...) or Enter must NOT repeat -- holding them would loop a navigation or spawn tabs. A printable character * (n > 0) repeats so a held letter types, mirroring every text widget.", "kind": "function", "line": 10136, "name": "key_is_repeatable", "signature": "static int key_is_repeatable(xkb_keysym_t sym, int n, int ctrl)"}, {"doc": "Arms the repeat timer for key: first fire after repeat_delay ms, then every * 1/repeat_rate s. A held key thus repeats until released (key_repeat_stop).", "kind": "function", "line": 10152, "name": "key_repeat_arm", "signature": "static void key_repeat_arm(browser_window *w, uint32_t key)"}, {"doc": "1/repeat_rate s. A held key thus repeats until released (key_repeat_stop). static void key_repeat_arm(browser_window *w, uint32_t key) { if (w->repeat_timer_fd < 0 || w->repeat_rate <= 0) return; w->repeat_key = key; long interval_ns = 1000000000L / w->repeat_rate; struct itimerspec its; its.it_value.tv_sec  = w->repeat_delay / 1000; its.it_value.tv_nsec = (long)(w->repeat_delay % 1000) * 1000000L; its.it_interval.tv_sec  = interval_ns / 1000000000L; its.it_interval.tv_nsec = interval_ns % 1000000000L; timerfd_settime(w->repeat_timer_fd, 0, &its, NULL); } /* Disarms repeat (key released, or a non-repeatable key was pressed).", "kind": "function", "line": 10165, "name": "key_repeat_stop", "signature": "static void key_repeat_stop(browser_window *w)"}, {"doc": "Re-fires the currently held key. Called from the event loop when the timer expires. Modifiers/keysym are recomputed from the live xkb_state, so a chord released * mid-repeat degrades correctly.", "kind": "function", "line": 10176, "name": "key_repeat_fire", "signature": "static void key_repeat_fire(browser_window *w)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 10189, "name": "keyboard_key", "signature": "static void keyboard_key(void *data, struct wl_keyboard *kbd, uint32_t serial,\n                  ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 10229, "name": "keyboard_modifiers", "signature": "static void keyboard_modifiers(void *data, struct wl_keyboard *kbd, uint32_t s,\n                 ..."}, C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "gui/browser_ui_internal.h", "kind": "module", "label": "browser_ui_internal.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "7e12746ce072985a", "symbol_count": 6, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 35, "name": "ui_rgb"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 37, "name": "ui_theme"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_BROWSER_UI_INTERNAL_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 23, "name": "UI_FONT_SIZE"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 25, "name": "UI_TEXT_MARGIN"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 26, "name": "UI_HEADING_LEVELS"}]}, {"id": "gui/bui_theme.c", "kind": "module", "label": "bui_theme.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "6de0cc47170cb8c7", "symbol_count": 6, "symbols": [{"doc": "bui_theme — presentation palettes for the Wayland/Cairo GUI.  Carved out of browser_ui.c: the light/dark/sepia themes and the small colour helpers, none of which touch window state. The theme is the single source of truth for every font size, spacing and colour the renderer and chrome use.  #include \"browser_ui_internal.h\" #include \"css_color.h\" /* The single source of truth for the look of the browser.", "kind": "function", "line": 14, "name": "ui_theme_default", "signature": "ui_theme ui_theme_default(void)"}, {"doc": "Dark reading palette. Shares all the metrics (font sizes, spacing) with the * default theme; only the colours change, so the box model stays in one place.", "kind": "function", "line": 73, "name": "ui_theme_dark", "signature": "ui_theme ui_theme_dark(void)"}, {"doc": "Sepia reading palette: warm paper background and dark-brown ink, easier on the eyes for long-form text. Shares all the metrics with the default theme; only the * colours change.", "kind": "function", "line": 118, "name": "ui_theme_sepia", "signature": "ui_theme ui_theme_sepia(void)"}, {"doc": "t.menu_bg        = (ui_rgb){ 0.95, 0.90, 0.80 }; t.menu_border    = (ui_rgb){ 0.55, 0.46, 0.32 }; t.menu_text      = (ui_rgb){ 0.26, 0.19, 0.10 }; t.check_border   = (ui_rgb){ 0.45, 0.36, 0.24 }; t.check_mark     = (ui_rgb){ 0.35, 0.45, 0.20 }; t.toast_bg       = (ui_rgb){ 0.30, 0.23, 0.14 }; t.toast_text     = (ui_rgb){ 0.96, 0.92, 0.84 }; t.scrollbar_track     = (ui_rgb){ 0.84, 0.78, 0.66 }; t.scrollbar_thumb     = (ui_rgb){ 0.58, 0.48, 0.34 }; t.scrollbar_thumb_hot = (ui_rgb){ 0.44, 0.35, 0.22 }; return t; } /* The single place that maps the theme mode to a palette.", "kind": "function", "line": 160, "name": "ui_theme_for", "signature": "ui_theme ui_theme_for(int mode)"}, {"doc": "t.scrollbar_thumb_hot = (ui_rgb){ 0.44, 0.35, 0.22 }; return t; } /* The single place that maps the theme mode to a palette. ui_theme ui_theme_for(int mode) { switch (mode) { case UI_THEME_DARK:  return ui_theme_dark(); case UI_THEME_SEPIA: return ui_theme_sepia(); default:             return ui_theme_default(); } } /* Converts a packed 0xRRGGBB author color into a theme RGB triple.", "kind": "function", "line": 169, "name": "rgb_from_packed", "signature": "ui_rgb rgb_from_packed(int packed)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 173, "name": "set_rgb", "signature": "void set_rgb(cairo_t *cr, ui_rgb c)"}]}, {"id": "gui/freedom_view.c", "kind": "module", "label": "freedom_view.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "436c5901764b7601", "symbol_count": 2, "symbols": [{"kind": "function", "line": 36, "name": "main", "signature": "int main(int argc, char **argv)"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 9, "name": "_POSIX_C_SOURCE"}]}, {"id": "gui/ui_render.c", "kind": "module", "label": "ui_render.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "ef859eeb44701809", "symbol_count": 29, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 65, "name": "ui_window"}, {"doc": "Rewrites s in place to well-formed UTF-8, replacing any byte that is not part of a valid sequence with '?'. cairo_show_text rejects invalid UTF-8 and then silently stops drawing, so a single bad byte (e.g. a Latin-1 page) would blank the rest of the frame. Mirrors browser.c's sanitizer for the freedom-view tool, * which does not route text through browser_set_page. Output length <= input.", "kind": "function", "line": 37, "name": "sanitize_utf8_inplace", "signature": "static void sanitize_utf8_inplace(char *s)"}, {"doc": "int    use_csd;    /* draw our own titlebar/controls (compositor has no SSD) int    maximized; double ptr_x, ptr_y; const char *title; const char *text; size_t      text_len; size_t      scroll; /* first visible line } ui_window; static void redraw(ui_window *w); /* Right-aligned control buttons: minimize, maximize, close.", "kind": "function", "line": 105, "name": "button_rects", "signature": "static void button_rects(const ui_window *w, double *min_x, double *max_x, double *close_x)"}, {"doc": "size_t      text_len; size_t      scroll; /* first visible line } ui_window; static void redraw(ui_window *w); /* Right-aligned control buttons: minimize, maximize, close. static void button_rects(const ui_window *w, double *min_x, double *max_x, double *close_x) { close_x = w->width - UI_BTN_W; max_x   = w->width - 2 * UI_BTN_W; min_x   = w->width - 3 * UI_BTN_W; } /* --- shm buffer ---", "kind": "function", "line": 112, "name": "buffer_release", "signature": "static void buffer_release(void *data, struct wl_buffer *wl_buffer)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 119, "name": "destroy_buffer", "signature": "static void destroy_buffer(ui_window *w)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 125, "name": "ensure_buffer", "signature": "static int ensure_buffer(ui_window *w)"}, {"doc": "WL_SHM_FORMAT_ARGB8888); wl_shm_pool_destroy(pool); close(fd); if (w->buffer == NULL) { munmap(data, size); return -1; } wl_buffer_add_listener(w->buffer, &buffer_listener, w); w->shm_data = data; w->shm_size = size; w->cairo_surface = cairo_image_surface_create_for_data( (unsigned char *)data, CAIRO_FORMAT_ARGB32, w->width, w->height, stride); return (cairo_surface_status(w->cairo_surface) == CAIRO_STATUS_SUCCESS) ? 0 : -1; } /* --- painting ---", "kind": "function", "line": 156, "name": "paint", "signature": "static void paint(ui_window *w)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 242, "name": "redraw", "signature": "static void redraw(ui_window *w)"}, {"doc": "Always repaint and re-commit. We do not gate on buffer release: a single shm buffer can be re-attached, and gating on release deadlocks against * compositors that only release once a new buffer arrives. paint(w); wl_surface_attach(w->surface, w->buffer, 0, 0); wl_surface_damage_buffer(w->surface, 0, 0, w->width, w->height); wl_surface_commit(w->surface); } /* --- xdg-shell ---", "kind": "function", "line": 257, "name": "wm_base_ping", "signature": "static void wm_base_ping(void *data, struct xdg_wm_base *b, uint32_t serial)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 263, "name": "xdg_surface_configure", "signature": "static void xdg_surface_configure(void *data, struct xdg_surface *s, uint32_t serial)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 271, "name": "toplevel_configure", "signature": "static void toplevel_configure(void *data, struct xdg_toplevel *t,\n                              ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 281, "name": "toplevel_close", "signature": "static void toplevel_close(void *data, struct xdg_toplevel *t)"}, {"doc": "The compositor tells us the actual decoration mode it granted. If it insists * on client-side, we draw our own titlebar; if server-side, we let it decorate.", "kind": "function", "line": 292, "name": "deco_configure", "signature": "static void deco_configure(void *data, struct zxdg_toplevel_decoration_v1 *d, uint32_t mode)"}, {"doc": "The compositor tells us the actual decoration mode it granted. If it insists * on client-side, we draw our own titlebar; if server-side, we let it decorate. static void deco_configure(void *data, struct zxdg_toplevel_decoration_v1 *d, uint32_t mode) { (void)d; ui_window *w = (ui_window *)data; w->use_csd = (mode == ZXDG_TOPLEVEL_DECORATION_V1_MODE_CLIENT_SIDE); fprintf(stderr, \"[ui] decoration granted: %s\\n\", w->use_csd ? \"client-side\" : \"server-side\"); redraw(w); } static const struct zxdg_toplevel_decoration_v1_listener deco_listener = { deco_configure }; /* --- input (pointer axis = scroll) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 302, "name": "ptr_enter", "signature": "static void ptr_enter(void *d, struct wl_pointer *p, uint32_t s,\n                      struct wl_..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 310, "name": "ptr_leave", "signature": "static void ptr_leave(void *d, struct wl_pointer *p, uint32_t s, struct wl_surface *sf)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 313, "name": "ptr_motion", "signature": "static void ptr_motion(void *d, struct wl_pointer *p, uint32_t t, wl_fixed_t x, wl_fixed_t y)"}, {"doc": "ui_window *w = (ui_window *)d; w->ptr_x = wl_fixed_to_double(x); w->ptr_y = wl_fixed_to_double(y); } static void ptr_leave(void *d, struct wl_pointer *p, uint32_t s, struct wl_surface *sf) { (void)d; (void)p; (void)s; (void)sf; } static void ptr_motion(void *d, struct wl_pointer *p, uint32_t t, wl_fixed_t x, wl_fixed_t y) { (void)p; (void)t; ui_window *w = (ui_window *)d; w->ptr_x = wl_fixed_to_double(x); w->ptr_y = wl_fixed_to_double(y); } /* Left-press on the client-side titlebar: hit-test the controls, else drag.", "kind": "function", "line": 320, "name": "ptr_button", "signature": "static void ptr_button(void *d, struct wl_pointer *p, uint32_t serial, uint32_t t,\n              ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 342, "name": "ptr_axis", "signature": "static void ptr_axis(void *data, struct wl_pointer *p, uint32_t time,\n                     uint32..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 364, "name": "seat_caps", "signature": "static void seat_caps(void *data, struct wl_seat *seat, uint32_t caps)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 374, "name": "seat_name", "signature": "static void seat_name(void *d, struct wl_seat *s, const char *name)"}, {"doc": "ui_window *w = (ui_window *)data; fprintf(stderr, \"[ui] seat caps: pointer=%s\\n\", (caps & WL_SEAT_CAPABILITY_POINTER) ? C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/box_style.h", "kind": "module", "label": "box_style.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "031345383ad6b867", "symbol_count": 4, "symbols": [{"doc": "See spec/box_style.md for the full contract.  typedef enum bx_display { BX_DISPLAY_BLOCK = 0,     /* stacks vertically, takes the available width BX_DISPLAY_INLINE,        /* flows with text; vertical margins ignored by layout BX_DISPLAY_INLINE_BLOCK,  /* in-line box with its own dimensions (img, input) BX_DISPLAY_LIST_ITEM,     /* like block, but the layout draws a marker (li) BX_DISPLAY_FLEX,          /* flex container: resolved by flex_layout BX_DISPLAY_GRID,          /* grid container: resolved by flex_layout BX_DISPLAY_NONE           /* not rendered and takes no space (head, script...) } bx_display; /* Box edges in em (multiples of the element's own font size).", "kind": "struct", "line": 39, "name": "bx_edges"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 43, "name": "bx_box"}, {"doc": "typedef struct bx_box { bx_display display; bx_edges   margin; bx_edges   padding; } bx_box; typedef enum bx_status { BX_OK = 0, BX_ERR_NULL_ARG,  /* token or out was NULL BX_ERR_SYNTAX     /* not a recognised display keyword (fails closed) } bx_status; /* Horizontal placement of an author box inside an available width (px).", "kind": "struct", "line": 56, "name": "bx_hplace"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_BOX_STYLE_H"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_BOX_TREE_H define FREEDOM_BOX_TREE_H  include <stddef.h>  include \"box_style.h\" include \"flex_layout.h\" include \"page_view.h\"   /* pv_box_def (box tree) */  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/box_tree.h", "kind": "module", "label": "box_tree.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "b48bfda3118e6718", "symbol_count": 15, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 58, "name": "bt_node"}, {"doc": "Stage 2: one positioned box in the final paint order. C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/compositor.h", "kind": "module", "label": "compositor.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "e6224deec2231ccf", "symbol_count": 3, "symbols": [{"doc": "A box's resolved style, in the SAME value-spaces as css.h: position uses css_position (CSS_POS_STATIC/RELATIVE/ABSOLUTE/FIXED/STICKY), mix_blend uses css_mix_blend (0 unset, CSS_MB_NORMAL, or a real blend), isolation uses css_isolation (0 unset, CSS_ISO_AUTO, CSS_ISO_ISOLATE). z_auto == 1 means the z-index is auto/unset (z_index is then ignored). opacity is a percent 0..100, or -1 for unset (opaque). is_float / is_inline classify the box's in-flow role. has_transform (M1.2): 1 iff the box has an author `transform` other than none (today: translate()/translateX()/translateY() only) -- a transformed element * establishes a stacking context per spec regardless of the actual offset.", "kind": "struct", "line": 46, "name": "cx_style"}, {"doc": "One box to order for painting: its layer, z-index (z_auto treated as 0 within the ZERO_Z layer), document order (stable tie-break), and an opaque caller handle * (e.g. a box index) that this module never interprets.", "kind": "struct", "line": 69, "name": "cx_item"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_COMPOSITOR_H"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_CSS_H define FREEDOM_CSS_H  include <stddef.h> include \"css_color.h\"  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/css.h", "kind": "module", "label": "css.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "9b4a7be15c20e86d", "symbol_count": 35, "symbols": [{"doc": "A resolved presentation. Each field uses a sentinel for \"unset\" so the caller can layer inheritance (take the first ancestor that sets each inheriting one). The flex/grid container fields (gap/justify/grid_cols) are NOT inherited: they describe the container element itself, so the caller reads them from that * element's resolved style, not up the ancestor chain.", "kind": "struct", "line": 431, "name": "css_style"}, {"doc": "Render-time media context for evaluating @media at parse time. width_px is a fixed, normalized desktop width, so a (min/max-width) query leaks no real viewport * size (anti-fingerprinting).", "kind": "struct", "line": 650, "name": "css_media"}, {"doc": "One element attribute, for attribute selectors ([attr], [attr=v], [attr~=v], ...). name is the lowercased local name; value is the attribute text (\"\" if empty). Both * alias caller storage (nothing is copied/owned).", "kind": "struct", "line": 687, "name": "css_attr"}, {"doc": "An element plus its ancestor chain, for combinator matching. Each field aliases caller storage (nothing is copied/owned). parent walks toward the root (NULL at the top). A bounded/partial chain is fine: a descendant compound that would have matched a missing deeper ancestor simply does not match (fail closed). attrs/nattrs * may be empty (NULL/0): an attribute selector then simply does not match.", "kind": "struct", "line": 697, "name": "css_element"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_CSS_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 65, "name": C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/css_select.h", "kind": "module", "label": "css_select.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "38afb2c9703b6c7e", "symbol_count": 15, "symbols": [{"doc": "Sub-selector for :not()/:is()/:where(): a simple compound with only tag/.class/#id/[attr] (no combinators, no pseudo-classes inside * sub-selectors). Bounded: CSS_MAX_SUB_SELS per selector, one level deep. #define CSS_MAX_SUB_SELS 4 #define CSS_SUB_MAX_ATTRS 1 /* One attribute selector inside a compound: name OP value, with a case flag.", "kind": "struct", "line": 65, "name": "css_attr_match"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 72, "name": "css_sub_sel"}, {"doc": "One pseudo-class inside a compound. a/b are the An+B coefficients of the nth-child family (unused otherwise). sub_first/sub_count index into the * parent css_sel.subs[] for :not/:is/:where, or -1/0 for no sub-selectors.", "kind": "struct", "line": 86, "name": "css_pseudo_match"}, {"doc": "One compound selector: optional type, optional id, zero+ classes, zero+ [attr], * zero+ pseudo-classes.", "kind": "struct", "line": 96, "name": "css_compound"}, {"doc": "A complex selector: a chain of compounds, parts[nparts-1] being the subject (the element a rule styles). comb[k] (k>=1) is the combinator to the LEFT of parts[k]; comb[0] is unused. A single compound is nparts == 1. subs[] holds sub-selectors * for :not()/:is()/:where() pseudo-classes, indexed by css_pseudo_match.sub_first.", "kind": "struct", "line": 113, "name": "css_sel"}, {"doc": "True if *sel matches element *el against its ancestor chain. target_id (optional) is the URL fragment for :target matching; NULL -> never matches. allow_pseudo_el: 1 = CSS cascade mode (::before/::after match), 0 = DOM * query mode (pseudo-elements never match real elements). int csel_matches(const css_sel *sel, const css_element *el, const char *target_id, int allow_pseudo_el); /* --- ASCII helpers shared by the selector and cascade sides (internal) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 136, "name": "csel_lower_ch", "signature": "static inline char csel_lower_ch(char c)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 140, "name": "csel_ci_eq", "signature": "static inline int csel_ci_eq(const char *a, const char *b)"}, {"doc": "static inline char csel_lower_ch(char c) { return (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z') ? (char)(c + 32) : c; } static inline int csel_ci_eq(const char *a, const char *b) { while (*a != '\\0' && *b != '\\0') { if (csel_lower_ch(*a) != csel_lower_ch(*b)) return 0; ++a; ++b; } return *a == '\\0' && *b == '\\0'; } /* True if a[0,n) equals b[0,n) up to n chars (b NUL-terminated), case-folded when ci.", "kind": "function", "line": 150, "name": "csel_span_eq", "signature": "static inline int csel_span_eq(const char *a, const char *b, size_t n, int ci)"}, {"doc": "Substring test (used both to drop any value carrying url() — always ci — and by * the attribute `*=` operator). A non-empty needle only; an empty one never matches.", "kind": "function", "line": 161, "name": "csel_substr", "signature": "static inline int csel_substr(const char *hay, const char *needle, int ci)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 168, "name": "csel_ident_ch", "signature": "static inline int csel_ident_ch(char c)"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_CSS_SELECT_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 27, "name": "CSS_TOK_MAX"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 28, "name": "CSS_MAX_CLASSES_PER_SEL"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 61, "name": "CSS_MAX_SUB_SELS"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 62, "name": "CSS_SUB_MAX_ATTRS"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_DATA_URL_H define FREEDOM_DATA_URL_H  include <stddef.h> include <stdint.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). Caps the O(n) shrink loop and * keeps the fixed-size scratch buffers bounded (anti-DoS; no VLAs). #define FX_MAX_ITEMS 512u typedef enum fx_justify { FX_JUSTIFY_START = 0, FX_JUSTIFY_END, FX_JUSTIFY_CENTER, FX_JUSTIFY_SPACE_BETWEEN, FX_JUSTIFY_SPACE_AROUND, FX_JUSTIFY_SPACE_EVENLY } fx_justify; /* One flex item, all sizes in px. Zero-initialise with fb_buffer_init.", "kind": "struct", "line": 46, "name": "fb_buffer"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_FREEBUG_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 56, "name": "FB_MAX_ENTRIES"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 57, "name": "FB_MAX_ENTRY_BYTES"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 58, "name": "FB_MAX_TOTAL_BYTES"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 62, "name": "FB_MAX_FILE_BYTES"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_HLS_H define FREEDOM_HLS_H  include <stddef.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). URLs are returned as they appear in the playlist (may be relative to the playlist's base URL).  See spec/hls.md for the full contract.  typedef enum hls_status { HLS_OK = 0, HLS_ERR_NULL_ARG, HLS_ERR_PARSE,      /* not a valid m3u8 playlist HLS_ERR_OOM } hls_status; /* One segment in the playlist.", "kind": "struct", "line": 29, "name": "hls_segment"}, {"doc": "HLS_OK = 0, HLS_ERR_NULL_ARG, HLS_ERR_PARSE,      /* not a valid m3u8 playlist HLS_ERR_OOM } hls_status; /* One segment in the playlist. typedef struct hls_segment { char  *url;          /* owned; the raw segment URL as written in the playlist size_t url_len; double duration;     /* segment duration in seconds (from #EXTINF) } hls_segment; /* One variant (quality tier) in a multi-variant (master) playlist.", "kind": "struct", "line": 36, "name": "hls_variant"}, {"doc": "size_t url_len; double duration;     /* segment duration in seconds (from #EXTINF) } hls_segment; /* One variant (quality tier) in a multi-variant (master) playlist. typedef struct hls_variant { char  *url;          /* owned; the variant playlist URL size_t url_len; long   bandwidth;    /* BANDWIDTH from #EXT-X-STREAM-INF, or 0 if absent int    width;        /* RESOLUTION width, or 0 int    height;       /* RESOLUTION height, or 0 } hls_variant; /* Parsed playlist.", "kind": "struct", "line": 45, "name": "hls_playlist"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_HLS_H"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_HOSTBLOCK_H define FREEDOM_HOSTBLOCK_H  include <stddef.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). Inline: text is a NUL-terminated buffer of `len` bytes (the NUL is not counted in len) and src == NULL. External: src is the RAW, unresolved value of the src attribute (hostile input; the parser never fetches) * and text == NULL. Both buffers are owned by the hp_script array.", "kind": "struct", "line": 76, "name": "hp_script"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_HTML_PARSE_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 40, "name": "HP_DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 48, "name": "HP_MAX_SCRIPTS"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 111, "name": "HP_MAX_STYLESHEETS"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_IMAGE_DECODE_H define FREEDOM_IMAGE_DECODE_H  include <stddef.h> include <stdint.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/image_decode.h", "kind": "module", "label": "image_decode.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "0ac63baeffb744d2", "symbol_count": 4, "symbols": [{"doc": "A decoded bitmap that owns its pixel buffer. The pixel format is Cairo's native premultiplied ARGB32: each pixel is a uint32_t 0xAARRGGBB, i.e. bytes B,G,R,A in little-endian memory, colour premultiplied by alpha. stride == width*4 (tightly packed). This module never includes cairo.h; it only honours that layout so the * parent can wrap data in a cairo_surface_t without re-encoding.", "kind": "struct", "line": 56, "name": "img_pixels"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_IMAGE_DECODE_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 65, "name": "IMG_MAX_DIM"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 66, "name": "IMG_MAX_PIXELS"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_INTERP_H define FREEDOM_INTERP_H  include <stdint.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11).
