C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/os_sandbox.h", "kind": "module", "label": "os_sandbox.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "dd91d28fa27e0c24", "symbol_count": 2, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 108, "name": "os_fs_rule"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_OS_SANDBOX_H"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_PAGE_VIEW_H define FREEDOM_PAGE_VIEW_H  include <stddef.h> include <stdint.h>  include \"dom.h\" include \"html_parse.h\"  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/page_view.h", "kind": "module", "label": "page_view.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "89eb1f9d391d8f72", "symbol_count": 10, "symbols": [{"doc": "One inline run in document order. text is owned, NUL-terminated, valid UTF-8 (the alt text for PV_IMAGE, possibly empty). href is owned and NUL-terminated for PV_LINK runs, NULL otherwise. src is owned and NUL-terminated for PV_IMAGE runs (the image URL, data with provenance, never fetched here), NULL otherwise; img_w/img_h carry the declared <img> dimensions in pixels, or -1 when unknown. An image is never a link, so href and src are never both set. fg_rgb carries the author foreground color (nearest inline style \"color:\" or <font color>) packed as 0xRRGGBB, or -1 when none/unparseable; it is presentation data the renderer * applies only when author CSS is enabled.", "kind": "struct", "line": 97, "name": "pv_run"}, {"doc": "Box engine (Hito 23b-8 Step D): one entry of the box-definition TREE. The box decoration and the parent link live here, not on each run, so a box is described once (extinguishing the Step A per-run duplication) and a TEXT-LESS wrapper box (one that owns no direct run, e.g. a card div whose only child is a body div) is still representable. A box's block_id IS its index in pv_view.boxes; a run carries only its block_id (which box it belongs to) and finds its parent via boxes[block_id].parent_id. parent_id is the block_id of the nearest box-carrying block ancestor, or -1 for a root box. The decoration fields mirror the Step A sentinels exactly (border widths/radius/outline width PV_LEN_UNSET, colors -1, the rest 0). The whole list is author presentation: render_doc copies it only with * caps.css (empty otherwise -> default render byte-identical).", "kind": "struct", "line": 282, "name": "pv_box_def"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 429, "name": "pv_view"}, {"doc": "The author text-presentation extensions of one run, resolved from the nearest ancestor that sets each field (they all inherit in CSS). The 20+ fields used to be positional parameters of pv_set_text_ext -- a signature that grew with every property batch; the struct is the contract now. list_style is resolved here too but is NOT a run field: page_view bakes the <li> marker into the run text (structure), so pv_set_text_ext ignores it. Initialise with pv_text_ext_reset * (every field to its \"unset\" sentinel), then set what the caller resolved.", "kind": "struct", "line": 562, "name": "pv_text_ext"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_PAGE_VIEW_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 42, "name": "PV_LEN_UNSET"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 43, "name": "PV_LEN_AUTO"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 44, "name": "PV_LEN_END"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 47, "name": "PV_GRID_TRACKS"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 53, "name": "PV_BG_URL_MAX"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_PDF_EXPORT_H define FREEDOM_PDF_EXPORT_H  include <stddef.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/pdf_export.h", "kind": "module", "label": "pdf_export.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "1f551b9bec69d38c", "symbol_count": 5, "symbols": [{"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_PDF_EXPORT_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 27, "name": "PE_NAME_MAX"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 29, "name": "PE_EXT"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 30, "name": "PE_EXT_PNG"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 31, "name": "PE_FALLBACK_NAME"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_PERF_TRACE_H define FREEDOM_PERF_TRACE_H  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif  include <stddef.h> include <stdint.h>", "id": "include/perf_trace.h", "kind": "module", "label": "perf_trace.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "92884861782fecca", "symbol_count": 4, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 34, "name": "pt_stage_stats"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 41, "name": "pt_trace"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_PERF_TRACE_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 31, "name": "PT_MAX_SAMPLES"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_PREFETCH_H define FREEDOM_PREFETCH_H  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif  include <stddef.h> include <pthread.h>", "id": "include/prefetch.h", "kind": "module", "label": "prefetch.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "5bc7cf1462e9f72f", "symbol_count": 8, "symbols": [{"doc": "One scanned reference. url is the RAW attribute value (the policy-gated fetcher * resolves and validates it, exactly as it does for the worker's own request).", "kind": "struct", "line": 42, "name": "pf_ref"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 50, "name": "pf_list"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 77, "name": "pf_job"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 87, "name": "pf_pool"}, {"doc": "Cache-first fetcher adapter, shared by every frontend (DRY): install pf_pooled_fetch as the tab fetcher with a pf_gated_fetch as its ctx. A GET whose URL is pooled is served from the pool (a failed prefetch propagates the failure -- never refetched); everything else (POST, miss, pool == NULL) goes straight to the inner policy-gated fetcher. The pf_gated_fetch must outlive * the tab_load_full call; reset the tab's fetcher to the inner one afterwards.", "kind": "struct", "line": 129, "name": "pf_gated_fetch"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_PREFETCH_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 46, "name": "PF_MAX_REFS"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 48, "name": "PF_MAX_THREADS"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_PREFS_H define FREEDOM_PREFS_H  include <stddef.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/prefs.h", "kind": "module", "label": "prefs.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "68c67e72c480a26d", "symbol_count": 10, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 45, "name": "prefs_entry"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 50, "name": "prefs_state"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_PREFS_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 25, "name": "PREFS_VERSION"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 27, "name": "PREFS_MAX_URL"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 28, "name": "PREFS_MAX_TITLE"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 29, "name": "PREFS_MAX_BOOKMARKS"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 30, "name": "PREFS_MAX_HISTORY"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 31, "name": "PREFS_MAX_TEXT"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 34, "name": "PREFS_PAGE_HISTORY"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_PROFILE_H define FREEDOM_PROFILE_H  include <stddef.h> include <stdint.h>  include \"local_store.h\" include \"prefs.h\"  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/profile.h", "kind": "module", "label": "profile.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "b62fb6f6867fb667", "symbol_count": 4, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 46, "name": "profile_ctx"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_PROFILE_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 31, "name": "PROFILE_KEY_FILE"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 33, "name": "PROFILE_PREFS_FILE"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_PSL_DATA_H define FREEDOM_PSL_DATA_H  include <stddef.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/psl_data.h", "kind": "module", "label": "psl_data.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "43afac7c39105cd1", "symbol_count": 1, "symbols": [{"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_PSL_DATA_H"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_RENDER_DOC_H define FREEDOM_RENDER_DOC_H  include <stddef.h>  include \"page_view.h\" include \"render_policy.h\"  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/render_doc.h", "kind": "module", "label": "render_doc.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "c9fe15c429823f04", "symbol_count": 3, "symbols": [{"doc": "One paint-ready block in document order. text is owned, NUL-terminated and valid UTF-8. href is owned and NUL-terminated for RD_LINK (link target), RD_IMAGE (image src) and RD_INPUT (the owning form's action); NULL otherwise. img_decision is meaningful only for RD_IMAGE. The input_* fields are meaningful only for RD_INPUT: input_type is a pv_input_type; name/value are owned (NULL when absent) and carry the submitted bytes verbatim; form_id groups controls of one * form (-1 = none); form_method is a pv_form_method.", "kind": "struct", "line": 60, "name": "rd_block"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 181, "name": "rd_doc"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_RENDER_DOC_H"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_RENDER_POLICY_H define FREEDOM_RENDER_POLICY_H  include <stdbool.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/render_policy.h", "kind": "module", "label": "render_policy.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "af7d59a35715fe12", "symbol_count": 3, "symbols": [{"doc": "Render capabilities for a page. The zero value ({0}) is the safe baseline: every rich/risky capability off. The only way to opt in is to set a field to * true explicitly, which keeps every opt-in auditable.", "kind": "struct", "line": 32, "name": "rdp_caps"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_RENDER_POLICY_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 27, "name": "RDP_TRACKER_MAX_DIM"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_RENDERER_H define FREEDOM_RENDERER_H  include <stddef.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/tab.h", "kind": "module", "label": "tab.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "3af4ae2309820bf4", "symbol_count": 5, "symbols": [{"doc": "Inert result of loading a page: title + extracted text + structured display list (all owned). text is kept for headless/plain output; view carries the * inline runs (headings, links, block breaks) the GUI lays out and paints.", "kind": "struct", "line": 49, "name": "tab_page"}, {"doc": "The page's document.cookie jar after the scripts ran (\"name=value; ...\"), or NULL when the jar was disabled (untrusted host) or empty. The trusted parent folds it back into the ephemeral network jar so JS-set session cookies reach the next request. In-memory only; never persisted (Zero Knowledge). Owned; freed by * tab_page_free. char    *set_cookies; } tab_page; /* Result of evaluating script: the value, or a JS error message.", "kind": "struct", "line": 80, "name": "tab_eval_result"}, {"doc": "Pixels of a decoded image, produced inside the confined worker. data is owned and holds premultiplied ARGB32 (Cairo's CAIRO_FORMAT_ARGB32 layout: bytes B,G,R,A; stride == width*4). data == NULL means the worker could not decode the * bytes (not a PNG, corrupt, or out of bounds): the caller shows the placeholder.", "kind": "struct", "line": 93, "name": "tab_image"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_TAB_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 100, "name": "TAB_MAX_INPUT"}]}, {"id": "include/text_shape.h", "kind": "module", "label": "text_shape.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "b709d31356fc8abe", "symbol_count": 4, "symbols": [{"doc": "Font selector: a css_font_family bucket (CSS_FF_*) plus weight/slant flags. * The engine matches the generic group only, never an exact author family.", "kind": "struct", "line": 30, "name": "tsh_font"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 19, "name": "FREEDOM_TEXT_SHAPE_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 35, "name": "TSH_MAX_GLYPHS"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 37, "name": "TSH_MAX_TEXT"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_TEXTFIELD_H define FREEDOM_TEXTFIELD_H  include <stddef.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/tls_impersonate.h", "kind": "module", "label": "tls_impersonate.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "591a247c180ede93", "symbol_count": 12, "symbols": [{"doc": "Request: parent -> helper. Pointers are borrowed by ti_encode_req (not copied); * ti_decode_req allocates its own copies, released by ti_req_free.", "kind": "struct", "line": 68, "name": "ti_req"}, {"doc": "Request: parent -> helper. Pointers are borrowed by ti_encode_req (not copied); * ti_decode_req allocates its own copies, released by ti_req_free. typedef struct ti_req { const char   *url;        /* https only on this route (bounded TI_MAX_URL) const char   *method;     /* \"GET\" / \"POST\" (bounded TI_MAX_METHOD) const char   *headers;    /* CRLF-joined, already normalized by the parent const uint8_t *body; size_t        body_len; ti_profile    profile; } ti_req; /* Response: helper -> parent. ti_decode_resp allocates; ti_resp_free releases.", "kind": "struct", "line": 78, "name": "ti_resp"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_TLS_IMPERSONATE_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 56, "name": "TI_MAGIC"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 57, "name": "TI_MAX_URL"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 58, "name": "TI_MAX_METHOD"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 59, "name": "TI_MAX_HEADERS"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 60, "name": "TI_MAX_BODY"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 61, "name": "TI_MAX_RESP_HDR"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 62, "name": "TI_MAX_RESP_BODY"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 63, "name": "TI_MAX_CHAIN"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 64, "name": "TI_MAX_GROUP"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_UI_H define FREEDOM_UI_H  include <stddef.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/ui.h", "kind": "module", "label": "ui.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "1fd11b8323bd4cd4", "symbol_count": 5, "symbols": [{"doc": "A laid-out line is a contiguous slice [offset, offset+len) of the source * text (no copy): render with text + offset over len bytes.", "kind": "struct", "line": 29, "name": "ui_line"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 34, "name": "ui_layout"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 68, "name": "rd_doc"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 94, "name": "tab"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_UI_H"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_URL_H define FREEDOM_URL_H  include <stddef.h>  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/url.h", "kind": "module", "label": "url.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "9bd6132a70820ea7", "symbol_count": 4, "symbols": [{"doc": "Components of a validated absolute https URL, sliced for a JS location object. Every field ALIASES the input url (not owned, valid while url is alive); lengths exclude any NUL. Absent components have length 0. pathname is the literal path * span and is empty when the URL has no path (the location shim presents \"\" as \"/\").", "kind": "struct", "line": 129, "name": "url_parts"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_URL_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 30, "name": "URL_MAX_LEN"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 76, "name": "URL_SEARCH_ENDPOINT"}]}, {"doc": "util.h — shared pure helpers (no I/O except where noted). Static inline so each compilation unit gets its own copy without adding link dependencies.", "id": "include/util.h", "kind": "module", "label": "util.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "f6949866a4f2c8ff", "symbol_count": 5, "symbols": [{"doc": "util.h — shared pure helpers (no I/O except where noted). Static inline so each * compilation unit gets its own copy without adding link dependencies. #ifndef UTIL_H #define UTIL_H #include <stddef.h> #include <stdint.h> #include <unistd.h> #include <errno.h> /* --- framed pipe I/O (EINTR-safe, all-or-nothing) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 13, "name": "write_full", "signature": "static inline int write_full(int fd, const void *buf, size_t n)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 24, "name": "read_full", "signature": "static inline int read_full(int fd, void *buf, size_t n)"}, {"doc": "static inline int read_full(int fd, void *buf, size_t n) { uint8_t *p = (uint8_t *)buf; size_t got = 0; while (got < n) { ssize_t r = read(fd, p + got, n - got); if (r < 0) { if (errno == EINTR) continue; return -1; } if (r == 0) return -1; /* unexpected EOF got += (size_t)r; } return 0; } /* --- UTF-8 lead-byte length table ---", "kind": "function", "line": 38, "name": "utf8_seq_len", "signature": "static inline size_t utf8_seq_len(unsigned char c)"}, {"doc": "return 0; } /* --- UTF-8 lead-byte length table --- static inline size_t utf8_seq_len(unsigned char c) { if (c < 0x80) return 1; if (c >= 0xC2 && c <= 0xDF) return 2; if (c >= 0xE0 && c <= 0xEF) return 3; if (c >= 0xF0 && c <= 0xF4) return 4; return 0; } /* --- FNV-1a 64-bit hash ---", "kind": "function", "line": 48, "name": "fnv1a", "signature": "static inline uint64_t fnv1a(const char *s, size_t n)"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 5, "name": "UTIL_H"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_WEBCAPS_H define FREEDOM_WEBCAPS_H  include <stdbool.h>  include \"js_policy.h\" include \"render_policy.h\"  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/webcaps.h", "kind": "module", "label": "webcaps.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "b7b56f7274397ad7", "symbol_count": 3, "symbols": [{"doc": "Full capability table for one page. The zero value ({0}) is the safe baseline: every capability off. Only an explicit derivation turns a field on, keeping every * opt-in auditable.", "kind": "struct", "line": 38, "name": "wc_caps"}, {"doc": "Per-host derivation inputs (GUI path). host_in_js / host_in_allow are nonzero iff the page host is on js.conf / allow.conf (e.g. hb_is_allowlisted, subdomain-aware). user_css / user_images are the user's session toggles; reader is distraction-free * (reader) mode.", "kind": "struct", "line": 57, "name": "wc_input"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_WEBCAPS_H"}]}, {"doc": "ifndef FREEDOM_ZOOM_H define FREEDOM_ZOOM_H  ifdef __cplusplus error \"Freedom is pure C (C11). C++ is not supported.\" endif", "id": "include/zoom.h", "kind": "module", "label": "zoom.h", "language": "h", "sha256": "baff91fb85e6f24f", "symbol_count": 4, "symbols": [{"kind": "macro", "line": 2, "name": "FREEDOM_ZOOM_H"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 20, "name": "ZM_MIN_PCT"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 22, "name": "ZM_MAX_PCT"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 23, "name": "ZM_DEFAULT_PCT"}]}, {"doc": "Exit immediately if a command exits with a non-zero status, if an undefined variable is used, or if any pipe fails.", "id": "install.sh", "kind": "module", "label": "install.sh", "language": "sh", "sha256": "c907d80fd6734993", "symbol_count": 0, "symbols": []}, {"doc": "Thin wrapper. Launches a nested weston (for boxes without a Wayland session), then runs the browser through the Makefile (single source of truth for the build). Usage: ./run_freedom.sh [url]", "id": "run_freedom.sh", "kind": "module", "label": "run_freedom.sh", "language": "sh", "sha256": "2dbc344946a584c6", "symbol_count": 0, "symbols": []}, {"id": "src/anti_fp.c", "kind": "module", "label": "anti_fp.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "2b96cd47c0b0021a", "symbol_count": 23, "symbols": [{"kind": "function", "line": 16, "name": "fp_coarsen_time_ms", "signature": "uint64_t fp_coarsen_time_ms(uint64_t raw_ms)"}, {"doc": "#include \"anti_fp.h\" /* --- clocks --- uint64_t fp_timer_resolution_ms(void) { return FP_TIMER_RESOLUTION_MS; } uint64_t fp_coarsen_time_ms(uint64_t raw_ms) { return raw_ms - (raw_ms % FP_TIMER_RESOLUTION_MS); } /* --- normalized identity (shared by all Freedom users) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 22, "name": "fp_user_agent", "signature": "const char *fp_user_agent(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 26, "name": "fp_accept_language", "signature": "const char *fp_accept_language(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 30, "name": "fp_accept_language_header", "signature": "const char *fp_accept_language_header(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 34, "name": "fp_timezone", "signature": "const char *fp_timezone(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 38, "name": "fp_platform", "signature": "const char *fp_platform(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 42, "name": "fp_vendor", "signature": "const char *fp_vendor(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 46, "name": "fp_hardware_concurrency", "signature": "int fp_hardware_concurrency(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 50, "name": "fp_device_memory_gb", "signature": "int fp_device_memory_gb(void)"}, {"doc": "const char *fp_vendor(void) { return \"\"; /* no vendor string: minimal entropy } int fp_hardware_concurrency(void) { return 2; /* fixed: do not reveal the real core count } int fp_device_memory_gb(void) { return 8; /* fixed: do not reveal the real memory size } /* --- legacy navigator properties (Hito 30b: blend-in) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 56, "name": "fp_app_version", "signature": "const char *fp_app_version(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 60, "name": "fp_app_code_name", "signature": "const char *fp_app_code_name(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 64, "name": "fp_product", "signature": "const char *fp_product(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 68, "name": "fp_app_name", "signature": "const char *fp_app_name(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 72, "name": "fp_product_sub", "signature": "const char *fp_product_sub(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 76, "name": "fp_oscpu", "signature": "const char *fp_oscpu(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 80, "name": "fp_build_id", "signature": "const char *fp_build_id(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 84, "name": "fp_max_touch_points", "signature": "int fp_max_touch_points(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 88, "name": "fp_on_line", "signature": "int fp_on_line(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 92, "name": "fp_cookie_enabled", "signature": "int fp_cookie_enabled(void)"}, {"doc": "int fp_max_touch_points(void) { return 0; } int fp_on_line(void) { return 1; } int fp_cookie_enabled(void) { return 1; } /* --- screen bucketing ---", "kind": "function", "line": 98, "name": "fp_bucket_screen", "signature": "void fp_bucket_screen(int w, int h, int *out_w, int *out_h)"}, {"doc": "long best_area = -1; size_t best = smallest; for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) { if (buckets[i][0] <= w && buckets[i][1] <= h) { long area = (long)buckets[i][0] * (long)buckets[i][1]; if (area > best_area) { best_area = area; best = i; } } } if (out_w != NULL) *out_w = buckets[best][0]; if (out_h != NULL) *out_h = buckets[best][1]; } /* --- readback poisoning ---", "kind": "function", "line": 120, "name": "splitmix64", "signature": "static uint64_t splitmix64(uint64_t *state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 127, "name": "fp_perturb", "signature": "void fp_perturb(uint8_t *buf, size_t len, uint64_t session_key)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 138, "name": "fp_origin_key", "signature": "uint64_t fp_origin_key(uint64_t session_key, const char *registrable_domain)"}]}, {"id": "src/box_style.c", "kind": "module", "label": "box_style.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "64b399cca6489d27", "symbol_count": 23, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 53, "name": "tag_row"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 129, "name": "disp_row"}, {"doc": "Longest recognised tag is \"blockquote\" (10); longest display keyword is \"inline-block\" (12). The buffers are generous so a normal token always fits and * anything longer fails closed instead of truncating. define BX_TAG_MAX     32u define BX_DISPLAY_MAX 24u", "kind": "function", "line": 23, "name": "lc", "signature": "static char lc(char c)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 27, "name": "is_ws", "signature": "static int is_ws(char c)"}, {"doc": "Copies in into out, trimming ASCII whitespace and lowercasing. "sha256": "20d7e8752bfad7f0", "symbol_count": 12, "symbols": [{"doc": "Block container: stack non-none children vertically, collapsing each child's top * margin with the previous child's bottom margin.", "kind": "function", "line": 35, "name": "layout_block", "signature": "static bt_status layout_block(bt_node *node, bt_node *const *kids, size_t nk,\n                   ..."}, {"doc": "if (cavail < 0.0) cavail = 0.0; bt_status r = layout_node(c, cavail, depth + 1); if (r != BT_OK) return r; double top_gap = (prev_bottom > c->margin.top) ? prev_bottom : c->margin.top; c->y = cursor + top_gap; c->x = pl + c->margin.left; cursor = c->y + c->h; prev_bottom = c->margin.bottom; } node->h = cursor + prev_bottom + pb; return BT_OK; } /* Negative inputs clamp to zero (a basis never subtracts from the packing math).", "kind": "function", "line": 56, "name": "bt_nn", "signature": "static double bt_nn(double v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 76, "name": "wrap_reverse", "signature": "*\n * wrap_reverse (node->wrap_reverse): when node->wrap is active and node->wrap_reverse\n * is no..."}, {"doc": "Grid: sized columns via flex_layout (fixed px reserved first, the rest split by fr weight; a NULL grid_track keeps the historical equal columns), placement row-major honouring per-item column spans (fx_grid_place_span: a span occupies N consecutive columns plus the gaps between them; one that does not fit in the row's remaining columns jumps to the next row), per-row max height. The column gap is always `gap`; the ROW gap is `row_gap` when has_row_gap is set (author `row-gap`, distinct from `column-gap`), else it falls back to `gap` too (a plain `gap: N` applies to both axes) -- has_row_gap defaults to 0 under zero-init, so * every caller that predates row-gap keeps its exact geometry.", "kind": "function", "line": 199, "name": "layout_grid", "signature": "static bt_status layout_grid(bt_node *node, bt_node *const *kids, size_t nk,\n                    ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 253, "name": "layout_node", "signature": "static bt_status layout_node(bt_node *node, double avail_w, unsigned depth)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 298, "name": "bt_layout", "signature": "bt_status bt_layout(bt_node *root, double avail_w)"}, {"doc": "Recursive doc_order assignment. The box tree is acyclic (each box has at most one parent_id), so the visited bitmap is just a guard against the degenerate * case of a cycle from a hostile document.", "kind": "function", "line": 339, "name": "assign_doc_order", "signature": "static void assign_doc_order(const pv_box_def *boxes, size_t nbox, size_t idx,\n                  ..."}, {"doc": "Walk the parent_id chain from `start` to find the nearest ancestor with position != STATIC. Returns the ancestor's index, or -1 if none exists (caller falls back to the viewport). Bounded by BT_MAX_DEPTH to keep a * hostile tree from looping.", "kind": "function", "line": 356, "name": "find_positioned_ancestor", "signature": "static int find_positioned_ancestor(const pv_box_def *boxes, size_t nbox,\n                       ..."}, {"doc": "Resolves an inset value: PV_LEN_UNSET or BT_LEN_AUTO → 0 (anchor at the * containing block's edge); otherwise the px value.", "kind": "function", "line": 370, "name": "resolve_inset", "signature": "static double resolve_inset(int v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 374, "name": "bt_resolve_positioning", "signature": "bt_status bt_resolve_positioning(const pv_box_def *boxes, size_t nbox,\n                          ..."}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 29, "name": "BT_LEN_AUTO"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 59, "name": "BT_WRAP_EPS"}]}, {"id": "src/browser.c", "kind": "module", "label": "browser.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "80910e0f039fdf1f", "symbol_count": 37, "symbols": [{"doc": "include <stdlib.h> include status.", "kind": "function", "line": 25, "name": "clear_status", "signature": "static void clear_status(browser_state *bs)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 29, "name": "free_history", "signature": undefined positions return 0 (the caller emits '?').", "kind": "function", "line": 86, "name": "cp1252_to_ucs", "signature": "static unsigned int cp1252_to_ucs(unsigned char c)"}, {"doc": "Encodes a "browser_status_text", "signature": "const char *browser_status_text(const browser_state *bs, uint64_t now_ms)"}, {"doc": "if (n >= BROWSER_STATUS_MAX) n = BROWSER_STATUS_MAX - 1; memcpy(bs->status_msg, msg, n); bs->status_msg[n] = '\\0'; bs->status_expiry_ms = now_ms + BROWSER_STATUS_DURATION_MS; return BROWSER_OK; } const char *browser_status_text(const browser_state *bs, uint64_t now_ms) { if (bs == NULL || bs->status_msg[0] == '\\0') return NULL; if (now_ms >= bs->status_expiry_ms) return NULL; return bs->status_msg; } /* --- exceptions (user override for weak TLS) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 412, "name": "host_equal", "signature": "static int host_equal(const char *a, const char *b)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 423, "name": "browser_is_exception", "language": "c", "sha256": "050c1ce1e31754d7", "symbol_count": 5, "symbols": [{"kind": "function", "line": 15, "name": "cx_forms_stacking_context", "signature": "int cx_forms_stacking_context(const cx_style *s)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 33, "name": "cx_box_layer", "signature": "cx_layer cx_box_layer(const cx_style *s)"}, {"doc": "Not a stacking context: a positioned box with z:auto still paints in the * positioned/zero layer (CSS 2.1 App E point 8). if (is_positioned(s->position)) return CX_LAYER_ZERO_Z; if (s->is_float) return CX_LAYER_FLOAT; if (s->is_inline) return CX_LAYER_INLINE; return CX_LAYER_BLOCK; } /* Effective z used to order within a layer: auto collapses to 0 (the ZERO_Z layer).", "kind": "function", "line": 50, "name": "eff_z", "signature": "static int eff_z(const cx_item *it)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 53, "name": "cx_item_compare", "signature": "int cx_item_compare(const cx_item *a, const cx_item *b)"}, {"doc": "Stable insertion sort: n is bounded by the caller (BT_MAX_POSITIONED), so O(n^2) is fine, and stability keeps equal-key boxes in their original relative order (document order is already the final tie-break, so this only matters on true * key collisions). No allocation.", "kind": "function", "line": 70, "name": "cx_sort", "signature": "void cx_sort(cx_item *items, size_t n)"}]}, {"id": "src/css.c", "kind": "module", "label": "css.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "28e3fa231e8b73f0", "symbol_count": 173, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 156, "name": "css_decl"}, {"doc": "One custom property (--name: value), for var() lookups. Both fields are bounded * like every other token buffer here.", "kind": "struct", "line": 164, "name": "css_custom_prop"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 172, "name": "css_sheet"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 953, "name": "calc_val"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 954, "name": "calc_parser"}, {"doc": "Parses a leading non-negative number (digits + optional fraction). Returns 1 on * success, setting *out and *endp to the first unconsumed char.", "kind": "function", "line": 209, "name": "parse_num", "signature": "static int parse_num(const char *s, double *out, const char **endp)"}, {"doc": "Rounds v to the nearest int, clamped to [lo, hi]. Clamping the double BEFORE the cast avoids undefined behaviour: casting an out-of-range double (e.g. a hostile \"99999999999px\") to int is UB. Every value interpreter that casts a parsed double * routes through here.", "kind": "function", "line": 229, "name": "round_clamp", "signature": "static int round_clamp(double v, int lo, int hi)"}, {"doc": "Rounds v to the nearest int, clamped to [lo, hi]. Clamping the double BEFORE the cast avoids undefined behaviour: casting an out-of-range double (e.g. a hostile \"99999999999px\") to int is UB. Every value interpreter that casts a parsed double * routes through here. static int round_clamp(double v, int lo, int hi) { if (v >= (double)hi) return hi; if (v <= (double)lo) return lo; return (int)(v + (v < 0.0 ? -0.5 : 0.5)); } /* --- value interpreters (return -1 / sentinel when the value is unsupported) --- /* Like cc_parse but returns packed int with sentinels for currentColor/transparent.", "kind": "function", "line": 238, "name": "parse_color", "signature": "static int parse_color(const char *v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 246, "name": "interp_color", "signature": "static int interp_color(const char *v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 250, "name": "interp_bg", "signature": "static int interp_bg(const char *v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 282, "name": "gradient", "signature": "* or fewer than 2 stops drop the gradient (and, for the `background` shorthand,\n * the whole decl..."}, {"doc": "Locates a plain linear-gradient(...) call in v (case-insensitive; an occurrence that is the tail of a longer ident, e.g. repeating-linear-gradient, does not count). Writes the call span [start,end) (end past the closing paren) and the * argument span. 1 = found, 0 = absent, -1 = found but unbalanced (malformed).", "kind": "function", "line": 330, "name": "find_linear_gradient", "signature": "static int find_linear_gradient(const char *v, size_t *start, size_t *end,\n                      ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 364, "name": "CSS_GRAD_STOPS_MAX", "signature": "* CSS_GRAD_STOPS_MAX (stops past the cap are kept out unvalidated), or 0 when the\n * gradient fai..."}, {"doc": "Emits the gradient decl group. nstops == 0 emits only the explicit reset * (P_BG_GRAD_N = 0), which is how a shorthand clears a lower-tier gradient.", "kind": "function", "line": 436, "name": "emit_gradient", "signature": "static int emit_gradient(css_decl *dst, int cap, int angle, int nstops,\n                         ..."}, {"doc": "R5c: detects radial-gradient(circle at center, color1, color2). Returns 1 * with the argument span set.", "kind": "function", "line": 493, "name": "find_radial_gradient", "signature": "static int find_radial_gradient(const char *v, size_t *start, size_t *end,\n                      ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 538, "name": "downstream", "signature": "* happens downstream (render_doc.c), gated by caps.images like an <img>. */\nstatic int expand_bg_..."}, {"doc": "background shorthand: CSS resets BOTH the color and the image layer (gradient or url), so a color emits gradient-unset+image-unset and a gradient/url emits color-unset. A present-but-broken gradient or malformed url(...) drops the whole declaration (fail closed); a value with no color, gradient nor url keeps the * historical drop path.", "kind": "function", "line": 598, "name": "expand_background", "signature": "static int expand_background(const char *val, css_decl *dst, int cap,\n                           ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 636, "name": "interp_align", "signature": "static int interp_align(const char *v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 644, "name": "interp_fontsize", "signature": "static int interp_fontsize(const char *v)"}, {"doc": "line-height as a percent of the natural line box. A unitless multiplier (\"1.5\" -> 150) or a percent (\"160%\" -> 160); \"normal\" is unset (the UA default). Absolute px/em line-heights need a font size we don't have here, so they are dropped (return -1). * Clamped to [CSS_LINE_MIN, CSS_LINE_MAX] (anti-DoS).", "kind": "function", "line": 671, "name": "interp_lineheight", "signature": "static int interp_lineheight(const char *v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 685, "name": "interp_weight", "signature": "static int interp_weight(const char *v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 694, "name": "interp_style", "signature": "static int interp_style(const char *v)"}, {"doc": "text-decoration / text-decoration-line: OR of the line keywords underline / overline / line-through found in the (space-separated) value. \"none\" -> 0 (explicit removal). Style/color/thickness tokens (wavy, red, 2px, solid, ...) are * ignored. A value carrying no line keyword at all is unsupported -> -1 (dropped).", "kind": "function", "line": 705, "name": "interp_textdeco", "signature": "static int interp_textdeco(const char *v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 724, "name": "interp_display", "signature": "static int interp_display(const char *v)"}, {"doc": "One gap length. Reuses interp_len (px / em / rem / bare 0 / calc() / math functions), so a `gap: 1em` is 16px instead of the old misparse-as-1px, and a * trailing junk token fails closed. Negative fails; clamped to CSS_GAP_MAX.", "kind": "function", "line": 743, "name": "interp_gap", "signature": "static int interp_gap(const char *v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 749, "name": "interp_justify", "signature": "static int interp_justify(const char *v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 775, "name": "count_tracks", "signature": "static int count_tracks(const char *s, size_t n)"}, {"doc": "Size of ONE track token: `<N>fr` -> -(N*100); a px/em/rem length -> px (> 0); minmax(a,b) -> the size of its max component b; auto/%/unknown -> 0 (an equal * `auto` share downstream, never a wrong guess).", "kind": "function", "line": 784, "name": "track_size_of", "signature": "static int track_size_of(const char *tok)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 813, "name": "count_one_repeat", "signature": "static int count_one_repeat(const char *s, size_t tokstart, size_t toklen,\n                      ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 856, "name": "walk_tracks", "signature": "static int walk_tracks(const char *s, size_t n, int *sizes, int szcap, int *pos)"}, {"doc": "grid-template-columns: track count PLUS the first CSS_GRID_TRACKS_MAX track sizes, emitted in lock-step (P_GRIDCOLS + P_GRID_TRACK0..7; unsized slots emit 0 = auto so a higher-tier declaration fully resets a lower-tier one). * none/url()/malformed drop the declaration, exactly like interp_gridcols.", "kind": "function", "line": 917, "name": "expand_grid_template_cols", "signature": "static int expand_grid_template_cols(const char *val, css_decl *dst, int cap)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 955, "name": "calc_skip_ws", "signature": "static void calc_skip_ws(calc_parser *p)"}, {"doc": "Max arguments of one min()/max() call (clamp() takes exactly three). More fail * the declaration (anti-DoS; the whole value already fits one CSS_TOK_MAX token). #define CSS_MATHFN_MAX_ARGS 8 typedef struct calc_val { double px; int is_length; } calc_val; typedef struct calc_parser { const char *s; size_t n, i; } calc_parser; static void calc_skip_ws(calc_parser *p) { while (p->i < p->n && (p->s[p->i] == ' ' || p->s[p->i] == '\\t')) ++p->i; } static int calc_expr(calc_parser *p, calc_val *out, int depth); /* Consumes \"name(\" (case-insensitive) at the cursor; 0 leaves the cursor put.", "kind": "function", "line": 963, "name": "calc_match_fn", "signature": "static int calc_match_fn(calc_parser *p, const char *name)"}, {"doc": "min()/max()/clamp() (2026-07-10): comma-separated full expressions, every argument the same shape (all lengths or all bare numbers, like +/-). clamp(lo, mid, hi) is max(lo, min(mid, hi)) per CSS and takes exactly three arguments; min/max take 1..CSS_MATHFN_MAX_ARGS. Depth-bounded with the parens. kind: 0 * min, 1 max, 2 clamp.", "kind": "function", "line": 978, "name": "calc_mathfn", "signature": "static int calc_mathfn(calc_parser *p, calc_val *out, int depth, int kind)"}, {"doc": "} if (p->i + 3 <= p->n && csel_lower_ch(p->s[p->i]) == 'r' && csel_lower_ch(p->s[p->i + 1]) == 'e' && csel_lower_ch(p->s[p->i + 2]) == 'm') { out->px = num * 16.0; out->is_length = 1; p->i += 3; return 1; } if (p->i + 2 <= p->n && csel_lower_ch(p->s[p->i]) == 'e' && csel_lower_ch(p->s[p->i + 1]) == 'm') { out->px = num * 16.0; out->is_length = 1; p->i += 2; return 1; } out->px = num;                      /* a bare number: length only if exactly 0 out->is_length = (num == 0.0); return 1; } /* '*' and '/' bind tighter than '+'/'-'.", "kind": "function", "line": 1062, "name": "calc_term", "signature": "static int calc_term(calc_parser *p, calc_val *out, int depth)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1081, "name": "calc_expr", "signature": "static int calc_expr(calc_parser *p, calc_val *out, int depth)"}, {"doc": "Evaluates the inside of a calc(...) (v[0,vlen), the \"calc(\" prefix and matching \")\" already stripped by the caller). Fails closed on any leftover/unparsed input, * mismatched parens, a dimensionless result, or a dimensional error.", "kind": "function", "line": 1099, "name": "calc_eval", "signature": "static int calc_eval(const char *v, size_t vlen, double *out_px)"}, {"doc": "True if s (already trimmed) is a \"calc(...)\" call spanning the whole string (case-insensitive keyword, balanced trailing paren); on success the argument * span is written to *inner_start / *inner_len.", "kind": "function", "line": 1112, "name": "calc_unwrap", "signature": "static int calc_unwrap(const char *s, size_t *inner_start, size_t *inner_len)"}, {"doc": "Parses one box-model length. Accepts \"Npx\", a bare \"0\", \"Nem\"/\"Nrem\" (x16 px, the engine's base font), \"calc(...)\" over the same units (+, -, *, /, parens; see calc_eval), and (when allow_auto) \"auto\". Rejects %/viewport units and bare non-zero numbers outside calc() (fail closed: they need a containing block the parser does not have). Returns 1 with *out = CSS_LEN_AUTO or a signed px clamped * to [-CSS_LEN_MAX, CSS_LEN_MAX]; 0 if unsupported.", "kind": "function", "line": 1129, "name": "interp_len", "signature": "static int interp_len(const char *v, int allow_auto, int *out)"}, {"doc": "Emits one box length declaration for slot into dst (cap permitting). A negative value is rejected unless allow_neg (margins allow it; padding/width do not). * Returns 1 on success, 0 if the value is unsupported or does not fit.", "kind": "function", "line": 1187, "name": "emit_len", "signature": "static int emit_len(css_decl *dst, int cap, int slot, const char *val,\n                    int al..."}, {"doc": "Emits a symbolic percentage width for slot (Hito 32): \"<num>%\" with num > 0, carried as per-mille (99.8% -> 998) and saturating at 1000% (10000). The parser has no containing block, so the value stays symbolic until layout resolves it * (bx_width_cap). Junk or a non-positive number fails closed (returns 0).", "kind": "function", "line": 1201, "name": "emit_pct", "signature": "static int emit_pct(css_decl *dst, int cap, int slot, const char *val)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1226, "name": "interp_len", "signature": "* this file that might hand a token to interp_len (transitively: margin/padding/\n * inset, flex-b..."}, {"doc": "Expands a two-slot logical shorthand (margin-inline / padding-block / inset-inline: one value sets both sides, two set start then end; 2026-07-10). * Fail closed on zero, more than two, or any uninterpretable token.", "kind": "function", "line": 1282, "name": "expand_box2", "signature": "static int expand_box2(const char *val, int slot_start, int slot_end,\n                       int ..."}, {"doc": "Maps one font-family name (a generic keyword or a common family) to a generic css_font_family bucket; -1 if unrecognised. Case-insensitive; multi-word names * (e.g. \"times new roman\") are compared whole.", "kind": "function", "line": 1305, "name": "family_of", "signature": "static int family_of(const char *name)"}, {"doc": "font-family: the first recognised name in the comma-separated stack wins (its * generic bucket). Quotes are stripped. url() defensively dropped. -1 if none known.", "kind": "function", "line": 1334, "name": "interp_fontfamily", "signature": "static int interp_fontfamily(const char *v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1355, "name": "interp_texttransform", "signature": "static int interp_texttransform(const char *v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1377, "name": "interp_valign", "signature": "static int interp_valign(const char *v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1384, "name": "interp_whitespace", "signature": "static int interp_whitespace(const char *v)"}, {"doc": "break-spaces preserves whitespace and wraps; this engine only models the * wrap/keep distinction, so it collapses to pre-wrap (2026-07-10). if (csel_ci_eq(v, \"break-spaces\")) return CSS_WS_PRE_WRAP; return -1; } /* tab-size: a non-negative integer (number of spaces). -1 if unsupported.", "kind": "function", "line": 1398, "name": "interp_tabsize", "signature": "static int interp_tabsize(const char *v)"}, {"doc": "} /* tab-size: a non-negative integer (number of spaces). -1 if unsupported. static int interp_tabsize(const char *v) { double num; const char *end; if (!parse_num(v, &num, &end)) return -1; while (*end == ' ' || *end == '\\t') ++end; if (*end != '\\0') return -1;  /* units/lengths dropped; only bare number int n = round_clamp(num, 0, 64); return (n > 0) ? n : -1;  /* 0 or unparseable -> unset } /* text-decoration-style: solid/wavy/dotted/dashed/double. -1 if unknown.", "kind": "function", "line": 1409, "name": "interp_textdeco_style", "signature": "static int interp_textdeco_style(const char *v)"}, {"doc": "text-decoration-thickness: `from-font` (keyword -> 0), or a non-negative length * (px -> px, em/rem x16). -1 if unsupported (negative, %, etc -> dropped).", "kind": "function", "line": 1420, "name": "interp_textdeco_thickness", "signature": "static int interp_textdeco_thickness(const char *v)"}, {"doc": "aspect-ratio: `auto`, a `<ratio>` such as `16/9` or `1.5`, or `auto <ratio>` (auto fallback). Stores both numerator and denominator x1000 (for sub-integer ratios like 1.5 -> 1500/1000). Returns 1 with *num and *den set, 0 if unsupported. Not normalized mod 360 (a static rotation of e.g. 720deg is visually * identical to 0deg once fed through cos/sin at paint time).", "kind": "function", "line": 2599, "name": "parse_rotate_deg", "signature": "static int parse_rotate_deg(const char *s, int *out)"}, {"doc": "transform function (skew/matrix/perspective/3D), multiple space-separated functions, or unparseable syntax reject the WHOLE declaration (no decl emitted -> cascades as unset, byte-identical to a page that never declared transform at all -- fail closed, never a half-applied transform). skew()/ matrix() are architecturally deferred: they need an arbitrary (non-pivotable or fully general) Cairo matrix, out of scope for this increment (see spec/compositor.md \"fuera de alcance\"). Transformed hit-testing (click, cursor, overflow-clip ancestor resolution) also stays out of scope -- the painter (gui/browser_ui.c box_transform_matrix) applies the real affine transform, but hit-testing still resolves against the UNTRANSFORMED layout rect, same documented limit as M1.2 translate. transform-origin is not parsed; the pivot is always the box's own center. \"none\" is not special-cased -- it simply fails every function-name match below and emits * nothing, same net effect (unset).", "kind": "function", "line": 2628, "name": "expand_transform", "signature": "static int expand_transform(const char *val, css_decl *dst, int cap)"}, {"doc": "gap / grid-gap (2026-07-10): one value keeps the pre-existing semantics (both axes; row-gap stays unset and falls back to gap downstream), two values are `<row> <col>` (row feeds row-gap, col feeds gap). column-gap stays a * single-value longhand in the dispatch below.", "kind": "function", "line": 2729, "name": "expand_gap", "signature": "static int expand_gap(const char *val, css_decl *dst, int cap)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 2748, "name": "ignored", "signature": "* engine slot and is ignored (documented simplification, like list-style's\n * ignored tokens). An empty segment matches (all).", "kind": "function", "line": 3336, "name": "media_segment_matches", "signature": "static int media_segment_matches(const char *s, size_t a, size_t b, const css_media *m)"}, {"doc": "if (strcmp(buf, \"and\") == 0 || strcmp(buf, \"only\") == 0) { /* connector / legacy keyword: ignore } else if (strcmp(buf, \"not\") == 0 || strcmp(buf, \"or\") == 0) { return 0;  /* negation / level-4 or: fail closed } else { if (!media_part_matches(buf, m)) result = 0; any = 1; } } } return any ? result : 1; } /* A media query list s[a,b): comma-separated segments OR'd together.", "kind": "function", "line": 3373, "name": "media_matches", "signature": "static int media_matches(const char *s, size_t a, size_t b, const css_media *m)"}, {"doc": "static int media_matches(const char *s, size_t a, size_t b, const css_media *m) { while (a < b && (s[a] == ' ' || s[a] == '\\t' || s[a] == '\\n' || s[a] == '\\r')) ++a; if (a >= b) return 1;  /* empty query == all size_t i = a; while (i < b) { size_t seg = i; while (i < b && s[i] != ',') ++i; if (media_segment_matches(s, seg, i, m)) return 1; if (i < b) ++i; } return 0; } /* True when s[i] ('@') begins an \"@media\" at-rule.", "kind": "function", "line": 3387, "name": "at_is_media", "signature": "static int at_is_media(const char *s, size_t i, size_t n)"}, {"doc": "Parses rules in s[start,end). so the css_element pointers stay valid for the chain's lifetime.", "kind": "struct", "line": 23, "name": "cch_node"}, {"doc": "Fills *node with element e's tag/id/class tokens (no style=, no parent link yet). * Over-long tokens are simply absent, which fails closed (does not match).", "kind": "function", "line": 35, "name": "fill_css_node", "signature": "static void fill_css_node(lxb_dom_element_t *e, cch_node *node)"}, {"doc": "Computes the 1-based index of n among its ELEMENT siblings and the total element-sibling count (both walks bounded, anti-DoS: past CCH_NTH_MAX the * position reads unknown and :nth-child()-style selectors do not match).", "kind": "function", "line": 120, "name": "sibling_position", "signature": "static void sibling_position(lxb_dom_node_t *n, int *nth, int *nsib)"}, {"doc": "Like sibling_position but only counts siblings with the SAME local name as n. * R2: for :nth-of-type() / :first-of-type / etc.", "kind": "function", "line": 138, "name": "sibling_type_position", "signature": "static void sibling_type_position(lxb_dom_node_t *n, int *nth, int *nsib)"}, {"doc": "Counts ALL child nodes (elements, text, comments) of the DOM element n. * R2: for :empty matching.", "kind": "function", "line": 166, "name": "count_children", "signature": "static int count_children(lxb_dom_node_t *n)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 180, "name": "inputs", "signature": "* identical inputs (single source of truth). */\nstatic const css_element *build_chain(lxb_dom_ele..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 241, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 24, "name": "cc_named"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 108, "name": "ascii_lower", "signature": "static int ascii_lower(int c)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 112, "name": "hex_val", "signature": "static int hex_val(int c)"}, {"doc": "Trims surrounding ASCII spaces and lowercases into out (CC_TOKEN_MAX bytes). * Returns 0, or -1 if the trimmed token is empty or does not fit.", "kind": "function", "line": 122, "name": "normalize", "signature": "static int normalize(const char *token, char *out)"}, {"doc": "static int normalize(const char *token, char *out) { size_t start = 0; while (token[start] == ' ' || token[start] == '\\t') ++start; size_t end = strlen(token); while (end > start && (token[end - 1] == ' ' || token[end - 1] == '\\t')) --end; size_t len = end - start; if (len == 0 || len >= CC_TOKEN_MAX) return -1; for (size_t i = 0; i < len; ++i) out[i] = (char)ascii_lower((unsigned char)token[start + i]); out[len] = '\\0'; return 0; } /* Parses the hex body (after '#'), lowercased. Returns 0 / -1.", "kind": "function", "line": 136, "name": "parse_hex", "signature": "static int parse_hex(const char *s, cc_rgb *out)"}, {"doc": "Parses one rgb() component in [b, e). For a color channel, the value is an integer 0..255 or a percentage 0%..100% (rounded). For the alpha channel * (is_alpha), the value is validated as numeric and discarded. Returns 0 / -1.", "kind": "function", "line": 162, "name": "parse_component", "signature": "static int parse_component(const char *b, const char *e, int is_alpha, int *out)"}, {"doc": "Parses one hsl() component: H is integer 0..360, S/L are 0%..100%. * Returns 0 / -1.", "kind": "function", "line": 197, "name": "parse_hsl_comp", "signature": "static int parse_hsl_comp(const char *b, const char *e, int is_hue, int *out)"}, {"doc": "Parses the functional rgb()/rgba()/hsl()/hsla() form (lowercased token). * Returns 0 / -1.", "kind": "function", "line": 271, "name": "parse_func", "signature": "static int parse_func(const char *s, cc_rgb *out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 340, "name": "named_cmp", "signature": "static int named_cmp(const void *key, const void *element)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 346, "name": "parse_named", "signature": "static int parse_named(const char *s, cc_rgb *out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 356, "name": "cc_parse", "signature": "cc_status cc_parse(const char *token, cc_rgb *out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 387, "name": "cc_pack", "signature": "int cc_pack(cc_rgb c)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 391, "name": "cc_unpack", "signature": "cc_rgb cc_unpack(int packed)"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 18, "name": "CC_TOKEN_MAX"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 21, "name": "CC_CHANNEL_MAX"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 22, "name": "CC_PERCENT_MAX"}]}, {"id": "src/css_select.c", "kind": "module", "label": "css_select.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "8a5db83284446172", "symbol_count": 16, "symbols": [{"doc": "Parses one attribute selector starting at s[*ip] == '[' (within s[.,b)) into *am. Advances *ip past the closing ']'. Returns 1 if supported, 0 (fail closed) on any malformation (no name, unknown operator, unterminated). Grammar: '[' ws name ws ( ']' | op '=' ws value ws (i|s)? ws ']' ) * value may be quoted (single/double; quotes stripped, may contain whitespace).", "kind": "function", "line": 19, "name": "parse_attr_sel", "signature": "static int parse_attr_sel(const char *s, size_t *ip, size_t b, css_attr_match *am)"}, {"doc": "Parses the An+B argument of the nth-child family, s[a,b) (surrounding space trimmed here). Accepts odd/even, N, An, An+B, An-B, n, -n+B, +n... (spaces around the +/- of the B part allowed, case-insensitive n). |A| and |B| are * bounded by CSS_NTH_MAX; anything else fails (drops the selector).", "kind": "function", "line": 76, "name": "parse_nth_arg", "signature": "static int parse_nth_arg(const char *s, size_t a, size_t b, int *A, int *B)"}, {"doc": "Parses one SIMPLE sub-selector span s[a,b) for :not()/:is()/:where(): only tag name, .class, #id, or [attr] (no pseudo-classes, no combinators). The caller MUST trim leading/trailing space. Returns 1 if any component was * parsed; 0 means the span is empty or junk (fail closed for that alternative).", "kind": "function", "line": 269, "name": "parse_sub_compound", "signature": "static int parse_sub_compound(const char *s, size_t a, size_t b, css_sub_sel *sub)"}, {"doc": "Parses one COMPOUND selector span s[a,b) (no combinators, no surrounding space) into *cp. sel holds the sub-selector storage for :not()/:is()/:where() * pseudo-classes being parsed. Returns 1 if supported.", "kind": "function", "line": 320, "name": "parse_compound", "signature": "static int parse_compound(const char *s, size_t a, size_t b, css_compound *cp,\n                  ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 379, "name": "selector", "signature": "* the whole selector (fail closed). A chain deeper than CSS_MAX_COMPOUNDS is\n * dropped. Whitespa..."}, {"doc": "The value of element attribute `name` (case-insensitive name), or NULL if absent. * A present attribute with no value reads as \"\".", "kind": "function", "line": 473, "name": "el_attr_value", "signature": "static const char *el_attr_value(const css_element *el, const char *name)"}, {"doc": "The value of element attribute `name` (case-insensitive name), or NULL if absent. * A present attribute with no value reads as \"\". static const char *el_attr_value(const css_element *el, const char *name) { if (el->attrs == NULL) return NULL; for (size_t i = 0; i < el->nattrs; ++i) { if (el->attrs[i].name != NULL && csel_ci_eq(el->attrs[i].name, name)) return el->attrs[i].value != NULL ? el->attrs[i].value : \"\"; } return NULL; } /* True if `v` ends with `suf` (non-empty), case-folded when ci.", "kind": "function", "line": 483, "name": "ends_with", "signature": "static int ends_with(const char *v, const char *suf, int ci)"}, {"doc": "True if `v` is a whitespace-separated list containing the word `w` (non-empty), * case-folded when ci (the `~=` operator).", "kind": "function", "line": 491, "name": "has_word", "signature": "static int has_word(const char *v, const char *w, int ci)"}, {"doc": "size_t wl = strlen(w); if (wl == 0) return 0; const char *p = v; while (*p != '\\0') { while (*p == ' ' || *p == '\\t' || *p == '\\n' || *p == '\\r' || *p == '\\f') ++p; const char *st = p; while (*p != '\\0' && *p != ' ' && *p != '\\t' && *p != '\\n' && p != '\\r' && *p != '\\f') ++p; if ((size_t)(p - st) == wl && csel_span_eq(st, w, wl, ci)) return 1; } return 0; } /* True if attribute selector `am` matches element `el`.", "kind": "function", "line": 506, "name": "attr_matches", "signature": "static int attr_matches(const css_attr_match *am, const css_element *el)"}, {"doc": "True if the 1-based index idx satisfies idx = A*m + B for some integer m >= 0. * idx <= 0 means \"unknown sibling position\" and never matches (fail closed).", "kind": "function", "line": 526, "name": "nth_matches", "signature": "static int nth_matches(int A, int B, int idx)"}, {"doc": "True if the 1-based index idx satisfies idx = A*m + B for some integer m >= 0. * idx <= 0 means \"unknown sibling position\" and never matches (fail closed). static int nth_matches(int A, int B, int idx) { if (idx <= 0) return 0; if (A == 0) return idx == B; long d = (long)idx - B; if (A > 0) return d >= 0 && d % A == 0; return d <= 0 && (-d) % (long)(-A) == 0; } /* Form controls for :enabled (per HTML: elements that can actually be disabled).", "kind": "function", "line": 535, "name": "is_form_control", "signature": "static int is_form_control(const char *tag)"}, {"doc": "if (A > 0) return d >= 0 && d % A == 0; return d <= 0 && (-d) % (long)(-A) == 0; } /* Form controls for :enabled (per HTML: elements that can actually be disabled). static int is_form_control(const char *tag) { if (tag == NULL) return 0; return csel_ci_eq(tag, \"input\") || csel_ci_eq(tag, \"button\") || csel_ci_eq(tag, \"select\") || csel_ci_eq(tag, \"textarea\") || csel_ci_eq(tag, \"option\") || csel_ci_eq(tag, \"optgroup\") || csel_ci_eq(tag, \"fieldset\"); } /* True if a css_sub_sel (tag/class/id/[attr]) matches element el.", "kind": "function", "line": 544, "name": "sub_sel_matches", "signature": "static int sub_sel_matches(const css_sub_sel *sub, const css_element *el)"}, {"doc": "ch != NULL && sp < 128; ch = ch->next) { stack[sp] = ch; depth[sp] = cd + 1; ++sp; } } } return 0;  /* no matching descendant found } case PSEUDO_BEFORE: case PSEUDO_AFTER:        return allow_pseudo_el;  /* R8: match only in CSS cascade default:                  return 0; } } /* True if one compound matches one element (no ancestor context).", "kind": "function", "line": 779, "name": "compound_matches", "signature": "static int compound_matches(const css_compound *c, const css_element *el,\n                       ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 804, "name": "sibling", "signature": "* tries each preceding sibling (the recursion backtracks). Bounded by k\n * (<= CSS_MAX_COMPOUNDS)..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 826, "name": "csel_matches", "signature": "int csel_matches(const css_sel *sel, const css_element *el, const char *target_id,\n              ..."}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 640, "name": "HAS_MAX_DEPTH"}]}, {"id": "src/data_url.c", "kind": "module", "label": "data_url.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "37bf1d69391c4e81", "symbol_count": 6, "symbols": [{"doc": "include <stdlib.h> include <string.h>", "kind": "function", "line": 15, "name": "lower", "signature": "static int lower(char c)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 19, "name": "ci_starts_with", "signature": "static int ci_starts_with(const char *s, const char *prefix)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 27, "name": "du_is_data_url", "signature": "int du_is_data_url(const char *url)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 31, "name": "du_base64_payload", "signature": "du_status du_base64_payload(const char *url, const char **payload, size_t *payload_len)"}, {"doc": "0-63 for a base64 alphabet character, -1 otherwise. case LS_ERR_KDF: case LS_ERR_CRYPTO: default:               return DS_ERR_CRYPTO; } } /* --- public ---", "kind": "function", "line": 65, "name": "ds_write", "signature": "ds_status ds_write(const char *path, const uint8_t key[LS_KEY_LEN], ls_aead aead,\n               ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 102, "name": "ds_read", "signature": "ds_status ds_read(const char *path, const uint8_t key[LS_KEY_LEN],\n                  uint8_t **ou..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 135, "name": "ds_free", "signature": "void ds_free(uint8_t *buf, size_t len)"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 10, "name": "_POSIX_C_SOURCE"}]}, {"id": "src/dom.c", "kind": "module", "label": "dom.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "6daf10e127768863", "symbol_count": 63, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 55, "name": "sm_entry"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 64, "name": "strmap"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 154, "name": "pm_entry"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 160, "name": "ptrmap"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 220, "name": "dom_index"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 803, "name": "ih_block"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 808, "name": "ih_acc"}, {"doc": "#include <stdint.h> #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> #include <lexbor/dom/dom.h> #include <lexbor/html/html.h> /* --- small character helpers (no locale dependence) --- static int is_ws(int c) { return c == ' ' || c == '\\t' || c == '\\n' || c == '\\f' || c == '\\r'; } /* Lowercase s[0..n) into out (NUL-terminated). Fails if it would not fit.", "kind": "function", "line": 33, "name": "to_lower_buf", "signature": "static int to_lower_buf(const char *s, size_t n, char *out, size_t outcap)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 43, "name": "ptr_hash", "signature": "static size_t ptr_hash(const void *p)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 69, "name": "sm_entry_append", "signature": "static int sm_entry_append(sm_entry *e, dom_node_id id)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 81, "name": "sm_grow", "signature": "static int sm_grow(strmap *m)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 98, "name": "sm_put", "signature": "static int sm_put(strmap *m, const char *key, size_t klen, dom_node_id id)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 126, "name": "sm_find", "signature": "static const sm_entry *sm_find(const strmap *m, const char *key, size_t klen)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 138, "name": "sm_free", "signature": "static void sm_free(strmap *m)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 165, "name": "pm_grow", "signature": "static int pm_grow(ptrmap *m)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 182, "name": "pm_put", "signature": "static int pm_put(ptrmap *m, const void *key, dom_node_id id)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 199, "name": "pm_get", "signature": "static int pm_get(const ptrmap *m, const void *key, dom_node_id *out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 210, "name": "pm_free", "signature": "static void pm_free(ptrmap *m)"}, {"doc": "/* --- the index --- struct dom_index { lxb_dom_node_t      **nodes;    /* arena: id -> element node, in document order size_t                count; size_t                cap;      /* capacity of nodes[] (grows when JS creates nodes) lxb_dom_document_t   *document; /* owning document (for title / text-node creation) strmap                by_id; strmap                by_tag; strmap                by_class; ptrmap                rev;      /* node pointer -> id }; /* Iterative pre-order successor within the subtree rooted at root.", "kind": "function", "line": 232, "name": "node_next", "signature": "static lxb_dom_node_t *node_next(lxb_dom_node_t *node, const lxb_dom_node_t *root)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 241, "name": "valid", "signature": "static int valid(const dom_index *idx, dom_node_id n)"}, {"doc": "if (node->first_child != NULL) return node->first_child; while (node != root && node->next == NULL) { if (node->parent == NULL) return NULL; node = node->parent; } if (node == root) return NULL; return node->next; } static int valid(const dom_index *idx, dom_node_id n) { return idx != NULL && n < idx->count; } /* Index a single element's id, class tokens and tag name.", "kind": "function", "line": 247, "name": "index_element", "signature": "static int index_element(dom_index *idx, lxb_dom_element_t *el, dom_node_id id)"}, {"doc": "} size_t tlen = 0; const lxb_char_t *tag = lxb_dom_element_qualified_name(el, &tlen); if (tag != NULL && tlen > 0) { char lower[256]; if (to_lower_buf((const char *)tag, tlen, lower, sizeof lower) == 0) { if (sm_put(&idx->by_tag, lower, tlen, id) != 0) return -1; } } return 0; } /* --- public: build / free ---", "kind": "function", "line": 283, "name": "dom_build", "signature": "dom_status dom_build(const hp_document *doc, dom_index **out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 324, "name": "dom_free", "signature": "void dom_free(dom_index *idx)"}, {"doc": "return DOM_ERR_OOM; } void dom_free(dom_index *idx) { if (idx == NULL) return; free(idx->nodes); sm_free(&idx->by_id); sm_free(&idx->by_tag); sm_free(&idx->by_class); pm_free(&idx->rev); free(idx); } /* --- public: queries ---", "kind": "function", "line": 336, "name": "dom_node_count", "signature": "size_t dom_node_count(const dom_index *idx)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 340, "name": "dom_get_element_by_id", "signature": "dom_node_id dom_get_element_by_id(const dom_index *idx, const char *id)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 346, "name": "copy_ids", "signature": "static size_t copy_ids(const sm_entry *e, dom_node_id *out, size_t cap)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 353, "name": "dom_get_by_tag", "signature": "size_t dom_get_by_tag(const dom_index *idx, const char *tag,\n                      dom_node_id *o..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 362, "name": "dom_get_by_class", "signature": "size_t dom_get_by_class(const dom_index *idx, const char *cls,\n                        dom_node_i..."}, {"doc": "Max complex selectors in one comma-separated list. A longer list is truncated * (anti-DoS): the extra selectors are dropped, which only narrows matches. #define DOM_QS_MAX_SELECTORS 16 /* Reverse lookup: node pointer -> handle, or DOM_NODE_NONE if unindexed.", "kind": "function", "line": 376, "name": "id_of", "signature": "static dom_node_id id_of(const dom_index *idx, const lxb_dom_node_t *node)"}, {"doc": "Parses a selector list \"a, b, c\" into up to cap css_sel. Splits only on TOP-LEVEL commas (respecting [], () and quotes so `[a=\"x,y\"]` and `:not(a,b)` do not split); each complex selector goes through csel_parse and an unsupported/malformed one is dropped (fail closed) while the rest survive. * Returns the number parsed.", "kind": "function", "line": 386, "name": "parse_selector_list", "signature": "static size_t parse_selector_list(const char *sel, css_sel *out, size_t cap)"}, {"doc": "s.order = (int)n; s.rule = 0; out[n++] = s; } } start = i + 1; } break; default: break; } } return n; } /* Nonzero iff element node `cn` matches any selector in the parsed list.", "kind": "function", "line": 419, "name": "node_matches_any", "signature": "static int node_matches_any(const lxb_dom_node_t *cn,\n                            const css_sel *..."},
