Keeps `need` exact even on the impossible * overflow of the local buffer.", "kind": "function", "line": 48, "name": "dd_printf", "signature": "static void dd_printf(dd_cursor *c, const char *fmt, ...)"}, {"doc": "A border/outline width or radius that is unset (PV_LEN_UNSET) or negative reads as * 0 (\"no border\"); otherwise the value.", "kind": "function", "line": 80, "name": "dd_w", "signature": "static int dd_w(int v)"}, {"doc": "A border/outline width or radius that is unset (PV_LEN_UNSET) or negative reads as * 0 (\"no border\"); otherwise the value. static int dd_w(int v) { return (v == PV_LEN_UNSET || v < 0) ? 0 : v; } /* \"#rrggbb\" for a packed colour, \"-\" when unset (-1).", "kind": "function", "line": 83, "name": "dd_color", "signature": "static void dd_color(dd_cursor *c, int rgb)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 87, "name": "dd_display_name", "signature": "static const char *dd_display_name(int d)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 95, "name": "dd_justify_name", "signature": "static const char *dd_justify_name(int j)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 107, "name": "dd_align_name", "signature": "static const char *dd_align_name(int a)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 117, "name": "dd_position_name", "signature": "static const char *dd_position_name(int p)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 128, "name": "dd_visibility_name", "signature": "static const char *dd_visibility_name(int v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 136, "name": "dd_mix_blend_name", "signature": "static const char *dd_mix_blend_name(int m)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 155, "name": "dd_overflow_name", "signature": "static const char *dd_overflow_name(int o)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 164, "name": "dd_cursor_name", "signature": "static const char *dd_cursor_name(int c)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 181, "name": "dd_text_overflow_name", "signature": "static const char *dd_text_overflow_name(int t)"}, {"doc": "case CSS_CUR_WAIT:        return \"wait\"; case CSS_CUR_CROSSHAIR:   return \"crosshair\"; case CSS_CUR_GRAB:        return \"grab\"; case CSS_CUR_ZOOM_IN:     return \"zoom-in\"; case CSS_CUR_NONE:        return \"none\"; default:                   return \"auto\"; } } static const char *dd_text_overflow_name(int t) { return (t == CSS_TO_ELLIPSIS) ? \"ellipsis\" : \"clip\"; } /* A px inset value for the dump: 0 for unset/auto (so the line stays compact).", "kind": "function", "line": 187, "name": "dd_inset", "signature": "static int dd_inset(int v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 190, "name": "dd_object_fit_name", "signature": "static const char *dd_object_fit_name(int o)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 201, "name": "dd_image_rendering_name", "signature": "static const char *dd_image_rendering_name(int r)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 210, "name": "dd_border_style_name", "signature": "static const char *dd_border_style_name(int s)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 243, "name": "dd_box_line", "signature": "static void dd_box_line(dd_cursor *c, size_t id, const pv_box_def *b)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 296, "name": "dd_block_line", "signature": "static void dd_block_line(dd_cursor *c, size_t i, const rd_block *b)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 358, "name": "dd_format", "signature": "size_t dd_format(const rd_doc *doc, char *out, size_t cap)"}, {"doc": "} dd_puts(&c, \"[blocks]\\n\"); for (size_t i = 0; i < nblocks; ++i) { const rd_block *b = rd_at(doc, i); if (b != NULL) dd_block_line(&c, i, b); } /* Always NUL-terminate when there is room for it. if (out != NULL && cap != 0) out[(c.pos < cap) ? c.pos : cap - 1] = '\\0'; return c.need; } /* CSS inspector: dumps every element with its full CSS property set.", "kind": "function", "line": 391, "name": "dd_format_css", "signature": "size_t dd_format_css(const rd_doc *doc, char *out, size_t cap)"}]}, {"id": "src/download.c", "kind": "module", "label": "download.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "3fc7f04a92806eb8", "symbol_count": 12, "symbols": [{"doc": "download — pure helpers for \"save this resource to disk\". See include/download.h and spec/download.md.  #include \"download.h\" #include \"pdf_export.h\"   /* pe_safe_basename: the single audited sanitizer #include <ctype.h> #include <string.h> /* ASCII lowercase, locale-independent (the C locale's tolower for bytes < 0x80).", "kind": "function", "line": 13, "name": "lc", "signature": "static int lc(int c)"}, {"doc": "#include \"download.h\" #include \"pdf_export.h\"   /* pe_safe_basename: the single audited sanitizer #include <ctype.h> #include <string.h> /* ASCII lowercase, locale-independent (the C locale's tolower for bytes < 0x80). static int lc(int c) { if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z') return c - 'A' + 'a'; return c; } /* Case-insensitive substring search (ASCII). Returns a pointer into hay or NULL.", "kind": "function", "line": 19, "name": "ci_find", "signature": "static const char *ci_find(const char *hay, const char *needle)"}, {"doc": "Writes the lowercased media type of content_type (the part before ';', with * surrounding spaces trimmed) into buf. Empty/NULL -> empty buf.", "kind": "function", "line": 33, "name": "media_type", "signature": "static void media_type(const char *content_type, char *buf, size_t bufsz)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 46, "name": "dl_should_download", "signature": "int dl_should_download(const char *content_type, const char *content_disposition)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 57, "name": "dl_ext_for_type", "signature": "const char *dl_ext_for_type(const char *content_type)"}, {"doc": "{ \"application/xhtml+xml\",  \".html\" }, { \"text/plain\",            \".txt\"  }, { \"image/png\",             \".png\"  }, { \"image/jpeg\",            \".jpg\"  }, { \"image/gif\",             \".gif\"  }, { \"image/svg+xml\",         \".svg\"  }, }; for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof MAP / sizeof MAP[0]; ++i) { if (strcmp(mt, MAP[i].type) == 0) return MAP[i].ext; } return \"\"; } /* Copies [src, end) into buf, bounded and NUL-terminated.", "kind": "function", "line": 85, "name": "copy_span", "signature": "static void copy_span(const char *src, const char *end, char *buf, size_t bufsz)"}, {"doc": "Extracts a filename candidate from a Content-Disposition value into buf. Handles filename=\"...\", filename=..., and RFC 5987 filename*=charset''value (the part after the second quote; not percent-decoded). Returns 1 if a * non-empty candidate was written.", "kind": "function", "line": 96, "name": "extract_disposition_name", "signature": "static int extract_disposition_name(const char *cd, char *buf, size_t bufsz)"}, {"doc": "Extracts the last path segment of url (without ?query or #fragment) into buf. * Returns 1 if a non-empty candidate was written.", "kind": "function", "line": 134, "name": "extract_url_name", "signature": "static int extract_url_name(const char *url, char *buf, size_t bufsz)"}, {"doc": "Does name already carry an extension (a '.' past the first byte with at least * one character after it)?", "kind": "function", "line": 148, "name": "has_extension", "signature": "static int has_extension(const char *name)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 152, "name": "dl_pick_name", "signature": "dl_status dl_pick_name(const char *url, const char *content_disposition,\n                       c..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 194, "name": "dl_build_path", "signature": "dl_status dl_build_path(const char *dir, const char *name, char *out, size_t outsz)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 212, "name": "dl_check_size", "signature": "dl_status dl_check_size(size_t len)"}]}, {"id": "src/flex_layout.c", "kind": "module", "label": "flex_layout.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "205c9a489597a53a", "symbol_count": 11, "symbols": [{"doc": "No I/O, no global state, no dynamic allocation: fixed-size stack scratch buffers bounded by FX_MAX_ITEMS (no VLAs). The flex algorithm follows the CSS single-line model: grow distributes positive free space by grow factor; shrink distributes negative free space by (shrink * basis), freezing any item that hits its minimum and redistributing among the rest; justify-content then places leftover space.  #include \"flex_layout.h\" #include <stddef.h> #define FX_EPS 1e-9 /* Negative inputs are clamped to zero (CSS treats them as invalid).", "kind": "function", "line": 18, "name": "nn", "signature": "static double nn(double v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 21, "name": "fx_flex_line", "signature": "fx_status fx_flex_line(const fx_item *items, size_t n, double avail, double gap,\n                ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 126, "name": "fx_grid_columns", "signature": "fx_status fx_grid_columns(double avail, size_t ncols, double gap,\n                          doubl..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 131, "name": "fx_grid_columns_weighted", "signature": "fx_status fx_grid_columns_weighted(double avail, size_t ncols, double gap,\n                      ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 165, "name": "fx_grid_place_span", "signature": "fx_status fx_grid_place_span(size_t nitems, size_t ncols, const int *span,\n                      ..."}, {"doc": "Shared packer: wrap == 0 is the single-row v1 contract (overflow clamps in place); wrap != 0 starts a new row (cursors reset) for an item that no longer fits between the cursors, reporting rows via out_row. Half-pixel tolerance so * a 99.8% item is not wrapped by floating-point jitter.", "kind": "function", "line": 232, "name": "float_pack_impl", "signature": "static fx_status float_pack_impl(const double *width, const int *side, size_t n,\n                ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 271, "name": "fx_float_pack", "signature": "fx_status fx_float_pack(const double *width, const int *side, size_t n,\n                        d..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 276, "name": "fx_float_pack_wrap", "signature": "fx_status fx_float_pack_wrap(const double *width, const int *side, size_t n,\n                    ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 282, "name": "fx_grid_cell", "signature": "void fx_grid_cell(size_t index, size_t ncols, size_t *row, size_t *col)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 293, "name": "fx_justify_name", "signature": "const char *fx_justify_name(fx_justify j)"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 14, "name": "FX_EPS"}]}, {"id": "src/form.c", "kind": "module", "label": "form.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "1bee6065d4e7b59e", "symbol_count": 8, "symbols": [{"doc": "No I/O, no global state. The action/names/values are hostile data: encoded (application/x-www-form-urlencoded) and resolved through the canonical url module, never executed. Fails closed: a non-https resolved target yields FM_BLOCKED rather than a downgraded request.  #include \"form.h\" #include <string.h> const char FM_CONTENT_TYPE_URLENCODED[] = \"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\"; /* Appends one byte, keeping room for the trailing NUL. Returns -1 on overflow.", "kind": "function", "line": 17, "name": "put_char", "signature": "static int put_char(char *out, size_t outsz, size_t *pos, char c)"}, {"doc": "Encodes one component per the WHATWG application/x-www-form-urlencoded byte * serialiser. Returns -1 on overflow.", "kind": "function", "line": 25, "name": "enc_component", "signature": "static int enc_component(const char *s, char *out, size_t outsz, size_t *pos)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 42, "name": "fm_encode", "signature": "fm_status fm_encode(const fm_field *fields, size_t n,\n                    char *out, size_t outsz..."}, {"doc": "if (fields[i].name == NULL) continue; /* a nameless control is not submitted if (!first && put_char(out, outsz, &pos, '&') != 0) { out[0] = '\\0'; return FM_ERR_OVERFLOW; } first = 0; if (enc_component(fields[i].name, out, outsz, &pos) != 0) { out[0] = '\\0'; return FM_ERR_OVERFLOW; } if (put_char(out, outsz, &pos, '=') != 0) { out[0] = '\\0'; return FM_ERR_OVERFLOW; } const char *v = (fields[i].value != NULL) ? fields[i].value : \"\"; if (enc_component(v, out, outsz, &pos) != 0) { out[0] = '\\0'; return FM_ERR_OVERFLOW; } } out[pos] = '\\0'; if (out_len != NULL) *out_len = pos; return FM_OK; } /* Copies src into dst if it fits (including the NUL). Returns -1 on overflow.", "kind": "function", "line": 66, "name": "copy_fit", "signature": "static int copy_fit(char *dst, size_t dstsz, const char *src)"}, {"doc": "Removes TAB/LF/CR and trims leading/trailing ASCII spaces (WHATWG cleaning of a * URL-bearing attribute). Returns -1 if the cleaned value does not fit.", "kind": "function", "line": 75, "name": "clean_action", "signature": "static int clean_action(const char *action, char *out, size_t outsz)"}, {"doc": "if (c == '\\t' || c == '\\n' || c == '\\r') continue; if (o + 1 >= outsz) return -1; out[o++] = c; } out[o] = '\\0'; size_t s = 0, e = o; while (s < e && out[s] == ' ') ++s; while (e > s && out[e - 1] == ' ') --e; if (s > 0) memmove(out, out + s, e - s); out[e - s] = '\\0'; return 0; } /* Truncates url at the first '?' or '#' (drops query and fragment in place).", "kind": "function", "line": 93, "name": "strip_query", "signature": "static void strip_query(char *url)"}, {"doc": "Resolves the cleaned action into a validated absolute https URL R, or returns a * block reason.", "kind": "function", "line": 101, "name": "resolve_target", "signature": "static fm_block_reason resolve_target(const char *base, const char *act,\n                        ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 119, "name": "fm_build", "signature": "fm_status fm_build(const char *base, const char *action, fm_method method,\n                   con..."}]}, {"id": "src/frame_clock.c", "kind": "module", "label": "kind": "module", "label": "freedom.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "dabe3e759870bb5f", "symbol_count": 27, "symbols": [{"doc": "define EXIT_OK     0 define EXIT_ERROR  1 define EXIT_USAGE  2", "kind": "function", "line": 43, "name": "print_usage", "signature": "static void print_usage(FILE *fp, const char *prog)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 66, "name": "is_https_url", "signature": "static int is_https_url(const char *s)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 70, "name": "is_http_url", "signature": "static int is_http_url(const char *s)"}, {"doc": "fprintf(fp, \"  --dump-video-url: headless, print the first detected video source URL to stdout (no truncation)\\n\"); fprintf(fp, \"  --dump-video=PATH: headless download and save video stream from a URL to PATH\\n\"); fprintf(fp, \"                     ('-' for stdout, pipable to ffplay -i pipe:0)\\n\"); } static int is_https_url(const char *s) { return strncmp(s, \"https://\", 8) == 0; } static int is_http_url(const char *s) { return strncmp(s, \"http://\", 7) == 0; } /* A plain-http overlay URL (an i2p eepsite today): fetched over the network, not a file.", "kind": "function", "line": 76, "name": "is_overlay_http", "signature": "static int is_overlay_http(const char *s)"}, {"doc": "When non-NULL (set by --dump-video=PATH), headless mode downloads the video stream from a URL and writes it to the specified path. '-' writes to stdout, * suitable for piping to `ffplay -i pipe:0`. static const char *g_video_out = NULL; static char global_tor_addr[64] = \"127.0.0.1:9050\"; static char global_i2p_addr[64] = \"127.0.0.1:4444\"; static hb_set *g_hosts = NULL; static hb_set *g_impersonate = NULL; /* impersonate.conf: triple-opt-in TLS-blend hosts /* Reads the whole file into a NUL-terminated buffer. Caller frees with free().", "kind": "function", "line": 149, "name": "read_file", "signature": "static char *read_file(const char *path, size_t *out_len)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 166, "name": "headless_load_hosts", "signature": "static void headless_load_hosts(void)"}, {"doc": "impersonate.conf lives in the same search dirs; load it as an allowlist so * hb_is_allowlisted answers the third gate signal (covers subdomains). if (g_impersonate != NULL) { n = snprintf(path, sizeof path, \"%s/impersonate.conf\", dirs[i]); if (n > 0 && (size_t)n < sizeof path) { char *itxt = read_file(path, NULL); if (itxt != NULL) { hb_load(g_impersonate, itxt, HB_LIST_ALLOW); free(itxt); } } } } } /* True if s holds only ASCII spaces/tabs (or is empty).", "kind": "function", "line": 205, "name": "is_blank_text", "signature": "static int is_blank_text(const char *s)"}, {"doc": "Writes the render document as deterministic, flowing plain text for a terminal and for an AI agent (content as data, never instruction). Inline runs (paragraphs and links) flow onto one line and break on a block boundary; headings get a leading \"#\"*level marker, images show their gate decision and source, and notices are prefixed with \"! \". It is the same rd_doc the GUI * paints.", "kind": "function", "line": 218, "name": "print_doc", "signature": "static void print_doc(const rd_doc *doc)"}, {"doc": "Prints the captured Freebug console (the developer-visible JS transcript) to stdout, one entry per line, prefixed with its level and (for located errors) its source name:line:col. Deterministic order; an empty buffer prints just the * header so \"nothing logged\" is unambiguous.", "kind": "function", "line": 311, "name": "print_console", "signature": "static void print_console(const fb_buffer *log)"}, {"doc": "Prints the paint-ready render tree (dom_debug) to stdout. Two-pass: measure, then allocate exactly and format. The dump is bounded by the document; dd_format never * overruns and allocates nothing itself.", "kind": "function", "line": 328, "name": "print_dom", "signature": "static void print_dom(const rd_doc *doc)"}, {"doc": "Prints the CSS property inspector (dd_format_css) to stdout. Same contract as * print_dom: two-pass measure-then-allocate.", "kind": "function", "line": 343, "name": "print_dom_css", "signature": "static void print_dom_css(const rd_doc *doc)"}, {"doc": "tab_fetch_fn for the headless renderer: a policy-checked subresource fetch for page XHR/fetch and external <script src>. ctx is the page's top URL (or NULL for a local file): the RAW url from the worker is resolved against it with ln_resolve, the same gate a click gets (https-only, no downgrade, no foreign scheme), so relative subresources work. Realm-routed (fail-closed); no per-host allowlist here (headless is operator-driven -- enabling --js is the trust signal). The confined worker never * touches a socket; this runs in the trusted parent.", "kind": "function", "line": 367, "name": "headless_fetch", "signature": "static int headless_fetch(void *ctx, const char *method, const char *url,\n                       ..."}, {"doc": "Folds a page's document.cookie jar (\"a=1; b=2\") back into the ephemeral network * jar, one pair at a time, so JS-set session cookies reach the next request.", "kind": "function", "line": 425, "name": "foldback_cookies", "signature": "static void foldback_cookies(const char *url, const char *jar)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 440, "name": "render_page", "signature": "static int render_page(const char *html, size_t len, const char *top_url,\n                       ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 628, "name": "sf_reason", "signature": "static const char *sf_reason(sf_status ss)"}, {"doc": "Fetches one url with secure_fetch and renders the result. Pure, no _GNU_SOURCE dependency.", "kind": "function", "line": 38, "name": "last_char", "signature": "static const char *last_char(const char *s, size_t n, int c)"}, {"doc": "Extracts a named attribute value from an #EXT-X-STREAM-INF attribute string. attr is the attribute name (e.g. \"BANDWIDTH=\"); end points to end of the line. * Writes the parsed long into *val. Returns 1 on success, 0 otherwise.", "kind": "function", "line": 48, "name": "parse_attr_long", "signature": "static int parse_attr_long(const char *attrs, const char *end,\n                           const c..."}, {"doc": "static int parse_attr_long(const char *attrs, const char *end, const char *attr, long *val) { size_t alen = strlen(attr); const char *p = (const char *)memmem(attrs, (size_t)(end - attrs), attr, alen); if (p == NULL) return 0; p += alen; char *endp = NULL; long v = strtol(p, &endp, 10); if (endp == p) return 0; val = v; return 1; } /* Extracts a RESOLUTION=WxH attribute. Writes w and h only (default 0).", "kind": "function", "line": 62, "name": "parse_attr_resolution", "signature": "static void parse_attr_resolution(const char *attrs, const char *end,\n                           ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 79, "name": "hls_parse", "signature": "hls_status hls_parse(const char *text, size_t len, hls_playlist **out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 201, "name": "hls_select_variant", "signature": "size_t hls_select_variant(const hls_playlist *pl, int max_w, int max_h)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 222, "name": "hls_resolve_url", "signature": "size_t hls_resolve_url(const char *base_url, const char *segment_url,\n                       char..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 252, "name": "hls_playlist_free", "signature": "void hls_playlist_free(hls_playlist *pl)"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 20, "name": "_GNU_SOURCE"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 22, "name": "_POSIX_C_SOURCE"}]}, {"id": "src/hostblock.c", "kind": "module", "label": "hostblock.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "b76868d91fc72eca", "symbol_count": 18, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 24, "name": "hb_table"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 30, "name": "hb_set"}, {"doc": "Finds the slot for key (length klen) in t, which must have a free slot. Returns * the index of either the matching entry or the first empty slot for it.", "kind": "function", "line": 38, "name": "table_probe", "signature": "static size_t table_probe(const hb_table *t, const char *key, size_t klen)"}, {"doc": "Finds the slot for key (length klen) in t, which must have a free slot. Returns * the index of either the matching entry or the first empty slot for it. static size_t table_probe(const hb_table *t, const char *key, size_t klen) { size_t mask = t->cap - 1; size_t i = (size_t)fnv1a(key, klen) & mask; while (t->slots[i] != NULL) { if (strlen(t->slots[i]) == klen && memcmp(t->slots[i], key, klen) == 0) break; i = (i + 1) & mask; } return i; } /* Grows (or first-allocates) t to newcap slots and rehashes. Returns 0, -1 OOM.", "kind": "function", "line": 49, "name": "table_grow", "signature": "static int table_grow(hb_table *t, size_t newcap)"}, {"doc": "Inserts key (length klen) into t, deduping. Returns 0 (inserted or already * present), -1 on OOM.", "kind": "function", "line": 71, "name": "table_insert", "signature": "static int table_insert(hb_table *t, const char *key, size_t klen)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 91, "name": "table_contains", "signature": "static int table_contains(const hb_table *t, const char *key)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 97, "name": "table_free", "signature": "static void table_free(hb_table *t)"}, {"doc": "static int table_contains(const hb_table *t, const char *key) { if (t->cap == 0) return 0; size_t i = table_probe(t, key, strlen(key)); return t->slots[i] != NULL; } static void table_free(hb_table *t) { if (t->slots == NULL) return; for (size_t i = 0; i < t->cap; ++i) free(t->slots[i]); free(t->slots); t->slots = NULL; t->cap = 0; t->count = 0; } /* --- token helpers ---", "kind": "function", "line": 106, "name": "lower", "signature": "static char lower(char c)"}, {"doc": "A token made only of digits, '.' and ':' is an IP literal (v4 or v6), not a * domain. An empty token never reaches here.", "kind": "function", "line": 113, "name": "is_ip_token", "signature": "static int is_ip_token(const char *s, size_t n)"}, {"doc": "A token made only of digits, '.' and ':' is an IP literal (v4 or v6), not a * domain. An empty token never reaches here. static int is_ip_token(const char *s, size_t n) { for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) { char c = s[i]; if (!((c >= '0' && c <= '9') || c == '.' || c == ':')) return 0; } return 1; } /* True if c may appear in a domain we accept (after lowercasing).", "kind": "function", "line": 122, "name": "is_domain_char", "signature": "static int is_domain_char(char c)"}, {"doc": "token is not a domain we accept (caller skips it). static size_t normalise_domain(const char *tok, size_t n, char *out, size_t out_size) { if (n == 0 || n + 1 > out_size) return 0; for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) out[i] = lower(tok[i]); out[n] = '\\0'; if (out[n - 1] == '.') { --n; out[n] = '\\0'; }  /* trim one trailing dot if (n == 0 || n > HB_MAX_HOST) return 0; for (size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) { if (!is_domain_char(out[i])) return 0; } return n; } /* --- public ---", "kind": "function", "line": 143, "name": "hb_new", "signature": "hb_set *hb_new(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 148, "name": "hb_free", "signature": "void hb_free(hb_set *s)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 155, "name": "hb_load", "signature": "hb_status hb_load(hb_set *s, const char *text, hb_list list)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 192, "name": "hb_check", "signature": "hb_decision hb_check(const hb_set *s, const char *host)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 217, "name": "hb_is_allowlisted", "signature": "int hb_is_allowlisted(const hb_set *s, const char *host)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 237, "name": "hb_count", "signature": "size_t hb_count(const hb_set *s, hb_list list)"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 18, "name": "HB_MAX_HOST"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 20, "name": "HB_INIT_CAP"}]}, {"id": "src/hostedit.c", "kind": "module", "label": "hostedit.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "881c5be06362dabe", "symbol_count": 13, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 136, "name": "suggest_ctx"}, {"doc": "include <string.h>", "kind": "function", "line": 11, "name": "he_lower", "signature": "static char he_lower(char c)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 15, "name": "is_label_char", "signature": "static int is_label_char(char c)"}, {"doc": "#include \"hostedit.h\" #include <string.h> static char he_lower(char c) { return (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z') ? (char)(c - 'A' + 'a') : c; } static int is_label_char(char c) { return (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z') || (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z') || (c >= '0' && c <= '9') || c == '-'; } /* True if host is a plausible registrable hostname (see he_make_line contract).", "kind": "function", "line": 22, "name": "valid_host", "signature": "static int valid_host(const char *host, size_t n)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 40, "name": "he_make_line", "signature": "he_status he_make_line(const char *host, char *out, size_t cap)"}, {"doc": "True if the token looks like an IPv4 dotted-quad / contains only digits and dots * (matches hostblock's is_ip_token spirit: such a leading token is the ignored IP).", "kind": "function", "line": 67, "name": "is_ip_token", "signature": "static int is_ip_token(const char *ts, const char *te)"}, {"doc": "Visits each domain token (non-comment, non-IP) of a hosts-format text in document order, calling fn(ts, tl, ctx). If fn returns nonzero, iteration stops and he_scan * returns 1; otherwise 0. Single source of the line/token tokenisation.", "kind": "function", "line": 80, "name": "he_scan", "signature": "static int he_scan(const char *text, int (*fn)(const char *, size_t, void *), void *ctx)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 104, "name": "has_host_cb", "signature": "static int has_host_cb(const char *ts, size_t tl, void *ctx)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 108, "name": "he_text_has_host", "signature": "int he_text_has_host(const char *text, const char *host)"}, {"doc": "} return 0; } static int has_host_cb(const char *ts, size_t tl, void *ctx) { return token_eq_host(ts, ts + tl, (const char *)ctx); } int he_text_has_host(const char *text, const char *host) { if (text == NULL || host == NULL || host[0] == '\\0') return 0; return he_scan(text, has_host_cb, (void *)host); } /* Lowercase substring search: 1 if needle (lowercased) occurs in [hs,he).", "kind": "function", "line": 115, "name": "contains_ci", "signature": "static int contains_ci(const char *hs, size_t hl, const char *needle)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 127, "name": "starts_with_ci", "signature": "static int starts_with_ci(const char *hs, size_t hl, const char *pfx)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 143, "name": "suggest_cb", "signature": "static int suggest_cb(const char *ts, size_t tl, void *vctx)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 163, "name": "he_suggest", "signature": "int he_suggest(const char *text, const char *query,\n               char results[][HE_MAX_HOST + 1..."}]}, {"id": "src/html_parse.c", "kind": "module", "label": "html_parse.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "e6a3672b1d53129d", "symbol_count": 27, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 20, "name": "hp_document"}, {"doc": "#include \"html_parse.h\" #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> #include <lexbor/html/html.h> #include <lexbor/dom/dom.h> #include <lexbor/tag/const.h> struct hp_document { lxb_html_document_t *doc; }; /* --- helpers ---", "kind": "function", "line": 25, "name": "dup_bytes", "signature": "static char *dup_bytes(const lxb_char_t *src, size_t len)"}, {"doc": "}; /* --- helpers --- static char *dup_bytes(const lxb_char_t *src, size_t len) { if (len == (size_t)-1) return NULL; /* guard: len+1 would overflow to 0 char *out = (char *)malloc(len + 1); if (out == NULL) return NULL; if (len != 0 && src != NULL) memcpy(out, src, len); out[len] = '\\0'; return out; } /* Iterative pre-order successor of node within the subtree rooted at root.", "kind": "function", "line": 36, "name": "node_next", "signature": "static lxb_dom_node_t *node_next(lxb_dom_node_t *node, const lxb_dom_node_t *root)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 45, "name": "attr_is_event_handler", "signature": "static int attr_is_event_handler(const lxb_dom_attr_t *attr)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 52, "name": "node_is_script", "signature": "static int node_is_script(const lxb_dom_node_t *node)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 58, "name": "strip_scripts", "signature": "static void strip_scripts(lxb_html_document_t *document)"}, {"doc": "Case-insensitive (ASCII) substring test over a length-delimited attribute value. * needle must be lowercase letters.", "kind": "function", "line": 85, "name": "mem_contains_ci", "signature": "static int mem_contains_ci(const lxb_char_t *hay, size_t hlen, const char *needle)"}, {"doc": "Classifies a <script>: a classic program runs only when its type is absent/empty or a JavaScript MIME type (contains \"javascript\"/\"ecmascript\"). Every other type is a data block the browser never executes -- JSON/LD data, ES modules (import/export cannot run as a classic script), and template blocks like text/x-jquery-tmpl / text/html / text/template (executing their markup throws a SyntaxError; this was Slashdot's \"#each\" errors). Fail closed: unrecognized type -> skip. A src attribute makes it external (its inline body, if any, is ignored -- browser rule); otherwise it runs from its inline source. The parser never fetches: external scripts are only REPORTED with their raw src. Mirrors * ctype_is_javascript in tab.c (same rule for the fetched Content-Type).", "kind": "function", "line": 109, "name": "script_classify", "signature": "static int script_classify(const lxb_dom_node_t *n,\n                           const lxb_char_t *..."}, {"doc": "Collects each executable <script> as its own entry, in document order: inline source for classic inline scripts, the raw src attribute for external ones. Browsers run each <script> as a separate program, so the worker evaluates them one by one: an uncaught exception in one script must not abort the others (concatenating into a single eval would let the first failure kill them all). Bounded by HP_MAX_SCRIPTS (fail-closed anti-DoS). NULL with *out_count == 0 when * there are none or on allocation failure; release with hp_free_scripts.", "kind": "function", "line": 138, "name": "hp_extract_script_list", "signature": "hp_script *hp_extract_script_list(const hp_document *doc, size_t *out_count)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 200, "name": "hp_free_scripts", "signature": "void hp_free_scripts(hp_script *scripts, size_t count)"}, {"doc": "Whitespace-separated token test over a length-delimited attribute value, ASCII case-insensitive: rel=\"preload stylesheet\" has the token \"stylesheet\"; * rel=\"notstylesheet\" does not. needle must be lowercase.", "kind": "function", "line": 214, "name": "attr_has_token_ci", "signature": "static int attr_has_token_ci(const lxb_char_t *val, size_t vlen, const char *needle)"}, {"doc": "An applicable stylesheet <link>: rel token \"stylesheet\" and not \"alternate\"; a non-empty href; media absent/empty or mentioning screen/all (fail closed). * On a match, href and hlen point into the element's attribute storage.", "kind": "function", "line": 235, "name": "link_is_active_stylesheet", "signature": "static int link_is_active_stylesheet(lxb_dom_element_t *el,\n                                     ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 256, "name": "hp_extract_stylesheet_hrefs", "signature": "char **hp_extract_stylesheet_hrefs(const hp_document *doc, size_t *out_count)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 290, "name": "hp_free_stylesheet_hrefs", "signature": "void hp_free_stylesheet_hrefs(char **hrefs, size_t count)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 296, "name": "strip_event_handlers", "signature": "static void strip_event_handlers(lxb_html_document_t *document)"}, {"doc": "for (lxb_dom_attr_t *attr = lxb_dom_element_first_attribute(el); attr != NULL; attr = lxb_dom_element_next_attribute(attr)) { if (attr_is_event_handler(attr)) { (void)lxb_dom_element_attr_remove(el, attr); again = 1; /* iterator invalidated; restart this element break; } } } } } /* --- public: config & validator ---", "kind": "function", "line": 319, "name": "hp_config_default", "signature": "hp_config hp_config_default(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 327, "name": "hp_validate_input", "signature": "hp_status hp_validate_input(const char *html, size_t len, const hp_config *cfg)"}, {"doc": "c.strip_scripts = 1; c.strip_event_handlers = 1; return c; } hp_status hp_validate_input(const char *html, size_t len, const hp_config *cfg) { if (html == NULL) return HP_ERR_NULL_ARG; size_t cap = (cfg != NULL && cfg->max_bytes != 0) ? cfg->max_bytes : HP_DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES; if (len == 0) return HP_ERR_EMPTY; if (len > cap) return HP_ERR_TOO_LARGE; return HP_OK; } /* --- public: parse ---", "kind": "function", "line": 337, "name": "hp_parse", "signature": "hp_status hp_parse(const char *html, size_t len, const hp_config *cfg, hp_document **out)"}, {"doc": "if (local.strip_scripts) strip_scripts(document); if (local.strip_event_handlers) strip_event_handlers(document); hp_document *wrap = (hp_document *)malloc(sizeof *wrap); if (wrap == NULL) { lxb_html_document_destroy(document); return HP_ERR_OOM; } wrap->doc = document; out = wrap; return HP_OK; } /* --- public: queries ---", "kind": "function", "line": 371, "name": "hp_element_count", "signature": "size_t hp_element_count(const hp_document *doc)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 381, "name": "hp_script_count", "signature": "size_t hp_script_count(const hp_document *doc)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 391, "name": "hp_event_handler_count", "signature": "size_t hp_event_handler_count(const hp_document *doc)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 407, "name": "hp_extract_text", "signature": "char *hp_extract_text(const hp_document *doc, size_t *out_len)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 426, "name": "hp_get_title", "signature": "char *hp_get_title(const hp_document *doc, size_t *out_len)"}, {"doc": "char *hp_get_title(const hp_document *doc, size_t *out_len) { if (out_len != NULL) *out_len = 0; if (doc == NULL || doc->doc == NULL) return NULL; size_t len = 0; const lxb_char_t *title = lxb_html_document_title(doc->doc, &len); if (title == NULL) return dup_bytes((const lxb_char_t *)\"\", 0); char *copy = dup_bytes(title, len); if (copy != NULL && out_len != NULL) *out_len = len; return copy; } /* --- public: cleanup ---", "kind": "function", "line": 441, "name": "hp_free", "signature": "void hp_free(char *buf)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 445, "name": "hp_document_free", "signature": "void hp_document_free(hp_document *doc)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 451, "name": "hp_document_root", "signature": "const void *hp_document_root(const hp_document *doc)"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 8, "name": "_POSIX_C_SOURCE"}]}, {"id": "src/image_decode.c", "kind": "module", "label": "image_decode.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "f658d18e65c2b16c", "symbol_count": 26, "symbols": [{"doc": "libjpeg error manager that longjmps instead of calling exit(), so a hostile JPEG * fails closed (IMG_ERR_DECODE) without tearing the worker down.", "kind": "struct", "line": 153, "name": "jpeg_err_ctx"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 255, "name": "gif_reader"}, {"doc": "return 1; } /* Skips a chain of data sub-blocks up to and including the 0 terminator. static int gr_skip_subblocks(gif_reader *r) { uint8_t n; for (;;) { if (!gr_u8(r, &n)) return 0; if (n == 0u) return 1; if (!gr_skip(r, n)) return 0; } } /* Bit reader over the LZW data sub-block chain, LSB-first.", "kind": "struct", "line": 290, "name": "gif_bits"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 51, "name": "read_be32", "signature": "static uint32_t read_be32(const uint8_t *p)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 56, "name": "img_png_dimensions", "signature": "img_status img_png_dimensions(const uint8_t *bytes, size_t len,\n                              uin..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 67, "name": "img_dimensions_ok", "signature": "int img_dimensions_ok(uint32_t w, uint32_t h)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 75, "name": "img_fit", "signature": "void img_fit(uint32_t iw, uint32_t ih, double box_w, double box_h,\n             double *out_w, do..."}, {"doc": "void img_fit(uint32_t iw, uint32_t ih, double box_w, double box_h, double *out_w, double *out_h) { if (out_w == NULL || out_h == NULL) return; out_w = 0.0; out_h = 0.0; if (iw == 0u || ih == 0u || box_w <= 0.0 || box_h <= 0.0) return; double sx = box_w / (double)iw; double sy = box_h / (double)ih; double s = (sx < sy) ? sx : sy; out_w = (double)iw * s; out_h = (double)ih * s; } /* Premultiplies straight-alpha BGRA in place: c' = round(c * a / 255).", "kind": "function", "line": 90, "name": "premultiply", "signature": "static void premultiply(uint8_t *data, size_t pixels)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 101, "name": "img_decode_png", "signature": "img_status img_decode_png(const uint8_t *bytes, size_t len, img_pixels *out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 157, "name": "jpeg_error_longjmp", "signature": "static void jpeg_error_longjmp(j_common_ptr cinfo)"}, {"doc": "libjpeg error manager that longjmps instead of calling exit(), so a hostile JPEG * fails closed (IMG_ERR_DECODE) without tearing the worker down. struct jpeg_err_ctx { struct jpeg_error_mgr pub; jmp_buf jb; }; static void jpeg_error_longjmp(j_common_ptr cinfo) { struct jpeg_err_ctx *e = (struct jpeg_err_ctx *)cinfo->err; longjmp(e->jb, 1); } /* Swallow libjpeg's warning/trace text; the worker has no business writing to stderr.", "kind": "function", "line": 164, "name": "jpeg_silence", "signature": "static void jpeg_silence(j_common_ptr cinfo)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 165, "name": "img_decode_jpeg", "signature": "img_status img_decode_jpeg(const uint8_t *bytes, size_t len, img_pixels *out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 259, "name": "gr_u8", "signature": "static int gr_u8(gif_reader *r, uint8_t *out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 265, "name": "gr_u16le", "signature": "static int gr_u16le(gif_reader *r, uint16_t *out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 272, "name": "gr_skip", "signature": "static int gr_skip(gif_reader *r, size_t n)"}, {"doc": "static int gr_u16le(gif_reader *r, uint16_t *out) { if (r->pos + 2u > r->len) return 0; out = (uint16_t)(r->p[r->pos] | ((uint16_t)r->p[r->pos + 1] << 8)); r->pos += 2u; return 1; } static int gr_skip(gif_reader *r, size_t n) { if (r->pos + n > r->len) return 0; r->pos += n; return 1; } /* Skips a chain of data sub-blocks up to and including the 0 terminator.", "kind": "function", "line": 280, "name": "gr_skip_subblocks", "signature": "static int gr_skip_subblocks(gif_reader *r)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 297, "name": "gb_next_code", "signature": "static int gb_next_code(gif_bits *b, unsigned width, unsigned *out)"}, {"doc": "} uint8_t byte; if (!gr_u8(b->r, &byte)) return 0; b->block_left--; b->acc |= (uint32_t)byte << b->nbits; b->nbits += 8u; } out = b->acc & ((1u << width) - 1u); b->acc >>= width; b->nbits -= width; return 1; } /* Visual row of interlaced-frame row `r` (4-pass GIF interlace).", "kind": "function", "line": 319, "name": "gif_deinterlace_row", "signature": "static uint32_t gif_deinterlace_row(uint32_t r, uint32_t fh)"}, {"doc": "Writes one palette pixel into the canvas at frame-relative index i (clipped to * the canvas; } static double sample_bezier_y(double t, double cy1, double cy2) { double u = 1.0 - t; return 3.0 * u * u * t * cy1 + 3.0 * u * t * t * cy2 + t * t * t; } /* Newton-Raphson to find t where Bx(t) = x.", "kind": "function", "line": 35, "name": "solve_bezier_t", "signature": "static double solve_bezier_t(double x, double cx1, double cx2)"}, {"doc": "double t = x; for (int i = 0; i < 8; ++i) { double bx = sample_bezier_x(t, cx1, cx2); double dx = sample_bezier_dx(t, cx1, cx2); if (fabs(dx) < 1e-12) break; t = t - (bx - x) / dx; if (t < 0.0) t = 0.0; if (t > 1.0) t = 1.0; if (fabs(bx - x) < 1e-7) break; } return t; } /* ---- ip_ease ------------------------------------------------------", "kind": "function", "line": 54, "name": "ip_ease", "signature": "double ip_ease(double t, const ip_ease_fn *fn)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 62, "name": "ip_ease", "signature": "case IP_EASE_EASE:\n        return ip_ease(t, &(ip_ease_fn)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 68, "name": "ip_ease", "signature": "case IP_EASE_EASE_IN:\n        return ip_ease(t, &(ip_ease_fn)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 74, "name": "ip_ease", "signature": "case IP_EASE_EASE_OUT:\n        return ip_ease(t, &(ip_ease_fn)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 80, "name": "ip_ease", "signature": "case IP_EASE_EASE_IN_OUT:\n        return ip_ease(t, &(ip_ease_fn)"}, {"doc": "double cx1 = clamp01(fn->cx1); double cy1 = clamp01(fn->cy1); double cx2 = clamp01(fn->cx2); double cy2 = clamp01(fn->cy2); double bt = solve_bezier_t(t, cx1, cx2); return sample_bezier_y(bt, cy1, cy2); } default: return t; } } /* ---- ip_lerp / ip_lerp_color -------------------------------------", "kind": "function", "line": 133, "name": "ip_lerp", "signature": "double ip_lerp(double a, double b, double t)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 138, "name": "ip_lerp_color", "signature": "uint32_t ip_lerp_color(uint32_t c1, uint32_t c2, double t)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 161, "name": "ip_interp", "signature": "double ip_interp(ip_val_kind kind, double a, double b, double t)"}, {"doc": "} double ip_interp(ip_val_kind kind, double a, double b, double t) { switch (kind) { case IP_VAL_SCALAR: return ip_lerp(a, b, t); The ONLY network primitive exposed to script; it does NOT touch a socket -- it calls the host fetch callback, which proxies to the trusted parent (full network policy re-applied there). /* sticky within one eval: an allocation was denied by the cap } js_mem_state; struct js_context { JSRuntime      *rt; JSContext      *ctx; js_mem_state    mem;       /* heap accounting + cap enforcement js_limits       limits;    /* resolved limits in effect for this context struct timespec deadline;  /* armed per-eval when has_deadline != 0 int             has_deadline; int             interrupted; /* set by the interrupt handler on timeout }; /* --- bounded allocator (QuickJS backend) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 44, "name": "jm_malloc", "signature": "static void *jm_malloc(void *opaque, size_t size)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 52, "name": "jm_calloc", "signature": "static void *jm_calloc(void *opaque, size_t count, size_t size)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 62, "name": "jm_free", "signature": "static void jm_free(void *opaque, void *ptr)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 68, "name": "jm_realloc", "signature": "static void *jm_realloc(void *opaque, void *ptr, size_t size)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 78, "name": "jm_usable_size", "signature": "static size_t jm_usable_size(const void *ptr)"}, {"doc": "if (p == NULL && size != 0) return NULL; /* original ptr left untouched m->used = m->used - old + ((p != NULL) ? malloc_usable_size(p) : 0); return p; } static size_t jm_usable_size(const void *ptr) { return malloc_usable_size((void *)ptr); } static const JSMallocFunctions FREEDOM_MF = { jm_calloc, jm_malloc, jm_free, jm_realloc, jm_usable_size }; /* --- helpers ---", "kind": "function", "line": 88, "name": "host_dup", "signature": "static char *host_dup(const char *src, size_t len)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 97, "name": "timespec_reached", "signature": "static int timespec_reached(const struct timespec *now, const struct timespec *deadline)"}, {"doc": "if (len == (size_t)-1) return NULL; /* guard: len+1 would overflow to 0 char *out = (char *)malloc(len + 1); if (out == NULL) return NULL; if (len != 0 && src != NULL) memcpy(out, src, len); out[len] = '\\0'; return out; } static int timespec_reached(const struct timespec *now, const struct timespec *deadline) { if (now->tv_sec != deadline->tv_sec) return now->tv_sec > deadline->tv_sec; return now->tv_nsec >= deadline->tv_nsec; } /* Returns nonzero to interrupt the engine once the per-eval budget is spent.", "kind": "function", "line": 104, "name": "js_interrupt_cb", "signature": "static int js_interrupt_cb(JSRuntime *rt, void *opaque)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 116, "name": "is_ascii_digit", "signature": "static int is_ascii_digit(char c)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 118, "name": "js_loc_from_stack", "signature": "int js_loc_from_stack(const char *stack, char *file_out, size_t file_cap,\n                      i..."}, {"doc": "if (out_col  != NULL) *out_col  = cl; } JS_FreeCString(ctx, ss); } else { JS_FreeValue(ctx, JS_GetException(ctx)); /* ToCString may have thrown } } JS_FreeValue(ctx, st); JS_FreeValue(ctx, exc); return msg; } /* --- public: defaults & validator ---", "kind": "function", "line": 236, "name": "js_limits_default", "signature": "js_limits js_limits_default(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 245, "name": "limits_resolve", "signature": "static js_limits limits_resolve(const js_limits *lim)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 254, "name": "js_validate_source", "signature": "js_status js_validate_source(const char *src, size_t len, const js_limits *lim)"}, {"doc": "if (l.time_budget_ms == 0)     l.time_budget_ms     = JS_DEFAULT_TIME_BUDGET; return l; } js_status js_validate_source(const char *src, size_t len, const js_limits *lim) { if (src == NULL) return JS_ERR_NULL_ARG; size_t cap = (lim != NULL && lim->max_source_bytes != 0) ? lim->max_source_bytes : JS_DEFAULT_MAX_SOURCE; if (len == 0) return JS_ERR_EMPTY; if (len > cap) return JS_ERR_TOO_LARGE; return JS_OK; } /* --- public: lifecycle ---", "kind": "function", "line": 265, "name": "js_context_new", "signature": "js_status js_context_new(const js_limits *lim, js_context **out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 304, "name": "js_context_free", "signature": "void js_context_free(js_context *ctx)"}, {"doc": "c->has_deadline = 0; Returns 0, or -1 if the href does not fit in out.", "kind": "function", "line": 19, "name": "clean_href", "signature": "static int clean_href(const char *href, char *out, size_t outsz)"}, {"doc": "if (o + 1 >= outsz) return -1; out[o++] = (char)c; } out[o] = '\\0'; size_t start = 0; while (out[start] == ' ') ++start; if (start > 0) memmove(out, out + start, strlen(out + start) + 1); size_t len = strlen(out); while (len > 0 && out[len - 1] == ' ') out[--len] = '\\0'; return 0; } /* Case-insensitive: does s begin with prefix?", "kind": "function", "line": 38, "name": "ci_prefix", "signature": "static int ci_prefix(const char *s, const char *prefix)"}, {"doc": "Splits the fragment (everything after the first '#') out of ref: copies the fragment id (without '#') into frag, then truncates ref at the '#'. A fragment * that does not fit is dropped (frag becomes \"\"), never truncated to a partial id. static void split_fragment(char *ref, char *frag, size_t fragsz) { frag[0] = '\\0'; char *hash = strchr(ref, '#'); if (hash == NULL) return; const char *f = hash + 1; size_t fl = strlen(f); if (fl + 1 <= fragsz) memcpy(frag, f, fl + 1); hash = '\\0'; } /* Classifies why a fragment-less reference could not become a navigable target.", "kind": "function", "line": 62, "name": "classify_block", "signature": "static ln_block_reason classify_block(const char *ref)"}, {"doc": "const char *f = hash + 1; size_t fl = strlen(f); if (fl + 1 <= fragsz) memcpy(frag, f, fl + 1); hash = '\\0'; } /* Classifies why a fragment-less reference could not become a navigable target. static ln_block_reason classify_block(const char *ref) { if (ci_prefix(ref, \"http://\")) return LN_BLOCK_DOWNGRADE; if (url_has_scheme(ref)) return LN_BLOCK_FOREIGN_SCHEME; return LN_BLOCK_UNSUPPORTED; } /* Length of base up to and including the last '/'; 0 when base has no '/'.", "kind": "function", "line": 69, "name": "file_dir_len", "signature": "static size_t file_dir_len(const char *base)"}, {"doc": "return LN_BLOCK_UNSUPPORTED; } /* Length of base up to and including the last '/'; 0 when base has no '/'. static size_t file_dir_len(const char *base) { size_t last = 0; int found = 0; for (size_t i = 0; base[i] != '\\0'; ++i) { if (base[i] == '/') { last = i; found = 1; } } return found ? last + 1 : 0; } /* Nonzero iff the last segment of body[0..blen) is exactly \"..\".", "kind": "function", "line": 79, "name": "last_seg_is_dotdot", "signature": "static int last_seg_is_dotdot(const char *body, size_t blen)"}, {"doc": "for (size_t i = 0; base[i] != '\\0'; ++i) { if (base[i] == '/') { last = i; found = 1; } } return found ? last + 1 : 0; } /* Nonzero iff the last segment of body[0..blen) is exactly \"..\". static int last_seg_is_dotdot(const char *body, size_t blen) { size_t s = blen; while (s > 0 && body[s - 1] != '/') --s; return (blen - s == 2 && body[s] == '.' && body[s + 1] == '.'); } /* Appends seg to body (with a '/' separator when non-empty). 0, or -1 on overflow.", "kind": "function", "line": 86, "name": "append_seg", "signature": "static int append_seg(char *body, size_t bodysz, size_t *blen,\n                      const char *..."}, {"doc": "/* Appends seg to body (with a '/' separator when non-empty). 0, or -1 on overflow. static int append_seg(char *body, size_t bodysz, size_t *blen, const char *seg, size_t seglen) { size_t need = (*blen > 0 ? 1u : 0u) + seglen; if (*blen + need + 1 > bodysz) return -1; if (*blen > 0) body[(*blen)++] = '/'; memcpy(body + *blen, seg, seglen); blen += seglen; body[*blen] = '\\0'; return 0; } /* Drops the last segment (and its preceding '/') from body.", "kind": "function", "line": 98, "name": "pop_seg", "signature": "static void pop_seg(char *body, size_t *blen)"}, {"doc": "Resolves a local file reference (relative or absolute path) against base into * out. The fragment (#...) is dropped. Returns 0, or -1 on overflow/empty.", "kind": "function", "line": 149, "name": "resolve_file", "signature": "static int resolve_file(const char *base, const char *ref, char *out, size_t outsz)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 171, "name": "ln_resolve", "signature": "ln_status ln_resolve(const char *base, const char *href, ln_result *out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 240, "name": "ln_block_reason_text", "signature": "const char *ln_block_reason_text(ln_block_reason reason)"}]}, {"doc": "include <openssl/kdf.h> ifndef OSSL_KDF_PARAM_ARGON2_LANES define OSSL_KDF_PARAM_ARGON2_LANES \"lanes\" endif ifndef OSSL_KDF_PARAM_ARGON2_MEMCOST define OSSL_KDF_PARAM_ARGON2_MEMCOST \"memcost\" endif ifndef OSSL_KDF_PARAM_THREADS define OSSL_KDF_PARAM_THREADS \"threads\" endif define _GNU_SOURCE include <openssl/kdf.h> ifndef OSSL_KDF_PARAM_ARGON2_LANES define OSSL_KDF_PARAM_ARGON2_LANES \"lanes\" endif ifndef OSSL_KDF_PARAM_ARGON2_MEMCOST define OSSL_KDF_PARAM_ARGON2_MEMCOST \"memcost\" endif ifndef OSSL_KDF_PARAM_THREADS define OSSL_KDF_PARAM_THREADS \"threads\" endif define _GNU_SOURCE include <openssl/kdf.h> ifndef OSSL_KDF_PARAM_ARGON2_LANES define OSSL_KDF_PARAM_ARGON2_LANES \"lanes\" endif ifndef OSSL_KDF_PARAM_ARGON2_MEMCOST define OSSL_KDF_PARAM_ARGON2_MEMCOST \"memcost\" endif ifndef OSSL_KDF_PARAM_THREADS", "id": "src/local_store.c", "kind": "module", "label": "local_store.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "428aa0a6d83f7ba0", "symbol_count": 235, "symbols": [{"doc": "#define LS_ARGON2_M_KIB  19456u  /* memory cost in KiB (19 MiB) #define LS_ARGON2_P      1u      /* lanes / parallelism /* Container field offsets. #define OFF_MAGIC   0u #define OFF_VERSION 4u #define OFF_AEAD    5u #define OFF_KDF     6u #define OFF_SALT    8u #define OFF_NONCE   24u static const uint8_t LS_MAGIC[4] = { 'F', 'R', 'Z', 'S' }; /* --- AEAD selection ---", "kind": "function", "line": 628, "name": "cipher_for", "signature": "static const EVP_CIPHER *cipher_for(ls_aead aead)"}, {"doc": "static const uint8_t LS_MAGIC[4] = { 'F', 'R', 'Z', 'S' }; /* --- AEAD selection --- static const EVP_CIPHER *cipher_for(ls_aead aead) { switch (aead) { case LS_AEAD_AES256_GCM:        return EVP_aes_256_gcm(); case LS_AEAD_CHACHA20_POLY1305: return EVP_chacha20_poly1305(); default:                        return NULL; } } /* --- KDF: Argon2id ---", "kind": "function", "line": 638, "name": "argon2id_derive", "signature": "static ls_status argon2id_derive(const uint8_t *pass, size_t pass_len,\n                          ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 667, "name": "ls_derive_key", "signature": "ls_status ls_derive_key(const uint8_t *passphrase, size_t pass_len,\n                        const..."}, {"doc": "int rc = EVP_KDF_derive(kctx, out, out_len, params); EVP_KDF_CTX_free(kctx); return (rc == 1) ? LS_OK : LS_ERR_KDF; } ls_status ls_derive_key(const uint8_t *passphrase, size_t pass_len, const uint8_t *salt, size_t salt_len, uint8_t out_key[LS_KEY_LEN]) { if (passphrase == NULL || salt == NULL || out_key == NULL) return LS_ERR_NULL_ARG; return argon2id_derive(passphrase, pass_len, salt, salt_len, out_key, LS_KEY_LEN); } /* --- AEAD primitives ---", "kind": "function", "line": 676, "name": "aead_encrypt", "signature": "static ls_status aead_encrypt(const EVP_CIPHER *cipher, const uint8_t *key,\n                     ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 702, "name": "aead_decrypt", "signature": "static ls_status aead_decrypt(const EVP_CIPHER *cipher, const uint8_t *key,\n                     ..."}, {"doc": "if (aad_len > 0 && EVP_DecryptUpdate(ctx, NULL, &len, aad, (int)aad_len) != 1) goto done; if (ct_len > 0) { if (EVP_DecryptUpdate(ctx, pt, &len, ct, (int)ct_len) != 1) goto done; total = len; } if (EVP_CIPHER_CTX_ctrl(ctx, EVP_CTRL_AEAD_SET_TAG, LS_TAG_LEN, (void *)tag) != 1) goto done; /* Tag verification happens here; a mismatch fails closed. st = (EVP_DecryptFinal_ex(ctx, pt + total, &len) == 1) ? The caller passes the complete segment data so avformat can probe it. On success the * decoder context is ready for decode_segment calls.", "kind": "function", "line": 96, "name": "decoder_init", "signature": "static int decoder_init(decoder_ctx *dc, const uint8_t *data, size_t len)"}, {"doc": "Decodes one segment of TS data and sends frames. close(pfd); if (rc != 0) { close(rfd); return OS_ERR_LANDLOCK; } } /* No-new-privs is required before restrict_self; it also pins the domain. if (prctl(PR_SET_NO_NEW_PRIVS, 1, 0, 0, 0) != 0) { close(rfd); return OS_ERR_LANDLOCK; } if (ll_restrict_self(rfd, 0) != 0) { close(rfd); return OS_ERR_LANDLOCK; } close(rfd); return OS_OK; } #else /* non-Linux, or kernel headers without Landlock", "page_view.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "638ce43adc3b1885", "symbol_count": 114, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 217, "name": "pv_node_map"}, {"doc": "Nearest-container info attached to a run, plus the flex per-item values (Stage 3): grow/shrink/basis/order come from the ITEM element (the container's direct child on the run's ancestor chain); direction from the container itself. Unset sentinels: grow/shrink -1, basis/order CSS_LEN_UNSET, direction 0. `item` is that same direct child (NULL = anonymous item: text directly inside the container); pv_build maps it * to the run's cont_item ordinal so inline fragments of one child share one item.", "kind": "struct", "line": 772, "name": "pv_cont_info"}, {"doc": "Per-container item-ordinal tracker: ord[cid] is the ordinal last handed out for container cid, node[cid] the direct-child element it belongs to. A run whose item differs from the tracked one (or is NULL = anonymous) opens the next ordinal, so consecutive runs of one child share it and nested-container interruptions do not * break the outer container's item continuity.", "kind": "struct", "line": 798, "name": "pv_item_track"}, {"doc": "Author box model resolved for a run: horizontal placement (l/r insets, w cap, centered) from the nearest block ancestor that declares a box, plus the leaf * block's own vertical-margin override (mt/mb, or PV_LEN_UNSET).", "kind": "struct", "line": 817, "name": "pv_box_info"}, {"doc": "Document-order registry of flex/grid container nodes, so the runs of one * container share a stable id.", "kind": "struct", "line": 980, "name": "pv_container_reg"}, {"doc": "Box engine (Hito 23b-8 Step D): document-order registry of box-carrying block nodes plus each box's resolved definition (decoration + parent link). A box's * block_id is its index here, so the painter can reconstruct the box tree.", "kind": "struct", "line": 997, "name": "pv_box_reg"}, {"doc": "Per-document memo of cch_element_style() results, keyed by element pointer. resolve_context()/in_hidden_subtree() call cch_element_style once per ANCESTOR on every walk from a node up to `base`; a common ancestor (e.g. <body>, a wrapper <div>) is revisited once per descendant text/element node, and each revisit re-runs the full O(nsels) cascade from scratch. On a page with a real stylesheet (hundreds to thousands of selectors -- see css.c's CSS_MAX_* caps) that redundant work dominates page_view's runtime. The cache trades it for one cascade per unique element plus a linear scan (bounded by the document's own element count, normally far smaller than the selector count). Grows like pv_node_map: a dynamic array, doubling, degrading to \"no cache\" (not a crash) * on OOM.", "kind": "struct", "line": 1138, "name": "pv_style_cache"}, {"doc": "One <form> seen in document order: its grouping id is its index. action is an owned NUL-terminated copy of the raw action attribute (or NULL); method is GET * unless method=\"post\".", "kind": "struct", "line": 1677, "name": "form_rec"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 1683, "name": "form_table"}, {"doc": "Cache of the per-table flow decision (a table subtree is scanned at most once, * anti-DoS). Registry full => grid (the previous behaviour), bounded fail-closed.", "kind": "struct", "line": 2096, "name": "pv_flow_reg"}, {"doc": "Unicode scalar for a Windows-1252 byte (only meaningful for c >= 0x80). 0xA0.. 0xFF map identically to Latin-1 (code point == byte). 0x80..0x9F carry the Windows-1252 printable glyphs; the five undefined positions return 0 (the caller * emits '?').", "kind": "function", "line": 55, "name": "cp1252_to_ucs", "signature": "static unsigned int cp1252_to_ucs(unsigned char c)"}, {"doc": "Encodes a BMP scalar (<= 0xFFFF, which covers all Windows-1252 targets) as * UTF-8 into out (up to 3 bytes); returns the byte count written.", "kind": "function", "line": 69, "name": "utf8_encode", "signature": "static size_t utf8_encode(unsigned int cp, char *out)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 81, "name": "utf8_sanitized_dup", "signature": "static char *utf8_sanitized_dup(const char *s)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 115, "name": "dup_n", "signature": "static char *dup_n(const char *s, size_t n)"}, {"doc": "Common field initialization shared by all append helpers. Keeps the three constructors DRY and guarantees every run starts from the same sentinel state * (including the Stage 0 node_id and the Step D block_id).", "kind": "function", "line": 130, "name": "run_init_common", "signature": "static void run_init_common(pv_run *r)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 222, "name": "pv_node_map_init", "signature": "static int pv_node_map_init(pv_node_map *m)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 230, "name": "pv_node_map_free", "signature": "static void pv_node_map_free(pv_node_map *m)"}, {"doc": "Builds a document-order map of all element nodes under root. 0; } const pv_box_def *pv_box_at(const pv_view *v, size_t i) { if (v == NULL || i >= v->nbox) return NULL; return &v->boxes[i]; } /* --- builder helpers --- /* Iterative pre-order successor of node within the subtree rooted at root.", "kind": "function", "line": 626, "name": "node_next", "signature": "static lxb_dom_node_t *node_next(lxb_dom_node_t *node, const lxb_dom_node_t *root)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 635, "name": "is_block_tag", "signature": "static int is_block_tag(lxb_tag_id_t t)"}, {"doc": "An element should be treated as block-like (eligible for box registration, hbox, float) when its CSS display property indicates a block-level box, even if the tag itself is normally inline. This lets author CSS like a { display: inline-block; width: 22px; } * actually create a box for the <a> tag.", "kind": "function", "line": 661, "name": "is_block_like", "signature": "static int is_block_like(lxb_tag_id_t t, css_display display)"}, {"doc": "Returns 1 when the element should cause a block break (start a new line). display:inline-block and display:inline do NOT cause a break; they flow * inline while still being eligible for box registration.", "kind": "function", "line": 671, "name": "causes_block_break", "signature": "static int causes_block_break(lxb_tag_id_t t, css_display display)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 676, "name": "heading_level", "signature": "static int heading_level(lxb_tag_id_t t)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 688, "name": "is_skipped_tag", "signature": "static int is_skipped_tag(lxb_tag_id_t t)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 701, "name": "node_tag", "signature": "static lxb_tag_id_t node_tag(const lxb_dom_node_t *n)"}, {"doc": "Nonzero if any ancestor up to base is a non-rendered container. When js_enabled, a <noscript> ancestor also suppresses content (the script would run, so the fallback is hidden); when JS is off, <noscript> content IS rendered (a no-JS * browser shows the fallback).", "kind": "function", "line": 710, "name": "in_skipped_subtree", "signature": "static int in_skipped_subtree(const lxb_dom_node_t *n, const lxb_dom_node_t *base,\n              ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 746, "name": "font_color_attr", "signature": "static int font_color_attr(lxb_dom_element_t *el)"}, {"doc": "Legacy bgcolor attribute (body/table/tr/td), the background twin of <font * color>: pre-CSS sites (Hacker News' orange bar and beige page) still use it.", "kind": "function", "line": 753, "name": "bgcolor_attr", "signature": "static int bgcolor_attr(lxb_dom_element_t *el)"}, {"doc": "Ordinal for a run of container `cid` whose direct-child item is `item` (NULL = * anonymous: every such run is its own item). -1 when the run has no container.", "kind": "function", "line": 805, "name": "item_ordinal", "signature": "static int item_ordinal(pv_item_track *tr, int cid, const lxb_dom_node_t *item)"}, {"doc": "The author text-presentation extensions struct (pv_text_ext) is public now (include/page_view.h): each field resolves from the nearest ancestor that sets it (they inherit in CSS). list_style drives the <li> marker (structural); the * rest are gated by caps.css downstream.", "kind": "function", "line": 827, "name": "pv_text_ext_reset", "signature": "void pv_text_ext_reset(pv_text_ext *e)"}, {"doc": "Merges one ancestor's resolved css_style into ext, nearest ancestor first (a field * already set is not overwritten — keeps the nearest, matching inheritance).", "kind": "function", "line": 847, "name": "pv_text_ext_merge", "signature": "static void pv_text_ext_merge(pv_text_ext *e, const css_style *cs)"}, {"doc": "caret-color: an explicit `auto` (CSS_LEN_AUTO) on a nearer ancestor is a * resolved value -- it stops the walk, and pv_set_text_ext maps it to -1. if (e->caret_color == -1 && cs->caret_color != -1) e->caret_color = cs->caret_color; if (e->object_fit == 0 && cs->object_fit != CSS_OFI_UNSET) e->object_fit = cs->object_fit; } /* True if the resolved style declares any HORIZONTAL box property.", "kind": "function", "line": 886, "name": "css_has_hbox", "signature": "static int css_has_hbox(const css_style *cs)"}, {"doc": "Pre-resolves the horizontal box (px) into a run's wire fields: l/r insets = padding + non-auto margin of each side (clamped >= 0); w = the tightest of * width/max-width (0 = none); center = margin: 0 auto with a width cap.", "kind": "function", "line": 897, "name": "css_hbox_resolve", "signature": "static void css_hbox_resolve(const css_style *cs, pv_box_info *out)"}, {"doc": "A real (non-static) position makes a block box-carrying too, so its position/insets/ * z-index ride the box-def tree (painted for relative; carried for the box engine).", "kind": "function", "line": 925, "name": "css_has_position", "signature": "static int css_has_position(const css_style *cs)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 929, "name": "css_has_boxdeco", "signature": "static int css_has_boxdeco(const css_style *cs)"}, {"doc": "Document-order registry of flex/grid container nodes, so the runs of one * container share a stable id. typedef struct pv_container_reg { const lxb_dom_node_t *node[PV_MAX_CONTAINERS]; size_t count; } pv_container_reg; /* Id of node in reg, registering it on first sight. -1 when reg is full.", "kind": "function", "line": 986, "name": "container_id", "signature": "static int container_id(pv_container_reg *reg, const lxb_dom_node_t *node)"}, {"doc": "Fills *d (decoration + hbox + bg; parent_id defaults to -1) from a resolved style. Padding unset -> 0 (geometry default); the rest keep the css module's sentinels * (PV_LEN_UNSET width/radius/outline width, -1 colors, 0 the rest).", "kind": "function", "line": 1006, "name": "boxdef_from_style", "signature": "static void boxdef_from_style(pv_box_def *d, const css_style *cs)"}, {"doc": "Id of node in the box registry, recording its decoration on first sight. -1 when * full. The parent link is stamped separately as the ancestor walk continues.", "kind": "function", "line": 1117, "name": "box_reg_id", "signature": "static int box_reg_id(pv_box_reg *r, const lxb_dom_node_t *node, const css_style *cs)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1143, "name": "pv_style_cache_init", "signature": "static int pv_style_cache_init(pv_style_cache *c)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1156, "name": "pv_style_cache_free", "signature": "static void pv_style_cache_free(pv_style_cache *c)"}, {"doc": "cch_element_style(el, sheet), memoized in *cache. A NULL cache (OOM at init, or a caller that opts out) simply calls through uncached -- never a hard * failure, matching every other degrade-on-OOM path in this module.", "kind": "function", "line": 1165, "name": "cached_element_style", "signature": "static css_style cached_element_style(lxb_dom_element_t *el, const css_sheet *sheet,\n            ..."}, {"doc": "Maps a css_justify (resolved by the css cascade) to a flex_layout fx_justify. * Unset / start / unknown all fall to FX_JUSTIFY_START (the default).", "kind": "function", "line": 1194, "name": "css_to_fx_justify", "signature": "static int css_to_fx_justify(css_justify j)"}, {"doc": "Inline emphasis carried by a tag: bold from <b>/<strong>/<th>, italic from * <i>/<em>. <th> is a header cell, conventionally bold.", "kind": "function", "line": 1209, "name": "is_bold_tag", "signature": "static int is_bold_tag(lxb_tag_id_t t)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1212, "name": "is_italic_tag", "signature": "static int is_italic_tag(lxb_tag_id_t t)"}, {"doc": "level, nearest block-level ancestor (defaults to base), the inherited author color (nearest ancestor that sets one, packed 0xRRGGBB, or -1), the author background (nearest ancestor that sets one), the inline emphasis (bold/italic), the author text-align (*align, a css_align) and font-size (*font_scale, percent), and the list context: the nearest <li> ancestor (*li, or NULL), the list nesting depth (*list_depth = count of <ul>/<ol> ancestors), and whether the innermost list is ordered (*ordered).  Author presentation now comes from the css module: for each ancestor element its computed css_style (document <style> sheet + that element's inline style=, inline winning) is merged into the inheriting fields, nearest ancestor first. Inline emphasis from tags (<b>/<em>/...) still applies; an explicit CSS font-weight / font-style on the nearest ancestor that sets it takes precedence over the tag * default. The <font color> attribute is a legacy fallback when no CSS color won.", "kind": "function", "line": 1231, "name": "resolve_context", "signature": "static void resolve_context(const lxb_dom_node_t *n, const lxb_dom_node_t *base,\n                ..."}, {"doc": "prev_align_self = cs.align_self; prev_col_span = (cs.grid_col_span > 0) ? cs.grid_col_span : 0; prev_row_span = (cs.grid_row_span > 0) ? cs.grid_row_span : 0; have_prev_el = 1; prev_el = p; } if (p == base) break; } bold = got_css_bold ? css_bold : tag_bold; italic = got_css_italic ? css_italic : tag_italic; } /* Collapses ASCII whitespace runs to a single space into a fresh buffer.", "kind": "function", "line": 1451, "name": "collapse_ws", "signature": "static char *collapse_ws(const char *s, size_t n)"}, {"doc": "Parses the leading non-negative integer of an HTML length attribute value (e.g. \"640\", \"640px\", \"50%\"). Falls back * to 100vw (full viewport) on parse failure — matching browser behavior.", "kind": "function", "line": 1587, "name": "srcset_slot_width", "signature": "static int srcset_slot_width(const lxb_char_t *sizes, size_t slen,\n                              ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1633, "name": "dimensions", "signature": "* viewport dimensions (data: inline detection, <picture> <source> scanning). */\nstatic void srcse..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1664, "name": "find_body", "signature": "static lxb_dom_node_t *find_body(lxb_dom_node_t *root)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1687, "name": "forms_free", "signature": "static void forms_free(form_table *ft)"}, {"doc": "} form_rec; typedef struct form_table { form_rec *recs; size_t    count, cap; } form_table; static void forms_free(form_table *ft) { for (size_t i = 0; i < ft->count; ++i) free(ft->recs[i].action); free(ft->recs); ft->recs = NULL; ft->count = ft->cap = 0; } /* Case-insensitive ASCII equality of a NUL-terminated string against a literal.", "kind": "function", "line": 1695, "name": "ascii_ieq", "signature": "static int ascii_ieq(const char *s, const char *lit)"}, {"doc": "Owned NUL-terminated copy of an attribute value, or NULL when the attribute is * absent. A present-but-empty attribute yields a \"\" string (distinguishable). static char *attr_dup(lxb_dom_element_t *el, const char *name, size_t namelen) { size_t L = 0; const lxb_char_t *v = lxb_dom_element_get_attribute(el, (const lxb_char_t *)name, namelen, &L); if (v == NULL) return NULL; return dup_n((const char *)v, L); } /* Records a <form> element. Returns 0, or -1 on OOM.", "kind": "function", "line": 1716, "name": "forms_add", "signature": "static int forms_add(form_table *ft, const lxb_dom_node_t *node)"}, {"doc": "} lxb_dom_element_t *el = lxb_dom_interface_element((lxb_dom_node_t *)node); char *method = attr_dup(el, \"method\", 6); int m = ascii_ieq(method, \"post\") ? PV_METHOD_POST : PV_METHOD_GET; free(method); form_rec *r = &ft->recs[ft->count++]; r->node = node; r->action = attr_dup(el, \"action\", 6); /* may be NULL (submit to current doc) r->method = m; return 0; } /* Index of the nearest enclosing recorded form, or -1 when the control has none.", "kind": "function", "line": 1736, "name": "form_for", "signature": "static int form_for(const form_table *ft, const lxb_dom_node_t *n,\n                    const lxb_..."}, {"doc": "Nonzero if a descendant text node sits under a non-rendered element (a <style> or <script> nested in the collected subtree -- with run_js the parser keeps <script> nodes in the tree, so a flattened cell or a <button> label would * otherwise paint raw CSS/JS source as content, as google.com's markup showed).", "kind": "function", "line": 1752, "name": "under_unrendered", "signature": "static int under_unrendered(const lxb_dom_node_t *n, const lxb_dom_node_t *el)"}, {"doc": "Concatenates the descendant text of el into an owned NUL-terminated string (the value of a <textarea> / the label of a <button> / a flattened table cell), * skipping text under non-rendered descendants. Never NULL on success.", "kind": "function", "line": 1765, "name": "collect_text", "signature": "static char *collect_text(const lxb_dom_node_t *el)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1788, "name": "classify_input", "signature": "static pv_input_type classify_input(const char *type)"}, {"doc": "1-based position of an <li> among its <li> siblings (an <ol> counter, basic: the `start`/`value` attributes are out of scope). Counts preceding element siblings * that are <li>.", "kind": "function", "line": 1936, "name": "li_ordinal", "signature": "static int li_ordinal(const lxb_dom_node_t *li)"}, {"doc": "int k = 0; if (n < 1) n = 1; while (n > 0 && k < (int)sizeof buf) { int r = (n - 1) % 26; buf[k++] = (char)((upper ? 'A' : 'a') + r); n = (n - 1) / 26; } size_t o = 0; while (k > 0 && o + 1 < cap) out[o++] = buf[--k];     /* reverse if (o + 2 < cap) { out[o++] = '.'; out[o++] = ' '; } if (o < cap) out[o] = '\\0'; } /* Roman-numeral ordinal (1->i, 4->iv, clamped to [1,3999]) as \"iv. \" into out.", "kind": "function", "line": 1962, "name": "roman_marker", "signature": "static void roman_marker(int n, int upper, char *out, size_t cap)"}, {"doc": "Builds the list marker for the first run of an <li>. With no author list-style (CSS_LS_UNSET) it is the UA default: \"N. \" for an ordered list, \"* \" (bullet) otherwise. An author list-style-type selects the glyph/numbering (disc/circle/ square or decimal/alpha/roman). Written into out (size cap); ASCII or short UTF-8, so it is valid UTF-8 and safe to paint. CSS_LS_NONE is handled by the caller (no * marker emitted at all).", "kind": "function", "line": 1987, "name": "list_marker", "signature": "static void list_marker(int ordered, const lxb_dom_node_t *li, int list_style,\n                  ..."}, {"doc": "case CSS_LS_CIRCLE:      snprintf(out, cap, \"\\xE2\\x97\\xA6 \"); return; /* U+25E6 white bullet case CSS_LS_SQUARE:      snprintf(out, cap, \"\\xE2\\x96\\xAA \"); return; /* U+25AA small square case CSS_LS_DECIMAL:     snprintf(out, cap, \"%d. \", ord); return; case CSS_LS_LOWER_ALPHA: alpha_marker(ord, 0, out, cap); return; case CSS_LS_UPPER_ALPHA: alpha_marker(ord, 1, out, cap); return; case CSS_LS_LOWER_ROMAN: roman_marker(ord, 0, out, cap); return; case CSS_LS_UPPER_ROMAN: roman_marker(ord, 1, out, cap); return; default: break;  /* unset: UA default below } if (ordered && li != NULL) snprintf(out, cap, \"%d. \", ord); else                       snprintf(out, cap, \"\\xE2\\x80\\xA2 \"); } /* Nearest <table> ancestor of n (up to base), or NULL.", "kind": "function", "line": 2007, "name": "nearest_table", "signature": "static const lxb_dom_node_t *nearest_table(const lxb_dom_node_t *n, const lxb_dom_node_t *base)"}, {"doc": "Nonzero if cell has a descendant <table>: it is then a structural CONTAINER, not a leaf cell. Only leaf cells (no nested table) are collected as one text run; a container cell is walked so the inner table's cells are collected separately. This is what stops a legacy table-in-table layout (e.g. Hacker News: the story list is a <table> nested inside a <td> of the outer table) from flattening its whole subtree * into one giant run. Early-exit on the first nested table (bounded by the subtree).", "kind": "function", "line": 2032, "name": "cell_has_nested_table", "signature": "static int cell_has_nested_table(const lxb_dom_node_t *cell)"}, {"doc": "Pre-order successor that does NOT descend into n's children (used to skip an * already-decided subtree during the table scan).", "kind": "function", "line": 2041, "name": "next_skip", "signature": "static lxb_dom_node_t *next_skip(lxb_dom_node_t *n, const lxb_dom_node_t *root)"}, {"doc": "First <a href> element in the cell's subtree, with *count receiving how many * such anchors exist, capped at 2 (only none / exactly-one / several matters).", "kind": "function", "line": 2052, "name": "cell_anchors", "signature": "static const lxb_dom_node_t *cell_anchors(const lxb_dom_node_t *cell, int *count)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 2070, "name": "links", "signature": "* its links (the Hacker News case: every story link lives inside a <td>), so the\n * caller flows ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 2101, "name": "flow_table", "signature": "static int flow_table(pv_flow_reg *fr, const lxb_dom_node_t *table)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 2117, "name": "table", "signature": "* FLOW table (multi-link: walked so its links survive) do NOT suppress their\n * content -- their ..."}, {"doc": "Concatenates the text of every <style> element in the document (head included) into one owned, NUL-terminated buffer, capped at PV_MAX_STYLE_BYTES. Returns NULL when there is no <style> (or on OOM, treated by the caller as \"no author CSS\"). * *outlen receives the length.", "kind": "function", "line": 2164, "name": "collect_style_text", "signature": "static char *collect_style_text(lxb_dom_node_t *root, size_t *outlen)"}, {"doc": "Nonzero if n or any ancestor up to base has display:none (from the <style> sheet or its inline style=). display:none is structural visibility, applied regardless of caps.css (hidden content stays hidden, like the JS-off display:none caveat).  When js_enabled == 0 (no JS execution), inline style=\"display:none\" is treated as a presentation hint rather than structural hiding: many sites use JS to toggle tab containers between display:none and display:block, and without JS the page would show no content at all. Stylesheet-level display:none is still honored regardless of JS state — that is an author's structural choice. * js_enabled == 1 means full JS; the page is expected to toggle its own tabs.", "kind": "function", "line": 2205, "name": "in_hidden_subtree", "signature": "static int in_hidden_subtree(const lxb_dom_node_t *n, const lxb_dom_node_t *base,\n               ..."}, {"doc": "Nonzero if n or any ancestor up to base is page boilerplate (<nav>/<header>/ <footer>/<aside>). Used only in distraction-free (reader) mode to drop chrome and * keep the main content. Deterministic, not heuristic article extraction.", "kind": "function", "line": 2235, "name": "in_boilerplate_subtree", "signature": "static int in_boilerplate_subtree(const lxb_dom_node_t *n, const lxb_dom_node_t *base)"}, {"doc": "Nonzero if n or any ancestor up to base is inside a <details> without an `open` attribute and n is NOT inside its <summary> (the summary is always visible). * Used to
