bytes, no DEL. * Space is allowed (local paths may carry it; fields are tab-separated).", "kind": "function", "line": 26, "name": "url_valid", "signature": "static int url_valid(const char *url)"}, {"doc": "Copies src into a fresh buffer, mapping control bytes/DEL to ' ' and truncating to PREFS_MAX_TITLE bytes on a UTF-8 sequence boundary. A NULL * src yields \"\". Returns NULL only on OOM.", "kind": "function", "line": 39, "name": "title_clean", "signature": "static char *title_clean(const char *src)"}, {"doc": "((unsigned char)dst[n - 1 - back] & 0xC0u) == 0x80u) { ++back; } if (back < n) { unsigned char lead = (unsigned char)dst[n - 1 - back]; size_t need = (lead >= 0xF0u) ? 4 : (lead >= 0xE0u) ? 3 : (lead >= 0xC0u) ? 2 : 1; if (need > back + 1) n -= back + 1;  /* incomplete: drop the sequence } dst[n] = '\\0'; return dst; } /* --- lifecycle -------------------------------------------------------------", "kind": "function", "line": 65, "name": "prefs_init", "signature": "void prefs_init(prefs_state *p)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 73, "name": "prefs_free", "signature": "void prefs_free(prefs_state *p)"}, {"doc": "Appends a cleaned bookmark. Duplicate URLs are skipped (PREFS_OK). Used by * the toggle's add path and by the parser.", "kind": "function", "line": 91, "name": "bookmark_push", "signature": "static prefs_status bookmark_push(prefs_state *p, const char *url, const char *title)"}, {"doc": "Appends a history entry at the BACK preserving document order (parser path; * the API path inserts at the front). Duplicates and overflow are skipped.", "kind": "function", "line": 109, "name": "history_push_back", "signature": "static prefs_status history_push_back(prefs_state *p, const char *url)"}, {"doc": "if (p->history_len >= PREFS_MAX_HISTORY) return PREFS_OK; for (size_t i = 0; i < p->history_len; ++i) if (strcmp(p->history[i], url) == 0) return PREFS_OK; char **grown = (char **)realloc(p->history, (p->history_len + 1) * sizeof *grown); if (grown == NULL) return PREFS_ERR_OOM; p->history = grown; char *u = strdup(url); if (u == NULL) return PREFS_ERR_OOM; p->history[p->history_len++] = u; return PREFS_OK; } /* --- public list operations ---------------------------------------------------", "kind": "function", "line": 124, "name": "prefs_bookmark_index", "signature": "int prefs_bookmark_index(const prefs_state *p, const char *url)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 131, "name": "prefs_bookmark_toggle", "signature": "prefs_status prefs_bookmark_toggle(prefs_state *p, const char *url,\n                             ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 151, "name": "prefs_history_add", "signature": "prefs_status prefs_history_add(prefs_state *p, const char *url)"}, {"doc": "p->history_len--; Output is never longer than the * input.", "kind": "function", "line": 25, "name": "utf8_sanitized_dup", "signature": "static char *utf8_sanitized_dup(const char *s)"}, {"doc": "Appends one block, taking owned copies of text (required) and href (optional). * Returns 0 on success, -1 on allocation failure (the doc is left consistent).", "kind": "function", "line": 59, "name": "rd_push", "signature": "static int rd_push(rd_doc *d, rd_kind kind, int heading_level, int block_break,\n                 ..."}, {"doc": "Appends an RD_INPUT block, copying text (placeholder/label), the form action * (href), and the control name/value. Returns 0, or -1 on allocation failure.", "kind": "function", "line": 166, "name": "rd_push_input", "signature": "static int rd_push_input(rd_doc *d, int block_break, const pv_run *r)"}, {"doc": "Resolves raw_src (possibly relative, or a data: URI) against top_level_url and judges it under the exact same policy an <img> already goes through: a data: URI is judged in place (never resolved, never touches the network either way); a file:// page confines a relative source to its own document subtree (no escaping \"../\", no absolute/foreign-scheme read); everything else resolves as https and is judged by rdp_image_decision (caps.images, tracker heuristics, scheme/host checks). resolved must be at least URL_MAX_LEN+1 bytes; *out_url is set to either raw_src or resolved. Shared by the PV_IMAGE run case and the box background-image case below (2026-07-16) so the two can never drift into different gates for what is, from a network-trust standpoint, the identical operation -- a CSS `background-image: url(...)` is not a lesser-trusted or * differently-trusted fetch than an `<img src>`.", "kind": "function", "line": 201, "name": "resolve_image_decision", #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> #define RP_MAX_HOST   255u  /* RFC 1035 maximum host length #define RP_MAX_LABELS 128u  /* far above any real host's label count /* --- helpers ---", "kind": "function", "line": 21, "name": "lower", "signature": "static char lower(char c)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 25, "name": "ci_starts_with", "signature": "static int ci_starts_with(const char *s, const char *prefix)"}, {"doc": "static char lower(char c) { return (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z') ? (char)(c + ('a' - 'A')) : c; } static int ci_starts_with(const char *s, const char *prefix) { while (*prefix) { if (*s == '\\0' || lower(*s) != lower(*prefix)) return 0; ++s; ++prefix; } return 1; } /* --- Public Suffix List lookup (binary search over the generated tables) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 35, "name": "psl_cmp", "signature": "static int psl_cmp(const void *key, const void *elem)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 39, "name": "psl_in", "signature": "static int psl_in(const char *const *arr, size_t n, const char *key)"}, {"doc": "Number of labels of the public suffix (eTLD) of a lowercased host, applying the full PSL algorithm: exception rules win; otherwise the longest matching normal or wildcard rule; otherwise the implicit \"*\" rule (one label). * off[] holds the byte offset of each label start; n is the label count.", "kind": "function", "line": 48, "name": "public_suffix_labels", "signature": "static size_t public_suffix_labels(const char *host, const size_t *off, size_t n)"}, {"doc": "} if (s + 1 < n) { const char *parent = host + off[s + 1]; /* labels[s+1..n-1] if (psl_in(psl_wildcards, psl_wildcards_n, parent)) { if (ncand > best) best = ncand; } } } if (exception >= 0) return (size_t)exception;     /* exceptions prevail return (best >= 1) ? (size_t)best : 1;            /* else longest, or \"*\" } /* --- public ---", "kind": "function", "line": 75, "name": "rp_host_of", "signature": "int rp_host_of(const char *url, char *out, size_t out_size)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 101, "name": "rp_site_of", "signature": "int rp_site_of(const char *host, char *out, size_t out_size)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 133, "name": "rp_same_site", "signature": "int rp_same_site(const char *top_level_url, const char *request_url)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 142, "name": "rp_evaluate", "signature": "rp_decision rp_evaluate(const char *top_level_url, const char *request_url)"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 16, "name": "RP_MAX_HOST"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 18, "name": "RP_MAX_LABELS"}]}, {"id": "src/secure_fetch.c", "kind": "module", "label": "secure_fetch.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "a5adb0a98329d7c1", "symbol_count": 42, "symbols": [{"kind": "struct", "line": 433, "name": "body_sink"}, {"doc": "Snapshot of the negotiated TLS state. curl exposes the live SSL* only while a transfer is in progress (via CURLINFO_TLS_SSL_PTR); after curl_easy_perform returns, internals is NULL. So we capture from inside the header/write callbacks and copy everything into owned storage, since SSL_get0_group_name / * the chain pointers are only valid while the SSL object lives.", "kind": "struct", "line": 446, "name": "tls_capture"}, {"kind": "struct", "line": 456, "name": "fetch_ctx"}, {"doc": "/* --- small helpers (no libc locale dependence) --- static int ci_equal(const char *a, const char *b) { while (*a && *b) { int ca = (unsigned char)*a, cb = (unsigned char)*b; if (ca >= 'A' && ca <= 'Z') ca += 'a' - 'A'; if (cb >= 'A' && cb <= 'Z') cb += 'a' - 'A'; if (ca != cb) return 0; ++a; ++b; } return *a == '\\0' && *b == '\\0'; } /* Case-insensitive: does haystack start with prefix?", "kind": "function", "line": 43, "name": "ci_starts_with", "signature": "static int ci_starts_with(const char *haystack, const char *prefix)"}, {"doc": "/* Case-insensitive: does haystack start with prefix? static int ci_starts_with(const char *haystack, const char *prefix) { while (*prefix) { int ch = (unsigned char)*haystack, cp = (unsigned char)*prefix; if (ch >= 'A' && ch <= 'Z') ch += 'a' - 'A'; if (cp >= 'A' && cp <= 'Z') cp += 'a' - 'A'; if (ch != cp) return 0; ++haystack; ++prefix; } return 1; } /* Case-insensitive index of needle in haystack, or -1.", "kind": "function", "line": 55, "name": "ci_index", "signature": "static long ci_index(const char *haystack, const char *needle)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 66, "name": "sf_share_lock", "signature": "static void sf_share_lock(CURL *handle, curl_lock_data data,\n                          curl_lock_..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 72, "name": "sf_share_unlock", "signature": "static void sf_share_unlock(CURL *handle, curl_lock_data data, void *userptr)"}, {"doc": "static pthread_mutex_t sf_cookie_lock = PTHREAD_MUTEX_INITIALIZER; static void sf_share_lock(CURL *handle, curl_lock_data data, curl_lock_access access, void *userptr) { (void)handle; (void)data; (void)access; (void)userptr; pthread_mutex_lock(&sf_cookie_lock); } static void sf_share_unlock(CURL *handle, curl_lock_data data, void *userptr) { (void)handle; (void)data; (void)userptr; pthread_mutex_unlock(&sf_cookie_lock); } /* --- public: defaults ---", "kind": "function", "line": 79, "name": "sf_global_init", "signature": "void sf_global_init(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 94, "name": "sf_cookie_line_matches", "signature": "int sf_cookie_line_matches(const char *line, const char *host, const char *path,\n                ..."}, {"doc": "Extracts host + path from a validated https url into caller buffers (path defaults * to \"/\"). Returns 0 on success, -1 on failure.", "kind": "function", "line": 150, "name": "sf_url_host_path", "signature": "static int sf_url_host_path(const char *url, char *host, size_t hostsz,\n                         ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 164, "name": "sf_cookie_header_for", "signature": "size_t sf_cookie_header_for(const char *url, char *out, size_t outsz)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 193, "name": "sf_cookie_put", "signature": "void sf_cookie_put(const char *url, const char *namevalue)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 214, "name": "sf_config_default", "signature": "sf_config sf_config_default(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 239, "name": "sf_user_agent_or_default", "signature": "const char *sf_user_agent_or_default(const char *ua)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 243, "name": "sf_impersonate_kex_groups", "signature": "const char *sf_impersonate_kex_groups(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 245, "name": "sf_impersonate_tls13_ciphers", "signature": "const char *sf_impersonate_tls13_ciphers(void)"}, {"doc": "c.progress_cb = NULL; c.range_start = -1; /* -1 means no byte-range c.range_end = -1; return c; } const char *sf_user_agent_or_default(const char *ua) { return (ua != NULL && ua[0] != '\\0') ? ua : SF_DEFAULT_USER_AGENT; } const char *sf_impersonate_kex_groups(void)    { return SF_IMPERSONATE_KEX_GROUPS; } const char *sf_impersonate_tls13_ciphers(void) { return SF_IMPERSONATE_TLS13_CIPHERS; } /* --- public: pure validators ---", "kind": "function", "line": 248, "name": "sf_validate_url", "signature": "sf_status sf_validate_url(const char *url)"}, {"doc": "Nonzero iff url is \"http://host...\" (case-insensitive) with a non-empty host. Plain http is only ever permitted for overlay (.onion/.i2p) realms, gated by * sf_config.allow_overlay_http; the overlay layer provides encryption + authenticity.", "kind": "function", "line": 257, "name": "sf_url_is_http", "signature": "static int sf_url_is_http(const char *url)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 268, "name": "sf_check_tls_version", "signature": "sf_status sf_check_tls_version(const char *negotiated_version)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 273, "name": "sf_check_group_is_pq", "signature": "sf_status sf_check_group_is_pq(const char *negotiated_group)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 282, "name": "sf_check_chain_policy", "signature": "sf_status sf_check_chain_policy(const sf_chain_info *chain, sf_policy policy)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 292, "name": "sf_enforce_policy", "signature": "sf_status sf_enforce_policy(const char *tls_version, const char *group,\n                         ..."}, {"doc": "The navigability fallback accepts a classical KE; every other policy requires * a PQ-hybrid group. The certificate chain is always validated below. /* Allow classical KE when --insecure is used if (policy != SF_POLICY_ALLOW_CLASSICAL_KE && policy != SF_POLICY_ALLOWLISTED_INSECURE) { s = sf_check_group_is_pq(group); if (s != SF_OK) return s; } if (chain == NULL) return SF_ERR_INTERNAL; /* fail closed: chain not inspectable return sf_check_chain_policy(chain, policy); } /* --- bounded string helper (overflow => -1, never truncate) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 323, "name": "copy_checked", "signature": "static int copy_checked(char *dst, size_t dstsz, const char *src)"}, {"doc": "if (chain == NULL) return SF_ERR_INTERNAL; /* fail closed: chain not inspectable return sf_check_chain_policy(chain, policy); } /* --- bounded string helper (overflow => -1, never truncate) --- static int copy_checked(char *dst, size_t dstsz, const char *src) { size_t n = strlen(src); if (n + 1 > dstsz) return -1; memcpy(dst, src, n + 1); return 0; } /* --- public: pure redirect logic ---", "kind": "function", "line": 332, "name": "sf_is_redirect_code", "signature": "int sf_is_redirect_code(long http_code)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 339, "name": "sf_parse_location_header", "signature": "sf_status sf_parse_location_header(const char *header_line, char *out, size_t outsz)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 357, "name": "sf_resolve_redirect", "signature": "sf_status sf_resolve_redirect(const char *base_url, const char *location,\n                       ..."}, {"doc": "Reference resolution + the https-only / no-downgrade policy live in the pure url module (DRY); a redirect is just a reference resolved against the * current URL, fail-closed to an absolute https URL. return (url_resolve_https(base_url, location, out, outsz) == URL_OK) ? SF_OK : SF_ERR_INVALID_URL; } /* Case-insensitive prefix test.", "kind": "function", "line": 370, "name": "sf_ci_prefix", "signature": "static int sf_ci_prefix(const char *s, const char *p)"}, {"doc": "if (sf_ci_prefix(location, \"http://\")) { if (snprintf(hloc, sizeof hloc, \"https://%s\", location + 7) >= (int)sizeof hloc) return SF_ERR_INVALID_URL; } else { if (snprintf(hloc, sizeof hloc, \"%s\", location) >= (int)sizeof hloc) return SF_ERR_INVALID_URL; } if (url_resolve_https(hbase, hloc, tmp, sizeof tmp) != URL_OK) return SF_ERR_INVALID_URL; if (!sf_ci_prefix(tmp, \"https://\")) return SF_ERR_INVALID_URL; if (snprintf(out, outsz, \"http://%s\", tmp + 8) >= (int)outsz) return SF_ERR_INVALID_URL; return SF_OK; } /* --- public: cleanup ---", "kind": "function", "line": 409, "name": "sf_response_free", "signature": "void sf_response_free(sf_response *resp)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 468, "name": "copy_bounded", "signature": "static void copy_bounded(char *dst, size_t dstsz, const char *src)"}, {"doc": "static int inspect_chain(SSL *ssl, sf_chain_info *info, char *sigbuf, size_t sigbuf_len); static void copy_bounded(char *dst, size_t dstsz, const char *src) { if (dstsz == 0) return; dst[0] = '\\0'; if (src == NULL) return; size_t i = 0; for (; src[i] != '\\0' && i + 1 < dstsz; ++i) dst[i] = src[i]; dst[i] = '\\0'; } /* Helper: Extracts the name of the TLS group actually negotiated from OpenSSL", "kind": "function", "line": 480, "name": "get_negotiated_group_name", "signature": "static const char *get_negotiated_group_name(SSL *ssl)"}, {"doc": "first shared group, which corresponds to the negotiated key exchange * group (for both TLS 1.2 ECDHE and TLS 1.3). nid = SSL_get_shared_group(ssl, 0); #endif if (nid == 0) { return NULL; /* There is no negotiated group (e.g., TLS 1.2 without PFS or failure) } /* OBJ_nid2sn returns the short name (e.g., \"X25519\", \"prime256v1\", \"x25519\") return OBJ_nid2sn(nid); individually.", "kind": "function", "line": 483, "name": "budget_remaining_ms", "signature": "static uint64_t budget_remaining_ms(const struct timespec *start, uint64_t budget_ms)"}, {"doc": "Content-Type gate for an external script's response (anti type-confusion, fail closed for real content types): accept a missing/empty type -- classic-script behaviour -- or one containing \"javascript\"/\"ecmascript\"; refuse anything else, * so an HTML error page or a JSON body is never evaluated as script.", "kind": "function", "line": 497, "name": "ctype_is_javascript", "signature": "static int ctype_is_javascript(const char *ctype)"}, {"doc": "Content-Type gate for an external stylesheet (Hito 27), same shape as the script gate: a missing/empty type is accepted, anything else must mention \"css\" -- an * HTML 404 page or a script body is never parsed as a sheet.", "kind": "function", "line": 506, "name": "ctype_is_css", "signature": "static int ctype_is_css(const char *ctype)"}, {"doc": "Freebug note about an external subresource (script/stylesheet) that was not used (skipped or refused). The raw hostile src is bounded by the message * buffer; freebug caps the entry.", "kind": "function", "line": 514, "name": "log_external_skip", "signature": "static void log_external_skip(fb_buffer *log, const char *kind, const char *why,\n                ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 532, "name": "run", "signature": "* already contains a PV_VIDEO run (avoids duplicates on repeated injection).\n * Call after every ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 565, "name": "window", "signature": "* net window (cs->net_active). */\nstatic void child_fetch_stylesheets(child_state *cs)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 609, "name": "child_handle_load", "signature": "static void child_handle_load(int wfd, child_state *cs, const char *html, size_t len,\n           ..."}, {"doc": "Smallest pending JS timer delay (__nextTimerMs), or -1 when JS is absent, the * eval fails, or nothing is pending. Does NOT touch the console transcript.", "kind": "function", "line": 876, "name": "child_next_timer_ms", "signature": "static int32_t child_next_timer_ms(child_state *cs)"}, {"doc": "Fire click handlers for node_id (OP_CLICK) or advance the virtual timer clock (OP_TICK), then re-derive the view so the parent can repaint mutations caused by the handlers. Response format matches the tail of OP_LOAD: [ok:int32] [title_len][title][text_len][text][view][nav_len=''][nav_replace][console] * [next_timer_ms:int32]. Neither produces a navigation.", "kind": "function", "line": 891, "name": "child_handle_mutation", "signature": "static void child_handle_mutation(int wfd, child_state *cs, int is_tick,\n                        ..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 955, "name": "child_handle_click", "signature": "static void child_handle_click(int wfd, child_state *cs, dom_node_id node_id)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 959, "name": "child_handle_tick", "signature": "static void child_handle_tick(int wfd, child_state *cs, int32_t elapsed_ms)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 969, "name": "child_handle_event", "signature": "static void child_handle_event(int wfd, child_state *cs)"}, {"doc": "Handles a mouse DOM event (OP_MOUSE). Reads: node_id:int32, event_type_len:size_t, event_type, client_x:int32, client_y:int32, * button:int32. Fires JS handlers via jd_fire_mouse_event, re-derives the view.", "kind": "function", "line": 1018, "name": "child_handle_mouse", "signature": "static void child_handle_mouse(int wfd, child_state *cs)"}, {"doc": "Fires a submit event on the form enclosing node_id. Walks up the DOM to find the <form> element, dispatches the event, and reports whether preventDefault was called. Response: [TAG_RESULT][ok:int32][prevented:int32]. No view * re-derivation — the submission is the default action, not a repaint.", "kind": "function", "line": 1052, "name": "child_handle_submit", "signature": "static void child_handle_submit(int wfd, child_state *cs, dom_node_id node_id)"}, {"doc": "Response: [ok:int32][is_exception:int32][value_len][value]. ok==0 means a worker-level failure (no page loaded); a JS-level error is ok==1 with the * exception flag set.", "kind": "function", "line": 1086, "name": "child_handle_eval", "signature": "static void child_handle_eval(int wfd, child_state *cs, const char *js, size_t len)"}, {"doc": "Response: [ok:int32] then, when ok, [w:u32][h:u32][stride:u32][len:size_t][data]. Decoding hostile image bytes happens here, inside the confinement; ok==0 means * the bytes were not a decodable PNG/JPEG (no partial pixels are sent).", "kind": "function", "line": 1119, "name": "child_handle_decode_image", "signature": "static void child_handle_decode_image(int wfd, const char *bytes, size_t len)"}, {"doc": "data: URI images: the parent only sliced the base64 payload (pure pointer arithmetic, no interpretation); the base64 DECODE of hostile bytes happens here, confined, same as the image-format decode it feeds into. A malformed payload (defense in depth -- render_policy already validated it at the decision layer) * answers exactly like an undecodable image: ok=0, no placeholder-worthy crash.", "kind": "function", "line": 1141, "name": "child_handle_decode_image_b64", "signature": "static void child_handle_decode_image_b64(int wfd, const char *b64, size_t len)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1152, "name": "gen_session_key", "signature": "static uint64_t gen_session_key(void)"}, {"doc": "The confined request loop. Runs in the re-exec'd worker image (see * tab_worker_dispatch). Never returns to the caller (always _exit).", "kind": "function", "line": 1173, "name": "tab_worker_run", "signature": "static void tab_worker_run(int rfd, int wfd)"}, {"doc": "else /* OP_DECODE_IMAGE_B64 */       child_handle_decode_image_b64(wfd, buf, len); free(buf); free(url); free(cookies); } child_reset_page(&cs); fb_buffer_free(&cs.log); _exit(0); } /* --- worker entry dispatch (the re-exec'd image lands here from main) --- /* Parses one strictly-decimal, non-negative, sanely-bounded fd. Idempotent; * no module-level mutable state of our own (the disposition is process-wide).", "kind": "function", "line": 1365, "name": "ignore_sigpipe", "signature": "static void ignore_sigpipe(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 1371, "name": "tab_refresh_alive", "signature": "static void tab_refresh_alive(tab *t)"}, {"doc": "Read one length-prefixed owned field from the child, capped against * amplification. *out is NUL-terminated.", "kind": "function", "line": 1391, "name": "read_field", "signature": "static int read_field(int fd, char **out, size_t *out_len)"}, {"doc": "Reads a display list serialised by write_view into a fresh pv_view. The run * count and each field are capped against amplification from a hostile child.", "kind": "function", "line": 1407, "name": "read_view", "signature": "static int read_view(int fd, pv_view **out)"}, {"doc": "Reads the console section written by write_console into out (a zero-initialised fb_buffer). "src/tls_impersonate.c", "kind": "module", "label": "tls_impersonate.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "05046deec3553588", "symbol_count": 18, "symbols": [{"doc": "helper. See spec/tls_impersonate.md and include/tls_impersonate.h.  No I/O, no global state. Two pieces: the triple opt-in gate, and a little-endian, length-prefixed, fail-closed serialization of the parent<->helper messages. Every length is validated against a TI_MAX_* bound before allocation, so a hostile or corrupt frame can never drive an over-read or an unbounded allocation.  #include \"tls_impersonate.h\" #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> /* --- the triple opt-in gate ---", "kind": "function", "line": 17, "name": "ti_should_impersonate", "signature": "int ti_should_impersonate(int host_in_allowlist, int host_js_enabled,\n                          i..."}, {"doc": "#include \"tls_impersonate.h\" #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> /* --- the triple opt-in gate --- int ti_should_impersonate(int host_in_allowlist, int host_js_enabled, int host_in_impersonate) { return (host_in_allowlist && host_js_enabled && host_in_impersonate) ? 1 : 0; } /* --- bounded string length (no dependency on strnlen / feature macros) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 24, "name": "bounded_len", "signature": "static size_t bounded_len(const char *s, size_t max)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 34, "name": "put_u8", "signature": "static void put_u8(ti_wr *w, uint8_t v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 39, "name": "put_u32", "signature": "static void put_u32(ti_wr *w, uint32_t v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 47, "name": "put_u64", "signature": "static void put_u64(ti_wr *w, uint64_t v)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 52, "name": "put_blob", "signature": "static void put_blob(ti_wr *w, const uint8_t *b, size_t n)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 64, "name": "get_u8", "signature": "static uint8_t get_u8(ti_rd *r)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 69, "name": "get_u32", "signature": "static uint32_t get_u32(ti_rd *r)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 79, "name": "get_u64", "signature": "static uint64_t get_u64(ti_rd *r)"}, {"doc": "| ((uint32_t)r->p[r->off + 3] << 24); r->off += 4; return v; } static uint64_t get_u64(ti_rd *r) { if (r->err || r->off + 8u > r->len) { r->err = 1; return 0; } uint64_t v = 0; for (int i = 0; i < 8; ++i) v |= (uint64_t)r->p[r->off + i] << (8 * i); r->off += 8; return v; } /* Reads a length-prefixed byte blob (<= cap). n==0 yields (NULL,0).", "kind": "function", "line": 89, "name": "get_bytes", "signature": "static void get_bytes(ti_rd *r, size_t cap, uint8_t **out, size_t *out_len)"}, {"doc": "static void get_bytes(ti_rd *r, size_t cap, uint8_t **out, size_t *out_len) { out = NULL; *out_len = 0; uint32_t n = get_u32(r); if (r->err) return; if ((size_t)n > cap || r->off + (size_t)n > r->len) { r->err = 1; return; } if (n == 0) return; uint8_t *b = malloc(n); if (b == NULL) { r->err = 1; return; } memcpy(b, r->p + r->off, n); r->off += n; out = b; *out_len = n; } /* Reads a length-prefixed string (<= cap chars), NUL-terminates it.", "kind": "function", "line": 103, "name": "get_str", "signature": "static char *get_str(ti_rd *r, size_t cap)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 115, "name": "valid_profile", "signature": "static int valid_profile(int p)"}, {"doc": "if (n == UINT32_MAX) { r->err = 1; return NULL; } char *s = malloc((size_t)n + 1u); if (s == NULL) { r->err = 1; return NULL; } if (n != 0) memcpy(s, r->p + r->off, n); s[n] = '\\0'; r->off += n; return s; } static int valid_profile(int p) { return p == TI_PROFILE_CHROME_CLASSIC || p == TI_PROFILE_CHROME_MLKEM; Fails closed: a non-https result is never produced; every assembly is bounded and reports overflow rather than truncate.  #define _POSIX_C_SOURCE 200809L #include \"url.h\" #include <stdlib.h> #include <string.h> /* --- small ASCII helpers (no locale dependence) --- /* Case-insensitive: does haystack start with prefix?", "kind": "function", "line": 19, "name": "ci_prefix", "signature": "static int ci_prefix(const char *haystack, const char *prefix)"}, {"doc": "/* Case-insensitive: does haystack start with prefix? static int ci_prefix(const char *haystack, const char *prefix) { while (*prefix) { int ch = (unsigned char)*haystack, cp = (unsigned char)*prefix; if (ch >= 'A' && ch <= 'Z') ch += 'a' - 'A'; if (cp >= 'A' && cp <= 'Z') cp += 'a' - 'A'; if (ch != cp) return 0; ++haystack; ++prefix; } return 1; } /* Bounded copy: out gets src (NUL-terminated). Nonzero on overflow.", "kind": "function", "line": 31, "name": "copy_checked", "signature": "static int copy_checked(char *out, size_t outsz, const char *src)"}, {"doc": "+haystack; ++prefix; } return 1; } /* Bounded copy: out gets src (NUL-terminated). Nonzero on overflow. static int copy_checked(char *out, size_t outsz, const char *src) { size_t n = strlen(src); if (n + 1 > outsz) return -1; memcpy(out, src, n + 1); return 0; } /* Bounded append: out += src. Nonzero on overflow.", "kind": "function", "line": 39, "name": "cat_checked", "signature": "static int cat_checked(char *out, size_t outsz, const char *src)"}, {"doc": "memcpy(out, src, n + 1); return 0; } /* Bounded append: out += src. Nonzero on overflow. static int cat_checked(char *out, size_t outsz, const char *src) { size_t cur = strlen(out); size_t n = strlen(src); if (cur + n + 1 > outsz) return -1; memcpy(out + cur, src, n + 1); return 0; } /* Bounded append of the first n bytes of src. Nonzero on overflow.", "kind": "function", "line": 48, "name": "ncat_checked", "signature": "static int ncat_checked(char *out, size_t outsz, const char *src, size_t n)"}, {"doc": "memcpy(out + cur, src, n + 1); return 0; } /* Bounded append of the first n bytes of src. Nonzero on overflow. static int ncat_checked(char *out, size_t outsz, const char *src, size_t n) { size_t cur = strlen(out); if (cur + n + 1 > outsz) return -1; memcpy(out + cur, src, n); out[cur + n] = '\\0'; return 0; } /* --- public: scheme / validation ---", "kind": "function", "line": 57, "name": "url_has_scheme", "signature": "int url_has_scheme(const char *s)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 71, "name": "url_is_https", "signature": "int url_is_https(const char *s)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 78, "name": "url_validate_https", "signature": "url_status url_validate_https(const char *url)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 89, "name": "url_authority_len", "signature": "size_t url_authority_len(const char *url)"}, {"doc": "while (i <= URL_MAX_LEN && url[i] != '\\0') ++i; if (i > URL_MAX_LEN) return URL_ERR_NOT_HTTPS; return url_is_https(url) ? URL_OK : URL_ERR_NOT_HTTPS; } size_t url_authority_len(const char *url) { size_t i = 8; /* strlen(\"https://\"); url assumed validated to start with it while (url[i] != '\\0' && url[i] != '/') ++i; return i; } /* --- public: dot-segment removal (RFC 3986 section 5.2.4) --- /* Removes the last path segment (and its preceding '/') from out[0..*olen).", "kind": "function", "line": 99, "name": "out_pop_segment", "signature": "static void out_pop_segment(char *out, size_t *olen)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 105, "name": "url_remove_dot_segments", "signature": "url_status url_remove_dot_segments(const char *path, char *out, size_t outsz)"}, {"doc": "Length of base up to and including the last path '/', ignoring query/fragment. * If the path carries no slash, returns the authority length.", "kind": "function", "line": 156, "name": "dir_len", "signature": "static size_t dir_len(const char *base)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 166, "name": "url_resolve_https", "signature": "url_status url_resolve_https(const char *base, const char *ref,\n                             char..."}, {"doc": "char norm[URL_MAX_LEN + 1]; url_status ns = url_remove_dot_segments(merged, norm, sizeof norm); if (ns != URL_OK) return ns; out[0] = '\\0'; if (ncat_checked(out, outsz, base, auth) != 0) return URL_ERR_OVERFLOW; if (cat_checked(out, outsz, norm) != 0) return URL_ERR_OVERFLOW; } return url_validate_https(out); } /* --- omnibox ---", "kind": "function", "line": 214, "name": "is_space", "signature": "static int is_space(int c)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 218, "name": "is_unreserved", "signature": "static int is_unreserved(int c)"}, {"doc": "Percent-encodes src into a query value appended to out (space -> '+', every * non-unreserved byte -> %XX).      /* same layer -> z asc cx_item e = mk(CX_LAYER_ZERO_Z, 0, 0, 3, 0); cx_item f = mk(CX_LAYER_ZERO_Z, 0, 1, 8, 0);        /* auto == 0 -> tie on z assert_true(cx_item_compare(&e, &f) < 0); "signature": "static void test_inline_text_decoration(void **state)"}, {"doc": "/* none is an explicit value (0), distinct from unset (-1). assert_int_equal(css_parse_inline(\"text-decoration:none\", 0).text_decoration, 0); /* Combined keywords OR together; CSS3 style/color/thickness tokens are ignored. assert_int_equal(css_parse_inline(\"text-decoration:underline overline\", 0).text_decoration, CSS_DECO_UNDERLINE | CSS_DECO_OVERLINE); assert_int_equal(css_parse_inline(\"text-decoration:underline wavy red 2px\", 0).text_decoration, CSS_DECO_UNDERLINE); /* A value with no line keyword at all (only a color) is dropped -> unset. assert_int_equal(css_parse_inline(\"text-decoration:red\", 0).text_decoration, -1); /* An unrelated property leaves it unset. assert_int_equal(css_parse_inline(\"color:red\", 0).text_decoration, -1); } /* --- text-presentation extensions (Hito 23b-6) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 103, "name": "test_font_family", 0).display, CSS_DISP_NONE); assert_int_equal(css_parse_inline(\"display:flex\", 0).display, CSS_DISP_FLEX); assert_int_equal(css_parse_inline(\"display:grid\", 0).display, CSS_DISP_GRID); assert_int_equal(css_parse_inline(\"display:block\", 0).display, CSS_DISP_BLOCK); assert_int_equal(css_parse_inline(\"color:red\", 0).display, CSS_DISP_UNSET); } /* --- flex/grid container props (Hito 0); assert_int_equal(f.display, CSS_DISP_FLEX); assert_int_equal(f.gap, 16); assert_int_equal(f.justify, CSS_JUSTIFY_SPACE_BETWEEN); css_style g = css_resolve(sh, \"section\", \"g\", NULL, 0, NULL, 0); assert_int_equal(g.display, CSS_DISP_GRID); assert_int_equal(g.grid_cols, 4); css_free(sh); } /* The container props go through the full cascade: inline beats the sheet.", "kind": "function", test_container_unset(void **state) { (void)state; css_style s = css_parse_inline(\"color:red\", 0); assert_int_equal(s.gap, -1); assert_int_equal(s.justify, CSS_JUSTIFY_UNSET); assert_int_equal(s.grid_cols, 0); } /* --- security: never phone home, ignore junk ---", "kind": "function", "line": 431, "name": "test_url_value_dropped", "signature": "static void test_url_value_dropped(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 441, "name": "test_unknown_props_ignored", "signature": "static void test_unknown_props_ignored(void **state)"}, {"doc": "/* A bare url() with no colour still yields -1. assert_int_equal(css_parse_inline(\"background: url(http://evil/x.png)\", 0).background, -1); /* A plain color background is fine. assert_int_equal(css_parse_inline(\"background:#112233\", 0).background, 0x112233); } static void test_unknown_props_ignored(void **state) { (void)state; css_style s = css_parse_inline(\"position:absolute; z-index:9; color:#abcdef; --x:1\", 0); assert_int_equal(s.color, 0xabcdef); assert_int_equal(s.text_align, CSS_ALIGN_UNSET); } /* --- linear-gradient backgrounds (2026-07-11) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 450, "name": "test_linear_gradient_basic", "signature": "static void test_linear_gradient_basic(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 461, lower-tier gradient (shorthand reset) const char *flat[] = { \"flat\" }; css_style a = css_resolve(sh, \"div\", NULL, flat, 1, NULL, 0); assert_int_equal(a.background, 0x333333); assert_int_equal(a.bg_grad_n, 0); /* and a higher-specificity gradient CLEARS a lower-tier color css_style b = css_resolve(sh, \"div\", \"grad\", NULL, 0, NULL, 0); assert_int_equal(b.bg_grad_n, 2); assert_int_equal(b.background, -1); css_free(sh); } /* --- background-image: url(...) = tag; e.id = id; e.classes = classes; e.nclasses = nc; e.attrs = NULL; e.nattrs = 0; e.parent = parent; e.nth = 0; e.nsib = 0; e.prev = NULL;   /* sibling context unknown by default e.nth_of_type = el_node(tag, NULL, NULL, 0, parent); e.nth = nth; e.nsib = nsib; e.prev = prev; e.nth_of_type = 0; e.nsib_of_type = 0; e.child_count = -1; return e; } /* R2: like el_sib_node but also sets the of-type 1000px) { div { color:#333333 } }\", 0, &sh), CSS_OK); /* 1920 >= 600 applies; 1920 >= 3000 does not; so p stays #111111. assert_int_equal(css_resolve(sh, \"p\", NULL, NULL, 0, NULL, 0).color, 16px serif\", 0); assert_int_equal(s.font_variant, CSS_FV_SMALL_CAPS); assert_int_equal(s.font_family, CSS_FF_SERIF); /* size + family are both required; system keywords drop the shorthand {"doc": "static void test_float_pack_wrap_full_width_stack(void **state) { (void)state; double w[3] = { 998.0, 998.0, 998.0 }; int side[3] = { 0, 0, 0 }; double x[3]; size_t row[3]; assert_int_equal(fx_float_pack_wrap(w, side, 3, 1000.0, 0, x, row), FX_OK); for (size_t i = 0; i < 3; ++i) { assert_int_equal((int)row[i], (int)i); assert_true(dbl_eq(x[i], 0.0)); } } /* When everything fits in one row the wrap variant must agree with v1.", "kind": "function", "line": 357, "name": "test_float_pack_wrap_fits_matches_v1", "signature": "static void {"id": "tests/test_freedom.c", "kind": "module", "label": "test_freedom.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "75b11ae95b877423", "symbol_count": 33, "symbols": [{"doc": "define FREEDOM_BIN \"./build/freedom\" define OUT_FILE    \"__freedom_test_out.txt\" define ERR_FILE    \"__freedom_test_err.txt\"", "kind": "function", "line": 29, "name": "run_freedom", "signature": "static int run_freedom(const char *arg, char *out, size_t out_size, int *exit_status)"}, {"doc": "Runs the binary with a raw argument string (no implicit --headless), capturing * stdout into OUT_FILE. Used by the --download-pdf tests, which set their own mode.", "kind": "function", "line": 52, "name": "run_freedom_raw", "signature": "static int run_freedom_raw(const char *args, int *exit_status)"}, {"doc": "Runs the binary with a raw argument string (no implicit --headless), capturing * stdout into OUT_FILE. Used by the --download-pdf tests, which set their own mode. static int run_freedom_raw(const char *args, int *exit_status) { char cmd[2048]; int n = snprintf(cmd, sizeof cmd, \"%s %s >\" OUT_FILE \" 2>\" ERR_FILE, FREEDOM_BIN, (args != NULL) ? args : \"\"); if (n < 0 || (size_t)n >= sizeof cmd) return -1; int rc = system(cmd); if (rc == -1) return -1; exit_status = WEXITSTATUS(rc); return 0; } /* True if path exists and its first bytes are the PDF magic \"%PDF\".", "kind": "function", "line": 64, "name": "is_pdf_file", "signature": "static int is_pdf_file(const char *path)"}, {"doc": "return 0; } /* True if path exists and its first bytes are the PDF magic \"%PDF\". static int is_pdf_file(const char *path) { FILE *f = fopen(path, \"rb\"); if (f == NULL) return 0; char magic[5] = {0}; size_t got = fread(magic, 1, 4, f); fclose(f); return got == 4 && memcmp(magic, \"%PDF\", 4) == 0; } /* True if path exists and its first bytes are the 8-byte PNG signature.", "kind": "function", "line": 74, "name": "is_png_file", "signature": "static int is_png_file(const char *path)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 83, "name": "cleanup_files", "signature": "static void cleanup_files(void)"}, {"doc": "if (f == NULL) return 0; unsigned char magic[8] = {0}; size_t got = fread(magic, 1, 8, f); fclose(f); static const unsigned char png_sig[8] = {0x89,'P','N','G',0x0D,0x0A,0x1A,0x0A}; return got == 8 && memcmp(magic, png_sig, 8) == 0; } static void cleanup_files(void) { (void)unlink(OUT_FILE); (void)unlink(ERR_FILE); } /* Reads a whole file into a malloc'd buffer (caller frees). NULL on any failure.", "kind": "function", "line": 90, "name": "read_file_all", "signature": "static uint8_t *read_file_all(const char *path, size_t *out_len)"}, {"doc": "if (f == NULL) return NULL; if (fseek(f, 0, SEEK_END) != 0) { fclose(f); return NULL; } long sz = ftell(f); if (sz < 0 || fseek(f, 0, SEEK_SET) != 0) { fclose(f); return NULL; } uint8_t *buf = (uint8_t *)malloc((size_t)sz); if (buf == NULL) { fclose(f); return NULL; } size_t got = fread(buf, 1, (size_t)sz, f); fclose(f); if (got != (size_t)sz) { free(buf); return NULL; } out_len = (size_t)sz; return buf; } /* --- argument handling ---", "kind": "function", "line": 106, "name": "test_help", "signature": "static void test_help(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 115, "name": "test_version", "signature": "static void test_version(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 124, "name": "test_no_args", "signature": "static void test_no_args(void **state)"}, {"doc": "assert_int_equal(run_freedom(\"--version\", out, sizeof out, &rc), 0); assert_int_equal(rc, 0); assert_non_null(strstr(out, \"Freedom\")); } static void test_no_args(void **state) { (void)state; char out[512]; int rc; assert_int_equal(run_freedom(NULL, out, sizeof out, &rc), 0); assert_int_equal(rc, 2); } /* --- local file rendering ---", "kind": "function", "line": 134, "name": "test_local_html", "signature": "static void test_local_html(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 157, "name": "test_local_form_renders_inputs", "signature": "static void test_local_form_renders_inputs(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 183, "name": "test_missing_file", "signature": "static void test_missing_file(void **state)"}, {"doc": "assert_null(strstr(out, \"[hidden\"));              /* ...nor is the hidden control unlink(path); } static void test_missing_file(void **state) { (void)state; char out[512]; int rc; assert_int_equal(run_freedom(\"__freedom_missing_file.html\", out, sizeof out, &rc), 0); assert_int_equal(rc, 1); } /* --- headless PDF export (--download-pdf, visual-review tooling) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 193, "name": "test_download_pdf_local", "signature": "static void test_download_pdf_local(void **state)"}, {"doc": "char args[512]; assert_true((size_t)snprintf(args, sizeof args, \"--download-pdf=%s %s\", pdf, path) < sizeof args); int rc = -1; assert_int_equal(run_freedom_raw(args, &rc), 0); assert_int_equal(rc, 0); assert_true(is_pdf_file(pdf)); /* a real vector PDF, not a stub unlink(path); unlink(pdf); } /* --download-pdf with no PATH is a usage error (fail closed: never guess a path).", "kind": "function", "line": 220, "name": "test_download_pdf_requires_path", "signature": "static void test_download_pdf_requires_path(void **state)"}, {"doc": "unlink(path); unlink(pdf); } /* --download-pdf with no PATH is a usage error (fail closed: never guess a path). static void test_download_pdf_requires_path(void **state) { (void)state; int rc = -1; assert_int_equal(run_freedom_raw(\"--download-pdf examples/sample.html\", &rc), 0); assert_int_equal(rc, 2); } /* --- headless PNG export (--download-png, visual-review tooling) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 228, "name": "test_download_png_local", "signature": "static void test_download_png_local(void **state)"}, {"doc": "-download-png --images decodes the page's local images through the confined worker and paints the real bitmap instead of the \"image (allowed)\" placeholder. Exercises the headless image pipeline end-to-end (render_doc_images -> load_images -> local disk read -> worker decode -> Cairo surface -> free_images); under `make asan` the asan-instrumented freedom subprocess turns any leak/UAF in that path into a failure here. The declared 120x80 clears the tracking-pixel gate so the image is ALLOWed and * actually decoded; the visual-review PNG confirms the pixels (see CLAUDE.md).", "kind": "function", "line": 261, "name": "test_download_png_images_local", "signature": "static void test_download_png_images_local(void **state)"}, {"doc": "char args[512]; assert_true((size_t)snprintf(args, sizeof args, \"--images --download-png=%s %s\", png, html_path) < sizeof args); int rc = -1; assert_int_equal(run_freedom_raw(args, &rc), 0); assert_int_equal(rc, 0); assert_true(is_png_file(png)); /* real bitmap, image decoded (not a placeholder-only render) unlink(img); unlink(html_path); unlink(png); } /* --download-png with no PATH is a usage error (fail closed: never guess a path).", "kind": "function", "line": 322, "name": "test_download_png_requires_path", "signature": "static void test_download_png_requires_path(void **state)"}, {"doc": "Regression for the M1.1-adjacent paint-order fix in write_doc_png/paint_structured (gui/browser_ui.c, paint_positioned_one): CSS 2.1 Appendix E puts a negative z-index stacking context in a paint-order layer that precedes in-flow content, so it must paint BEHIND, not on top of, an overlapping in-flow box. Before the fix, Stage 2's two passes both ran after the in-flow paint, so #front (in-flow, blue, no explicit position) never covered #behind (position:absolute; z-index:-1; red) even though #behind is meant to sit underneath it -- the sampled pixel came back pure red. Decodes the exported PNG (via image_decode, the project's own PNG * decoder) and asserts the overlap pixel is blue, not red.", "kind": "function", "line": 338, "name": "test_download_png_negative_zindex_paints_behind_inflow", "signature": "static void test_download_png_negative_zindex_paints_behind_inflow(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 393, "name": "blend", "signature": "* not some other blend (double-composited or wrong alpha). */\nstatic void test_download_png_group..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 507, "name": "blend", "signature": "* visibly different from either input color or an OVER blend (which would show\n * opaque blue). E..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 621, "name": "markup", "signature": "* against an unrotated control render of the identical markup (a 50-char-wide box\n * at x:[24,975..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 680, "name": "markup", "signature": "* unscaled control render of the identical markup (box y:[24,49] at x=500,\n * center y~36.5): y=2..."}, {"doc": "uint8_t br = (uint8_t)(below >> 16), bg = (uint8_t)(below >> 8), bb = (uint8_t)below; uint8_t mr = (uint8_t)(middle >> 16), mg = (uint8_t)(middle >> 8), mb = (uint8_t)middle; assert_true(ar <= 5 && ag <= 5 && ab >= 250); assert_true(br <= 5 && bg <= 5 && bb >= 250); assert_true(mr <= 5 && mg <= 5 && mb >= 250); img_pixels_free(&px); unlink(path); unlink(png); } /* --- headless console (Freebug --dump-console) --- /* --dump-console runs the page's JS and prints console.* output + uncaught errors.", "kind": "function", "line": 739, "name": "test_dump_console_shows_output_and_error", "signature": "static void test_dump_console_shows_output_and_error(void **state)"}, {"doc": "size_t got = fread(out, 1, sizeof out - 1, o); out[got] = '\\0'; fclose(o); assert_non_null(strstr(out, \"Freebug console\")); assert_non_null(strstr(out, \"[log] LOGMARK 2\"));        /* console.log + computed arg assert_non_null(strstr(out, \"[warn] WARNMARK\"));         /* console.warn assert_non_null(strstr(out, \"[error] \"));               /* uncaught ReferenceError assert_non_null(strstr(out, \"boomUndefined\"));           /* error names the missing fn unlink(path); } /* Plain headless (no --dump-console) does not run JS and prints no console section.", "kind": "function", "line": 774, "name": "test_no_dump_console_without_flag", "signature": "static void test_no_dump_console_without_flag(void **state)"}, {"doc": "-dump-dom prints the agent-readable render tree (header + per-block lines) instead * of the normal text render, and does not run JS.", "kind": "function", "line": 799, "name": wrapper re-opened per child) and the LAST wrapper piece\n * became the contain..."}, {"doc": "The relative panel is in flow: at least one box, and no positioned box left it * at the page bottom (the grey-stripe bug had npositioned pushing it away). assert_non_null(strstr(out, \"nbox=1\")); assert_non_null(strstr(out, \"npositioned=0\")); unlink(path); } /* --- network policy ---", "kind": rounding assert_true(r < 30);               /* no red component — pure greenish-black assert_true(b < 30);               /* no blue component img_pixels_free(&px); unlink(path); unlink(png); } /* --- absent/empty or a JavaScript MIME type. Fail closed: only a recognized JS type survives.", "kind": "function", "line": 227, "name": "test_extract_script_list_skips_non_js_type", "signature": "static void assert_string_equal(s[2].text, \"var c=3;\"); hp_free_scripts(s, n); hp_document_free(doc); } /* No inline scripts => NULL list and zero count (never a bogus empty buffer).", "kind": "function", (skipped, fail-closed); a rel merely CONTAINING the word inside a longer token does not * match.", "kind": "function", "line": 330, "name": "test_extract_stylesheets_rel_tokens", "signature": "static long)n, 0UL); hp_document_free(doc); n = 123; assert_null(hp_extract_stylesheet_hrefs(NULL, &n)); assert_int_equal((unsigned long)n, 0UL); hp_free_stylesheet_hrefs(NULL, 0); } /* Fail-closed anti-DoS: Only for relative URLs starting with '/'.", "kind": "function", "line": 1335, "name": "test_video_shim_relative_url_resolved", "signature": "static void test_video_shim_relative_url_resolved(void **state)"}, {"doc": "assert_int_equal(js_eval(f->ctx, \"var video_data = '<iframe src=\\\"/jkplayer/umv?e=abc\\\"></iframe>';\", strlen(\"var video_data = '<iframe src=\\\"/jkplayer/umv?e=abc\\\"></iframe>';\"), &r), JS_OK); js_result_free(&r); assert_int_equal(jd_inject_video_shim(f->ctx), JD_OK); dom_node_id ids[2]; assert_int_equal(dom_get_by_tag(f->idx, \"iframe\", ids, 2), 1); size_t slen = 0; const char *src = dom_get_attribute(f->idx, ids[0], \"src\", &slen); assert_non_null(src); assert_string_equal(src, \"https://anime.test/jkplayer/umv?e=abc\"); } /* `video[1]` is empty string: no iframe created.", "kind": "function", "line": 1353, "name": "test_video_shim_empty_html", "signature": "static void test_video_shim_empty_html(void **state)"}, {"doc": "/* `video[1]` is empty string: no iframe created. static void test_video_shim_empty_html(void **state) { fixture *f = (fixture *)*state; js_result r; assert_int_equal(js_eval(f->ctx, \"var video = []; video[1] = '';\", strlen(\"var video = []; video[1] = '';\"), &r), JS_OK); js_result_free(&r); assert_int_equal(jd_inject_video_shim(f->ctx), JD_OK); assert_int_equal(dom_get_by_tag(f->idx, \"iframe\", NULL, 0), 0); } /* NULL context returns JD_ERR_NULL_ARG.", "kind": "function", "line": 1365, "name": "test_video_shim_null_ctx", "signature": "static void test_video_shim_null_ctx(void **state)"}, {"doc": "video_data with variable-to-variable assignment: video_data = video[N]. * The C extractor falls back to the video[N] pattern for the URL.", "kind": "function", "line": 1397, "name": "test_video_from_scripts_video_data_is_variable", "signature": "static void test_video_from_scripts_video_data_is_variable(void **state)"}, {"doc": "video_data = video[1] is not a string literal, but the video[N] scan * finds video[1]. Should create 1 iframe (best candidate). assert_int_equal(n, 1); } /* jkanime-style with root-relative URLs that get resolved.", "kind": "function", "line": 1412, "name": "test_video_from_scripts_relative_resolved", "signature": "static void test_video_from_scripts_relative_resolved(void **state)"}, {"doc": "const char *texts[] = {script}; size_t lens[] = {strlen(script)}; size_t n = jd_video_from_scripts(f->idx, texts, lens, 1, \"https://anime.test/dragon-ball/1\"); assert_int_equal(n, 1); dom_node_id ids[2]; assert_int_equal(dom_get_by_tag(f->idx, \"iframe\", ids, 2), 1); size_t sl = 0; const char *src = dom_get_attribute(f->idx, ids[0], \"src\", &sl); assert_non_null(src); assert_string_equal(src, \"https://anime.test/jkplayer/umv?e=ABC\"); } /* video_data = '<iframe ...>' string literal (jkanime's pattern for some pages).", "kind": "function", "line": 1430, "name": "test_video_from_scripts_video_data_string", "signature": "static void test_video_from_scripts_video_data_string(void **state)"}, {"doc": "\"var video_data = '<iframe src=\\\"https://data.example/vid\\\"></iframe>';\"; size_t sl = 0; const char *src = dom_get_attribute(f->idx, ids[0], \"src\", &sl); assert_non_null(src); assert_string_equal(src, \"https://data.example/vid\"); } /* No video data at all: should create zero iframes.", "kind": "function", "line": 1447, "name": "test_video_from_scripts_no_video", "signature": "static void test_video_from_scripts_no_video(void **state)"}, {"doc": "} /* No video data at all: should create zero iframes. static void test_video_from_scripts_no_video(void **state) { fixture *f = (fixture *)*state; assert_string_equal(R.target, \"https://b.example/path\"); } /* --- local file base ---", "kind": "function", "line": 124, "name": "test_file_relative", "signature": "static void test_file_relative(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 132, "name": "test_file_parent", "signature": "static void test_file_parent(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 140, "name": "test_file_absolute_path", "signature": "static void test_file_absolute_path(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 148, "name": "test_file_drops_fragment", "signature": "static void test_file_drops_fragment(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 156, "name": "test_file_base_to_https", "signature": "static void test_file_base_to_https(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 164, "name": "test_file_base_blocks_schemes_and_scheme_relative", "signature": "static void test_file_base_blocks_schemes_and_scheme_relative(void **state)"}, {"doc": "static void test_file_base_blocks_schemes_and_scheme_relative(void **state) { (void)state; assert_int_equal(ln_resolve(\"/home/u/a.html\", \"http://h.example/\", &R), LN_OK); assert_int_equal(R.action, LN_BLOCKED); assert_int_equal(ln_resolve(\"/home/u/a.html\", \"javascript:alert(1)\", &R), LN_OK); assert_int_equal(R.action, LN_BLOCKED); assert_int_equal(ln_resolve(\"/home/u/a.html\", \"//h.example/x\", &R), LN_OK); assert_int_equal(R.action, LN_BLOCKED); } /* --- no base ---", "kind": "function", "line": 178, "name": "test_no_base", "signature": "static void test_no_base(void **state)"}, {"doc": "(void)state; assert_int_equal(ln_resolve(NULL, \"relative/path\", &R), LN_OK); assert_int_equal(R.action, LN_BLOCKED); assert_int_equal(ln_resolve(NULL, \"http://h.example/\", &R), LN_OK); assert_int_equal(R.action, LN_BLOCKED); } /* --- overflow fails closed ---", "kind": "function", "line": 194, "name": "test_overflow_blocked", "signature": "static void test_overflow_blocked(void **state)"}, {"doc": "/* --- overflow fails closed --- static void test_overflow_blocked(void **state) { (void)state; assert_int_equal(R.action, LN_BLOCKED); } /* --- block reasons (for the precise UI notice) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 206, "name": "test_block_reasons", "signature": "static void test_block_reasons(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 233, "name": "test_block_reason_text", "signature": "static void test_block_reason_text(void **state)"}, {"doc": "assert_int_equal(ln_resolve(\"https://h.example/\", \"/x\", &R), LN_OK); assert_int_equal(R.action, LN_NAVIGATE); assert_int_equal(R.reason, LN_BLOCK_NONE); } static void test_block_reason_text(void **state) { (void)state; assert_string_equal(ln_block_reason_text(LN_BLOCK_DOWNGRADE), \"blocked: insecure http link\"); assert_non_null(ln_block_reason_text(LN_BLOCK_FOREIGN_SCHEME)); assert_non_null(ln_block_reason_text(LN_BLOCK_NO_BASE)); assert_non_null(ln_block_reason_text((ln_block_reason)999)); /* unknown is safe } /* --- fragment capture (for a later anchor scroll) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 243, "name": "test_fragment_capture", "signature": "static void test_fragment_capture(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 272, "name": "main", "signature": "int main(void)"}]}, {"id": "tests/test_local_store.c", "kind": "module", "label": "test_local_store.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "6c3673e827c12ea0", "symbol_count": 17, "symbols": [{"doc": "#include <cmocka.h> #include \"local_store.h\" static const uint8_t KEY[LS_KEY_LEN] = { 0x00, 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04, 0x05, 0x06, 0x07, 0x08, 0x09, 0x0a, 0x0b, 0x0c, 0x0d, 0x0e, 0x0f, 0x10, 0x11, 0x12, 0x13, 0x14, 0x15, 0x16, 0x17, 0x18, 0x19, 0x1a, 0x1b, 0x1c, 0x1d, 0x1e, 0x1f, }; static const char PT[] = \"freedom: secret bookmark https://example.onion\"; /* --- raw-key round trip, both AEADs ---", "kind": "function", "line": 29, "name": "roundtrip_raw", "signature": "static void roundtrip_raw(ls_aead aead)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 47, "name": "test_roundtrip_aes", "signature": "static void test_roundtrip_aes(void **s)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 49, "name": "test_roundtrip_chacha", "signature": "static void test_roundtrip_chacha(void **s)"}, {"doc": "assert_int_equal(blob_len, LS_OVERHEAD + (sizeof PT - 1)); assert_int_equal(ls_open(KEY, blob, blob_len, &out, &out_len), LS_OK); assert_int_equal(out_len, sizeof PT - 1); assert_memory_equal(out, PT, sizeof PT - 1); ls_free(blob, blob_len); ls_free(out, out_len); } static void test_roundtrip_aes(void **s) { (void)s; roundtrip_raw(LS_AEAD_AES256_GCM); } static void test_roundtrip_chacha(void **s) { (void)s; roundtrip_raw(LS_AEAD_CHACHA20_POLY1305); } /* --- empty plaintext ---", "kind": "function", "line": 52, "name": "test_empty_plaintext", "signature": "static void test_empty_plaintext(void **s)"}, {"doc": "static void test_empty_plaintext(void **s) { (void)s; assert_int_equal(out_len, 0); ls_free(blob, blob_len); ls_free(out, out_len); } /* --- wrong key fails closed ---", "kind": "function", "line": 66, "name": "test_wrong_key", "signature": "static void test_wrong_key(void **s)"}, {"doc": "size_t blob_len = 0, out_len = 1; assert_null(out); /* no plaintext released ls_free(blob, blob_len); } /* --- tampering any region fails closed ---", "kind": "function", "line": 84, "name": "tamper_at", "signature": "static void tamper_at(size_t off)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 98, "name": "test_tamper_ciphertext", "signature": "static void test_tamper_ciphertext(void **s)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 100, "name": "test_tamper_tag", "signature": "static void test_tamper_tag(void **s)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 101, "name": "test_tamper_nonce", "signature": "static void test_tamper_nonce(void **s)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 102, "name": "test_tamper_salt", "signature": "static void test_tamper_salt(void **s)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 103, "name": "test_tamper_aead_id", "signature": "static void test_tamper_aead_id(void **s)"}, {"doc": "blob[off] ^= 0x01; ls_status st = ls_open(KEY, blob, blob_len, &out, &out_len); assert_true(st == LS_ERR_AUTH || st == LS_ERR_FORMAT); assert_null(out); ls_free(blob, blob_len); } static void test_tamper_ciphertext(void **s) { (void)s; tamper_at(LS_HEADER_LEN + 2); } static void test_tamper_tag(void **s)        { (void)s; tamper_at(LS_OVERHEAD + (sizeof PT - 1) - 1); } static void test_tamper_nonce(void **s)      { (void)s; tamper_at(24); } static void test_tamper_salt(void **s)       { (void)s; tamper_at(8); } static void test_tamper_aead_id(void **s)    { (void)s; tamper_at(5); } /* --- nondeterministic output (random nonce/salt) ---", "kind": "function", "line": 106, "name": "test_nondeterministic", "signature": "static void test_nondeterministic(void **s)"}, {"doc": "static void test_nondeterministic(void **s) { (void)s; assert_int_equal(al, bl); assert_memory_not_equal(a, b, al); /* different nonce => different blob ls_free(a, al); ls_free(b, bl); } /* --- passphrase round trip + wrong passphrase ---", "kind": "function", "line": 120, "name": "test_passphrase_roundtrip", "signature": "static void test_passphrase_roundtrip(void **s)"}, {"doc": "assert_memory_equal(out, PT, sizeof PT - 1); assert_null(out2); ls_free(blob, blob_len); } /* --- KDF determinism ---", "kind": "function", "line": 148, "name": "test_derive_key", "signature": "static void test_derive_key(void **s)"}, {"doc": "const char *pass = \"passphrase\"; assert_int_equal(ls_derive_key((const uint8_t *)pass, strlen(pass), salt1, sizeof salt1, k1b), LS_OK); assert_int_equal(ls_derive_key((const uint8_t *)pass, strlen(pass), salt2, sizeof salt2, k2), LS_OK); assert_int_equal(nr_classify_host(\"stats.i2p\"), NR_I2P); assert_int_equal(nr_classify_host(\"a.b.i2p\"), NR_I2P); assert_int_equal(nr_classify_host(\"SITE.I2P\"), NR_I2P); } static void test_classify_host_clearnet(void **state) { (void)state; assert_int_equal(nr_classify_host(\"example.com\"), NR_CLEARNET); assert_int_equal(nr_classify_host(\"news.ycombinator.com\"), NR_CLEARNET); } /* Lookalikes that must NOT be treated as a realm (the suffix is the FINAL label).", "kind": "function", "line": 44, "name": "test_classify_host_lookalikes", "signature": "static void test_classify_host_lookalikes(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 53, "name": "test_classify_host_edges", "signature": "static void test_classify_host_edges(void **state)"}, {"doc": "} static void test_classify_host_edges(void **state) { (void)state; assert_int_equal(nr_classify_host(NULL), NR_CLEARNET); assert_int_equal(nr_classify_host(\"\"), NR_CLEARNET); assert_int_equal(nr_classify_host(\".onion\"), NR_CLEARNET);  /* empty leading label char big[300]; memset(big, 'a', sizeof big); big[sizeof big - 1] = '\\0'; assert_int_equal(nr_classify_host(big), NR_CLEARNET);       /* oversize } /* --- nr_classify_url ---", "kind": "function", "line": 66, "name": "test_classify_url", "signature": "static void test_classify_url(void **state)"}, {"doc": "/* --- nr_classify_url --- static void test_classify_url(void **state) { (void)state; assert_int_equal(nr_classify_url(\"https://abc.onion/path?q=1\"), NR_ONION); assert_int_equal(nr_classify_url(\"http://site.i2p/\"), NR_I2P); assert_int_equal(nr_classify_url(\"https://example.com:443/x\"), NR_CLEARNET); assert_int_equal(nr_classify_url(\"https://host.onion:8080/\"), NR_ONION); assert_int_equal(nr_classify_url(\"garbage-no-host\"), NR_CLEARNET); assert_int_equal(nr_classify_url(NULL), NR_CLEARNET); } /* --- nr_route_for: the routing table ---", "kind": "function", "line": 78, "name": "test_route_onion", "signature": "static void test_route_onion(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 87, "name": "test_route_i2p", "signature": "static void test_route_i2p(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 95, "name": "test_route_clearnet", "signature": "static void test_route_clearnet(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 109, "name": "test_route_null_blocked", "signature": "static void test_route_null_blocked(void **state)"}, {"doc": "assert_int_equal(nr_route_for(\"https://example.com/\", direct), NR_ROUTE_DIRECT); assert_int_equal(nr_route_for(\"https://example.com/\", tor_no_torify), NR_ROUTE_DIRECT); assert_int_equal(nr_route_for(\"https://example.com/\", torify), NR_ROUTE_TOR); /* torify requested but Tor not enabled: clearnet stays direct (torify is moot). assert_int_equal(nr_route_for(\"https://example.com/\", torify_but_no_tor), NR_ROUTE_DIRECT); } static void test_route_null_blocked(void **state) { (void)state; nr_config all_on = { .tor_enabled = 1, .i2p_enabled = 1, .torify_clearnet = 1 }; assert_int_equal(nr_route_for(NULL, all_on), NR_ROUTE_BLOCKED); } /* --- name helpers ---", "kind": "function", "line": 117, "name": "test_realm_allows_http", "signature": "static void test_realm_allows_http(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 125, "name": "test_names", "signature": "static void test_names(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 139, "name": "main", "signature": "int main(void)"}]}, {"id": "tests/test_os_sandbox.c", "kind": "module", "label": "test_os_sandbox.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "632fb8417daffaf2", "symbol_count": 19, "symbols": [{"doc": "#include <stdlib.h> #include <linux/audit.h> #include <sys/mman.h> #include <sys/prctl.h> #include <sys/resource.h> #include <sys/socket.h> #include <sys/stat.h> #include <sys/syscall.h> #include <sys/wait.h> #include <unistd.h> #include \"os_sandbox.h\" /* --- pure policy ---", "kind": "function", "line": 42, "name": "test_policy_allows_safe", "signature": "static void test_policy_allows_safe(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 50, "name": "test_policy_denies_dangerous", "signature": "static void test_policy_denies_dangerous(void **state)"}, {"doc": "io_uring is a seccomp-bypass primitive (its IORING_OP_* operations are dispatched without re-entering the syscall entry point the BPF filters), so none of its three entry syscalls may ever be on the allowlist. Locks the deny-by-default invariant so * nobody can quietly add io_uring \"for async I/O\" (see spec/os_sandbox.md §13).", "kind": "function", "line": 64, "name": "test_policy_denies_io_uring", "signature": "static void test_policy_denies_io_uring(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 70, "name": "test_policy_size", "signature": "static void test_policy_size(void **state)"}, {"doc": "(void)state; assert_false(os_policy_allows(__NR_io_uring_setup)); assert_false(os_policy_allows(__NR_io_uring_enter)); assert_false(os_policy_allows(__NR_io_uring_register)); } static void test_policy_size(void **state) { (void)state; assert_true(os_policy_size() > 0); } /* --- enforcement (fork-based) --- /* KILL: a denied syscall must terminate the child with SIGSYS.", "kind": "function", "line": 79, "name": "test_harden_kills_denied_syscall", "signature": "static void test_harden_kills_denied_syscall(void **state)"}, {"doc": "assert_true(pid >= 0); if (pid == 0) { if (os_harden(OS_VIOLATION_KILL) != OS_OK) _exit(98); (void)syscall(__NR_getpid); /* allowed: must survive (void)syscall(__NR_socket, AF_INET, SOCK_STREAM, 0); /* denied -> SIGSYS _exit(0); /* reached only if the filter failed to block } int st = 0; assert_int_equal(waitpid(pid, &st, 0), pid); assert_true(WIFSIGNALED(st)); assert_int_equal(WTERMSIG(st), SIGSYS); } /* KILL: an allowed syscall must pass and let the child exit cleanly.", "kind": "function", "line": 96, "name": "test_harden_allows_permitted_syscall", "signature": "static void test_harden_allows_permitted_syscall(void **state)"}, {"doc": "pid_t pid = fork(); assert_true(pid >= 0); if (pid == 0) { if (os_harden(OS_VIOLATION_KILL) != OS_OK) _exit(98); long p = syscall(__NR_getpid); _exit(p > 0 ? 42 : 43); } int st = 0; assert_int_equal(waitpid(pid, &st, 0), pid); assert_true(WIFEXITED(st)); assert_int_equal(WEXITSTATUS(st), 42); } /* ERRNO: a denied syscall must return -1/EPERM instead of killing.", "kind": "function", "line": 112, "name": "test_harden_errno_denies_with_eperm", "signature": "static void test_harden_errno_denies_with_eperm(void **state)"}, {"doc": "KILL: io_uring_setup (the mandatory entry point of the whole subsystem -- no ring exists without it) must be killed with SIGSYS. Proves a compromised worker cannot * stand up an io_uring instance to smuggle openat/connect past the BPF filter.", "kind": "function", "line": 130, "name": "test_harden_kills_io_uring_setup", "signature": "static void test_harden_kills_io_uring_setup(void **state)"}, {"doc": "Pure mirror: mmap/mprotect are members of the allowlist but lose the request * when it asks for PROT_EXEC; other syscalls keep their membership decision.", "kind": "function", "line": 151, "name": "test_prot_allowed_wx", "signature": "static void test_prot_allowed_wx(void **state)"}, {"doc": "Pure mirror: mmap/mprotect are members of the allowlist but lose the request * when it asks for PROT_EXEC; other syscalls keep their membership decision. static void test_prot_allowed_wx(void **state) { (void)state; assert_true(os_prot_allowed(__NR_mmap, PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE)); assert_false(os_prot_allowed(__NR_mmap, PROT_READ | PROT_EXEC)); assert_false(os_prot_allowed(__NR_mprotect, PROT_EXEC)); assert_true(os_prot_allowed(__NR_read, 0));             /* non-memory: allowed assert_true(os_prot_allowed(__NR_munmap, 0));           /* memory, not prot-filtered assert_false(os_prot_allowed(__NR_socket, PROT_READ));  /* denied stays denied } /* KILL: a PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE mmap survives but a PROT_EXEC mmap is killed.", "kind": "function", "line": 162, "name": "test_harden_blocks_exec_mmap", "signature": "static void test_harden_blocks_exec_mmap(void **state)"}, {"doc": "long rw = syscall(__NR_mmap, (void *)0, (size_t)4096, PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE, MAP_PRIVATE | MAP_ANONYMOUS, -1, (off_t)0); if (rw == -1) _exit(60); /* a benign RW mapping must still work (void)syscall(__NR_mmap, (void *)0, (size_t)4096, PROT_READ | PROT_EXEC, MAP_PRIVATE | MAP_ANONYMOUS, -1, (off_t)0); _exit(0); /* reached only if the exec mapping was NOT blocked } int st = 0; assert_int_equal(waitpid(pid, &st, 0), pid); assert_true(WIFSIGNALED(st)); assert_int_equal(WTERMSIG(st), SIGSYS); } /* KILL: flipping a writable page to executable via mprotect is killed.", "kind": "function", "line": 182, "name": "test_harden_blocks_exec_mprotect", "signature": "static void test_harden_blocks_exec_mprotect(void **state)"}, {"doc": "Probed BEFORE seccomp so prctl/getrlimit are still reachable: after os_no_dump * the process is undumpable and the core-file limit is zero.", "kind": "function", "line": 214, "name": "test_no_dump_undumpable", "signature": "static void test_no_dump_undumpable(void **state)"}, {"doc": "if (pid == 0) { if (os_no_dump() != OS_OK) _exit(98); if (prctl(PR_GET_DUMPABLE) != 0) _exit(70); struct rlimit rl; if (getrlimit(RLIMIT_CORE, &rl) != 0) _exit(71); _exit(rl.rlim_cur == 0 ? 42 : 72); } int st = 0; assert_int_equal(waitpid(pid, &st, 0), pid); assert_true(WIFEXITED(st)); assert_int_equal(WEXITSTATUS(st), 42); } /* --- Landlock filesystem confinement ---", "kind": "function", "line": 232, "name": "test_landlock_abi_present", "signature": "static void test_landlock_abi_present(void **state)"}, {"doc": "int st = 0; assert_int_equal(waitpid(pid, &st, 0), pid); assert_true(WIFEXITED(st)); assert_int_equal(WEXITSTATUS(st), 42); } /* --- Landlock filesystem confinement --- static void test_landlock_abi_present(void **state) { (void)state; assert_true(os_landlock_abi() > 0); /* this host supports Landlock } /* deny-all: after restricting with no rules, opening any file must fail EACCES.", "kind": "function", "line": 239, "name": "test_landlock_deny_all", "signature": "static void test_landlock_deny_all(void **state)"}, {"doc": "allow-read: a read rule on a temp dir lets reads inside through and blocks * everything else.", "kind": "function", "line": 257, "name": "test_landlock_allow_read", "signature": "static void test_landlock_allow_read(void **state)"}, {"doc": "The pure flag set: the worker requests an isolated network (no remote access), * a user namespace (the unprivileged enabler), and IPC + UTS isolation. static void test_namespace_flags(void **state) { (void)state; int f = os_namespace_flags(); assert_true((f & CLONE_NEWNET) != 0);   /* no network: the worker never fetches assert_true((f & CLONE_NEWUSER) != 0);  /* enables the rest unprivileged assert_true((f & CLONE_NEWIPC) != 0); assert_true((f & CLONE_NEWUTS) != 0); } /* The net-namespace inode of a process. Returns 0 on failure.", "kind": "function", "line": 304, "name": "net_ns_inode", "signature": "static unsigned long net_ns_inode(void)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 313, "name": "suite", "signature": "* suite (it is best-effort defense in depth), and on a host that allows them the\n * isolation mus..."}, {"kind": "function", "line": 334, "name": "main", "signature": "int main(void)"}, {"kind": "macro", "line": 13, "name": "_GNU_SOURCE"}]}, {"id": "tests/test_page_view.c", "kind": "module", "label": "test_page_view.c", "language": "c", "sha256": "671d98caf50d3d7c", "symbol_count": 98, "symbols": [{"doc": "#include <stdint.h> #include <stdlib.h> #include <setjmp.h> #include <string.h> #include <cmocka.h> #include \"box_style.h\" #include \"css.h\" #include \"dom.h\" #include \"flex_layout.h\" #include \"html_parse.h\" #include \"page_view.h\" /* Parse helper: aborts the test on parse failure. Caller frees *doc.", "kind": "function", "line": 27, "name": "parse", "signature": "static hp_document *parse(const char *html)"}, {"doc": "#include \"dom.h\" #include \"flex_layout.h\" #include \"html_parse.h\" #include \"page_view.h\" /* Parse helper: aborts the test on parse failure. Caller frees *doc. static hp_document *parse(const char *html) { hp_document *doc = NULL; assert_int_equal(hp_parse(html, strlen(html), NULL, &doc), HP_OK); assert_non_null(doc); return doc; } /* Finds the i-th run whose collapsed text equals `text`; NULL if none.", "kind": "function", "line": 35, "name": "find_text", "signature": "static const pv_run *find_text(const pv_view *v, const char *text)"}, {"doc": "assert_non_null(doc); return doc; } /* Finds the i-th run whose collapsed text equals `text`; NULL if none. static const pv_run *find_text(const pv_view *v, const char *text) { for (size_t i = 0; i < pv_count(v); ++i) { const pv_run *r = pv_at(v, i); if (r->text != NULL && strcmp(r->text, text) == 0) return r; } return NULL; } /* Finds the first image run whose src equals `src`; NULL if none.", "kind": "function", "line": 44, "name": "find_image", "signature": "static const pv_run *find_image(const pv_view *v, const char *src)"}, {"doc": "} return NULL; } /* Finds the first image run whose src equals `src`; NULL if none. static const pv_run *find_image(const pv_view *v, const char *src) { for (size_t i = 0; i < pv_count(v); ++i) { const pv_run *r = pv_at(v, i); if (r->kind == PV_IMAGE && r->src != NULL && strcmp(r->src, src) == 0) return r; } return NULL; } /* Finds the first video run whose src equals `src`; NULL if none.", "kind": "function", "line": 53, "name": "find_video", "signature": "static const pv_run *find_video(const pv_view *v, const char *src)"}, {"doc": "} return NULL; } /* Finds the first video run whose src equals `src`; NULL if none. static const pv_run *find_video(const pv_view *v, const char *src) { for (size_t i = 0; i < pv_count(v); ++i) { const pv_run *r = pv_at(v, i); if (r->kind == PV_VIDEO && r->src != NULL && strcmp(r->src, src) == 0) return r; } return NULL; } /* --- pure model: pv_new / pv_append / pv_free ---", "kind": "function", "line": 62, "name": "test_new_is_empty", "signature": "static void test_new_is_empty(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 71, "name": "test_append_copies_fields", "signature": "static void test_append_copies_fields(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 98, "name": "test_append_image_copies_fields", "signature": "static void test_append_image_copies_fields(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 114, "name": "test_append_image_null_args", "signature": "static void test_append_image_null_args(void **state)"}, {"doc": "A lone high byte that is invalid UTF-8 is reinterpreted as Windows-1252 (a superset of Latin-1) and re-emitted as UTF-8, recovering accents from a legacy * page rather than dropping them to '?'. 0xE1 -> U+00E1 (a-acute) -> 0xC3 0xA1.", "kind": "function", "line": 130, "name": "test_append_transcodes_latin1", "signature": "static void test_append_transcodes_latin1(void **state)"}, {"doc": "A lone high byte that is invalid UTF-8 is reinterpreted as Windows-1252 (a superset of Latin-1) and re-emitted as UTF-8, recovering accents from a legacy * page rather than dropping them to '?'. 0xE1 -> U+00E1 (a-acute) -> 0xC3 0xA1. static void test_append_transcodes_latin1(void **state) { (void)state; pv_view *v = pv_new(); const char latin1[] = { 'I', 'm', (char)0xE1, 'g', '\\0' }; /* lone 0xE1 = a-acute assert_int_equal(pv_append(v, PV_TEXT, 0, 0, latin1, NULL), PV_OK); assert_string_equal(pv_at(v, 0)->text, \"Im\\xC3\\xA1g\"); /* \"Imág\" pv_free(v); } /* 0xE9 -> U+00E9 (e-acute); a full word of Latin-1 accents round-trips to UTF-8.", "kind": "function", "line": 140, "name": "test_append_transcodes_word", "signature": "static void test_append_transcodes_word(void **state)"}, {"doc": "Windows-1252 0x80-0x9F carry printable glyphs (unlike Latin-1 C1 controls): * 0x93/0x94 are curly double quotes -> U+201C/U+201D (3-byte UTF-8 each).", "kind": "function", "line": 151, "name": "test_append_transcodes_cp1252_quotes", "signature": "static void test_append_transcodes_cp1252_quotes(void **state)"}, {"doc": "Windows-1252 0x80-0x9F carry printable glyphs (unlike Latin-1 C1 controls): * 0x93/0x94 are curly double quotes -> U+201C/U+201D (3-byte UTF-8 each). static void test_append_transcodes_cp1252_quotes(void **state) { (void)state; pv_view *v = pv_new(); const char cp1252[] = { (char)0x93, 'h', 'i', (char)0x94, '\\0' }; assert_int_equal(pv_append(v, PV_TEXT, 0, 0, cp1252, NULL), PV_OK); assert_string_equal(pv_at(v, 0)->text, \"\\xE2\\x80\\x9C\" \"hi\" \"\\xE2\\x80\\x9D\"); pv_free(v); } /* Undefined Windows-1252 positions (0x81 here) have no glyph and still fail to '?'.", "kind": "function", "line": 161, "name": "test_append_undefined_cp1252_is_qmark", "signature": "static void test_append_undefined_cp1252_is_qmark(void **state)"}, {"doc": "pv_free(v); } /* Undefined Windows-1252 positions (0x81 here) have no glyph and still fail to '?'. static void test_append_undefined_cp1252_is_qmark(void **state) { (void)state; pv_view *v = pv_new(); const char bad[] = { 'a', (char)0x81, 'b', '\\0' }; assert_int_equal(pv_append(v, PV_TEXT, 0, 0, bad, NULL), PV_OK); assert_string_equal(pv_at(v, 0)->text, \"a?b\"); pv_free(v); } /* Valid multi-byte UTF-8 input passes through untouched (no double-encoding).", "kind": "function", "line": 171, "name": "test_append_valid_utf8_passthrough", "signature": "static void test_append_valid_utf8_passthrough(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 178, "name": "test_append_null_args", "signature": "static void test_append_null_args(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 186, "name": "test_free_null_and_double", "signature": "static void test_free_null_and_double(void **state)"}, {"doc": "pv_view *v = pv_new(); assert_int_equal(pv_append(NULL, PV_TEXT, 0, 0, \"x\", NULL), PV_ERR_NULL_ARG); assert_int_equal(pv_append(v, PV_TEXT, 0, 0, NULL, NULL), PV_ERR_NULL_ARG); pv_free(v); } static void test_free_null_and_double(void **state) { (void)state; pv_free(NULL); pv_view *v = pv_new(); pv_free(v); } /* --- builder: pv_build ---", "kind": "function", "line": 195, "name": "test_build_null_args", "signature": "static void test_build_null_args(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 204, "name": "test_build_plain_text", "signature": "static void test_build_plain_text(void **state)"}, {"kind": "function", "line": 217, "name": "test_build_heading_level", "signature": "static void test_build_heading_level(void **state)"}, {"doc": "Inline emphasis: <b>/<strong> set bold, <i>/<em> set italic, on the wrapped run * only; surrounding text stays plain. Nested b>i is both.", "kind": "function", "line": 235, "name": "test_build_inline_emphasis", "signature": "static void test_build_inline_emphasis(void **state)"}, {"doc": "Unordered list: each <li>'s first run is prefixed with a bullet marker and * carries list depth 1.", "kind": "function", "line": 276, "name": "test_build_unordered_list", "signature": "static void test_build_unordered_list(void **state)"}, {"doc": "assert_int_equal(pv_build(doc, &v), PV_OK); const pv_run *a = find_text(v, \"\\xE2\\x80\\xA2 apple\"); /* \"* apple\" assert_non_null(a); assert_int_equal(a->indent, 1); const pv_run *p = find_text(v, \"\\xE2\\x80\\xA2 pear\"); assert_non_null(p); assert_int_equal(p->indent, 1); pv_free(v); hp_document_free(doc); } /* Ordered list: markers are 1-based ordinals; nested lists deepen the indent.", "kind": "function", "line": 294, "name": "test_build_ordered_and_nested_list", "signature": "static void test_build_ordered_and_nested_list(void **state)"}, {"doc": "A table becomes a grid: each cell is one collected text run sharing the table's * cont_id, with the column count = widest row. <th> cells are bold.", "kind": "function", "line": 317, "name": "test_build_table_grid", "signature": "static void test_build_table_grid(void **state)"}, {"doc": "Cell inner markup is flattened into the cell's text and not re-emitted as a * separate run; the column count comes from the widest row.", "kind": "function", "line": 351, "name": "test_build_table_flattens_cell", "signature": "static void test_build_table_flattens_cell(void **state)"}, {"doc": "Legacy bgcolor attribute is the background fallback when no CSS background won (like <font color> for the foreground).
